---
title: "SheMed GLP-1 6 Month Clinical Outcome Analysis and Figures"
author: "Jamie L. C. Wong"
format: html
---

################################################################################ 
# 0. Overview & Setup
################################################################################ 

This script present analysis of 6 month clinical outcomes from the SheMed GLP-1 programme.


**Plots & Diagrams**

Reference Outputs for Manuscript:
RTab1. Outlier threshold table
RTab2. Crude paired t-test results for 6 month change in all outcomes
RTab3. Marginal adjusted mean 6 month change in all outcomes
RTab4. Misc estimates for write-up
RTab5. Characeristics Tables for Patients with Missing Data

Main Paper Outputs:
MFig1. CONSORT style diagram
MTab1. Patient characteristics table (by weight)
MFig2. Weight change trajectory and waterfall plots (main)
MFig3. Weight change trajectory plots (subgroup stratified)
MFig4a. HbA1c change figure (diabetic evolution)
MFig4b. Biochemistry lipid change waterfall plots
MFig4c. HbA1c and Lipids Combined
MFig5. Dosing schedule (mounjaro only) with side effect and attrition counts

Supplement Outputs:
STab1. Patient characteristics table (by age)
SFig1. Weight and BMI change (absolute and percentage) by subgroup heatmap
SFig2. Biochemistry and weight change relationship forest plot
SFig3. KM plot for attrition with mutually adjusted cox regression output
SFig4. Side effect counts and severity by subgroups
SFig5. Weight loss at time of discontinuation vs continued patients by subgroups
SFig6. Average dosage trajectories subgroup stratified
SFig7. Sankey plot of HbA1c diabetes evolution

Additional Legacy Outputs (For Reference Only):
LFig1. Dosing schedule stacked bar chart
LFig2. Attrition by side effect count, severity, and care package use
LFig3. Average dosage trajectories subgroup stratified (Mounjaro and Wegovy)
LFig4. Side effect counts in discontinued vs continued patients
LFig5. Discontinuation cox regression by patients with and without side effects, with IMD as exposure


**Object Title Key**
fig = Figure (Main)
sfig = Figure (Supplement)


**Colour Palettes**
Colour palettes have been used from the paletteer package,
these can be browsed on the linked webpage: https://r-graph-gallery.com/color-palette-finder


**Setup**

Load functions, file paths, and packages
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Set cran repo
options(repos = c(
  CRAN      = "https://cloud.r-project.org",
  CRANextra = "https://cloud.r-project.org"
))

# Load packages (update if necessary)
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
#pacman::p_update()
pacman::p_load(
  # For reproducibility
  here,
  # For reading and exporting data 
  readxl, readODS, haven, archive, bit64,
  # For data wrangling & cleaning
  tidyverse, data.table, lubridate, janitor, glue, tictoc, tools, broom,
  # For summarising data
  skimr, table1, htmltools, gt,
  # For plotting
  zoo, ggplot2, ggh4x, patchwork, paletteer, cowplot, scales, ggbreak,
  grid, gridExtra, gtable, stringr, forestploter, tidytext, ggalluvial,
  svglite,
  # For LMM
  lme4, lmerTest, splines, emmeans,
  # For KM plots
  survival, survminer
)

# Set working directory
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here())   # set as default github wd
here()                                    # checks that the wd has been set

# Read in cleaned datasets
data_raw <- fread("data_clean.csv")

# Restrict to first 6 months of follow-up (weeks 0-26)
data <- data_raw[glp1_idx >= 0 & glp1_idx <= 26]

```

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create shared theme for key figures

fig_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)),
    axis.line.x  = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    axis.line.y  = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    plot.margin  = margin(12, 12, 12, 12),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

```



################################################################################ 
# 0.2 Custom helper functions
################################################################################ 

Custom function for p-value extraction
```{r}
# Custom function to format p-value for plot
format_pval <- function(p, digits = 3, prefix = "P") {
  stopifnot(length(p) == 1, is.numeric(p), !is.na(p))
  
  if (p < 10^(-digits)) {
    paste0(prefix, " < ", formatC(10^(-digits), format = "f", digits = digits))
  } else {
    # Determine decimals needed to show meaningful precision
    dec <- max(1, digits - floor(log10(p)) - 1)
    
    paste0(
      prefix, " = ",
      formatC(p, format = "f", digits = dec)
    )
  }
}

```

Custom function for title automation in HbA1c diabetic subgroup box and whisker plots
```{r}
# Custom function to build automated title for boxplots (baseline subgroup)

make_box_title <- function(
  dt,
  subgroup_filter,
  title_prefix = "Diabetic HbA1c at Baseline",
  denom_filter = NULL,
  digits_pct = 1
) {
  # Numerator: unique patients in subgroup
  n_num <- dt[eval(subgroup_filter), uniqueN(patid)]

  # Denominator: default = all unique patients with patid in dt
  # (or pass denom_filter if you want a specific denominator)
  if (is.null(denom_filter)) {
    n_denom <- dt[, uniqueN(patid)]
  } else {
    n_denom <- dt[eval(denom_filter), uniqueN(patid)]
  }

  pct <- if (is.finite(n_denom) && n_denom > 0) 100 * n_num / n_denom else NA_real_

  paste0(
    title_prefix,
    " (N = ", scales::comma(n_num),
    if (is.finite(pct)) paste0("; ", sprintf(paste0("%.", digits_pct, "f"), pct), "%") else "",
    ")"
  )
}
```

Custom function for patient count table plot (for dosing schedule figure)
```{r}
make_count_table <- function(dt, title = NULL, base_size = 11, text_size = 3.0) {
  ggplot(dt, aes(x = month_lab, y = row_lab, label = label)) +
    geom_text(size = text_size) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(dt$row_lab))) +
    theme_void(base_size = base_size) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = seq_along(levels(dt$row_lab)) + 0.5,
      colour = "grey85",
      linewidth = 0.3
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 1),
      plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 6)),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 2, r = 5.5, b = 0, l = 5.5)
    ) +
    { if (!is.null(title)) ggtitle(title) else NULL }
}

```

Custom function for full summary stats (mean sd, median iqr, min max)
```{r}
sum_stats <- function(dt, vars = NULL, digits = 2, na.rm = TRUE) {

  # Convert to data.table if needed
  if (!is.data.table(dt)) dt <- as.data.table(dt)

  # If no vars specified, select numeric vars
  if (is.null(vars)) {
    vars <- names(dt)[sapply(dt, is.numeric)]
  }

  # Internal summary function
  sum_fun <- function(x) {
    x_valid <- if (na.rm) x[!is.na(x)] else x
    n  <- length(x_valid)

    mean_x   <- mean(x_valid, na.rm = na.rm)
    sd_x     <- sd(x_valid, na.rm = na.rm)
    median_x <- median(x_valid, na.rm = na.rm)
    q1       <- quantile(x_valid, 0.25, na.rm = na.rm)
    q3       <- quantile(x_valid, 0.75, na.rm = na.rm)
    min_x    <- min(x_valid, na.rm = na.rm)
    max_x    <- max(x_valid, na.rm = na.rm)

    list(
      N        = n,
      Mean_SD  = sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f (%.", digits, "f)"),
                         round(mean_x, digits), round(sd_x, digits)),
      Median_IQR = sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f (%.", digits, "f–%.", digits, "f)"),
                           round(median_x, digits),
                           round(q1, digits),
                           round(q3, digits)),
      Min_Max  = sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f–%.", digits, "f"),
                         round(min_x, digits),
                         round(max_x, digits))
    )
  }

  # Apply across variables
  result <- rbindlist(lapply(vars, function(v) {
    out <- sum_fun(dt[[v]])
    data.table(
      Variable = v,
      N        = out$N,
      Mean_SD  = out$Mean_SD,
      Median_IQR = out$Median_IQR,
      Min_Max  = out$Min_Max
    )
  }))

  return(result[])
}

```



################################################################################ 
# 0.3 Restrict sample to individuals with baseline blood tests completed
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# 0 month blood test performed

## Consider performed if blood test date present
## As some specific measures have been removed as outliers

# Count patients with blood tests performed
vars_0m_date <- c(
  "blood_alt_ul_0m_date",
  "blood_ast_ul_0m_date",
  "blood_chol_mmoll_0m_date",
  "blood_hdl_mmoll_0m_date",
  "blood_ldl_mmoll_0m_date",
  "blood_chol_hdl_ratio_0m_date",
  "blood_ggt_ul_0m_date",
  "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m_date",
  "blood_tsh_miul_0m_date",
  "blood_tg_mmoll_0m_date"
)
data[, blood_test_any_0m := as.integer(rowSums(!is.na(.SD)) > 0), .SDcols = vars_0m_date]

# Convert to wide format and check counts
data_blood0m_chk <- data[, .SD[1], by = patid]
table(data_blood0m_chk$blood_test_any_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_blood0m_chk$blood_test_any_0m, useNA = "always"))

# Remove patients without baseline blood test (only keep pat with tests)
## 373 patients removed
data <- data[blood_test_any_0m == 1]

# Recheck counts
table(data$blood_test_any_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data$blood_test_any_0m, useNA = "always"))

#-----------------------------#
# 6 month blood test performed

## Consider performed if blood test date present
## As some specific measures have been removed as outliers

vars_6m_date <- c(
  "blood_alt_ul_6m_date",
  "blood_ast_ul_6m_date",
  "blood_chol_mmoll_6m_date",
  "blood_hdl_mmoll_6m_date",
  "blood_ldl_mmoll_6m_date",
  "blood_chol_hdl_ratio_6m_date",
  "blood_ggt_ul_6m_date",
  "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m_date",
  "blood_tsh_miul_6m_date",
  "blood_tg_mmoll_6m_date"
)
data[, blood_test_any_6m := as.integer(rowSums(!is.na(.SD)) > 0), .SDcols = vars_6m_date]

# Convert to wide format and check counts
data_blood6m_chk <- data[, .SD[1], by = patid]
table(data_blood6m_chk$blood_test_any_6m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_blood6m_chk$blood_test_any_6m, useNA = "always"))

#-----------------------------#
# Check completeness of baseline linearly interpolated weight post restriction
data[glp1_idx == 0L & !is.na(wt_kg_lin), uniqueN(patid)]

```



################################################################################ 
# 0.4 Recategorise and create relevant vars for subsequent analyses
################################################################################ 

Create attrition variable
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Data prep - define main study attrition vars

#-------------------------------#
# Create filtering vars

# Define attrition as not having a prescription in 9 weeks - Rachael

# Check final data collection date
summary(data$wt_date)
## The final data collection date is 2025-08-10 (final wt_date)
## The latest initial glp-1 dose was prescribed on 2025-02-06, allowing sufficient 6m fu


# Define follow-up end date, glp-1 prescription window, and gap weeks
fu_end <- as.Date("2025-08-10")   # final data collection date
glp1_gap_weeks <- 9               # week count to consider someone as dropped out

# Order dataset
setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# Define valid follow-up and a prescription event
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    fu_valid = wk_st_date <= fu_end,
    rx_event = (!is.na(mounjaro_mg) | !is.na(wegovy_mg))
  )
]

# Note last prescription week (carry forward the latest rx_event week)
data[
  ,
  rx_last_wk := fifelse(rx_event, glp1_idx, NA_integer_),
  by = patid
][
  ,
  rx_last_wk := nafill(rx_last_wk, type = "locf"),
  by = patid
]

#-------------------------------#
# Identify attrition

# Identify attrition: declare attrition once someone has gone 9 weeks with NO prescription
## Only assess the uncovered gap that occurs after the FINAL prescription event
## (i.e. ignore any earlier gaps if the patient later restarted treatment)

# Patient-level final prescription week (within valid follow-up)
data_last_rx <- data[
  fu_valid == TRUE & rx_event == TRUE,
  .(rx_final_wk = max(glp1_idx)),
  by = patid
]

# Patient-level attrition week/date:
# - If a patient has a final rx week, attrition is declared at the START of week (rx_final_wk + 9),
#   provided that week exists within valid follow-up.
# - This matches: "once someone has gone 9 weeks with no prescription" (counting from the last rx week).
data_attr <- data[
  fu_valid == TRUE
][
  data_last_rx, on = "patid"
][
  ,
  .(
    rx_final_wk = rx_final_wk[1],

    # attrition trigger week index (the week at which we declare attrition)
    attr_wk = if (is.na(rx_final_wk[1])) NA_integer_ else rx_final_wk[1] + glp1_gap_weeks,

    # attrition date = start date of the attrition trigger week (if present)
    attr_date = {
      if (is.na(rx_final_wk[1])) {
        as.Date(NA)
      } else {
        target_wk <- rx_final_wk[1] + glp1_gap_weeks
        wk_st_date[glp1_idx == target_wk][1]
      }
    }
  ),
  by = patid
][
  # keep only patients where the attrition trigger week exists within valid follow-up rows
  !is.na(attr_wk) & !is.na(attr_date),
  .(patid, rx_final_wk, attr_wk, attr_date)
]

# Merge back and create patient-level attrition ever flag
data <- merge(
  data,
  data_attr[, .(patid, attr_date, attr_wk)],
  by = "patid",
  all.x = TRUE
)
data[, attr_ever_yn := !is.na(attr_date)]

# Flag the single row corresponding to the attrition week (one-week marker)
data[, attr_wk_flag := FALSE]
data[!is.na(attr_wk) & glp1_idx == attr_wk, attr_wk_flag := TRUE]

# Create variable showing patient-level dropout status over time
# 0 before attrition week, 1 at and after attrition week
data[, attr_status := 0L]
data[!is.na(attr_wk) & glp1_idx >= attr_wk, attr_status := 1L]

```

Create attrition weight variables (from linearly interpolated weight)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assign last recorded weight and glp1_idx of this measure for attrited patients

# Set order
setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# For patients who ever attrition, select the last non-NA wt_kg_lin value and glp1_idx
attr_last_wt <- data[
  attr_ever_yn == 1 & !is.na(wt_kg_lin),
  .SD[.N],                      # last non-NA weight row within patid
  by = patid,
  .SDcols = c("wt_kg_lin", "glp1_idx")
][
  ,
  .(
    patid,
    attr_wt    = wt_kg_lin,
    attr_wt_wk = glp1_idx
  )
]

# Merge back (attr_wt / attr_wt_wk will be NA for non-attriters and for attriters with no non-NA weights)
data <- merge(data, attr_last_wt, by = "patid", all.x = TRUE)

```

Create LOCF GLP-1 dosage vars (up to attrition) for monthly analysis
(i.e. we can extract mean dose at each month)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# LOCF GLP-1 dosage vars (only before attrition)

# Collapse mounjaro and wegovy before LOCF (or else it carries forward both)
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    glp1_drug = fifelse(!is.na(mounjaro_mg), "Mounjaro",
                            fifelse(!is.na(wegovy_mg), "Wegovy", NA_character_)),
    glp1_dose = fifelse(!is.na(mounjaro_mg), mounjaro_mg,
                            fifelse(!is.na(wegovy_mg),  wegovy_mg,  NA_character_))
  )
]

# Ensure ordering
setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# LOCF for all drugs
## Drug name
data[attr_status == 0L, 
     glp1_drug_locf := zoo::na.locf(glp1_drug, na.rm = FALSE),
     by = patid
]
## Dosage
data[attr_status == 0L, 
     glp1_dose_locf := zoo::na.locf(glp1_dose, na.rm = FALSE),
     by = patid
]

# Split back into two columns
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    mounjaro_mg_locf = fifelse(glp1_drug_locf == "Mounjaro", glp1_dose_locf, NA_character_),
    wegovy_mg_locf   = fifelse(glp1_drug_locf == "Wegovy",   glp1_dose_locf, NA_character_)
  )
]

```

Extract values only vars (no denominator) for GLP-1 dosage (LOCF)
```{r}
# Check GLP-1 dosage categories
table(data$mounjaro_mg_locf, useNA = "always")
table(data$wegovy_mg_locf, useNA = "always")

# Extract mg count (note that we are using LOCF vars)
data[, mounjaro_mg_locf_num := as.numeric(sub("^([0-9.]+)mg.*$", "\\1", mounjaro_mg_locf))]
data[, wegovy_mg_locf_num := as.numeric(sub("^([0-9.]+)mg.*$", "\\1", wegovy_mg_locf))]

summary(data$mounjaro_mg_locf_num)
summary(data$wegovy_mg_locf_num)

```

Create baseline and 6 month GLP-1 type var
```{r}
# Create baseline and 6m GLP-1 type var
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    glp1_drug_locf_0m = glp1_drug_locf[glp1_idx == 0][1],
    glp1_drug_locf_6m = glp1_drug_locf[glp1_idx == 26][1]
  ),
  by = patid
]

```

Create treatment switching flag var + ever on mounjaro vs wegovy var
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Treatment Switching

# Flag any glp1 switching
data[
  ,
  glp1_ever_switch := as.integer(
    any(glp1_drug == "Mounjaro", na.rm = TRUE) &
    any(glp1_drug == "Wegovy",  na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = patid
]

# Flag any mj to wg switch and vice versa
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    glp1_mj_to_wg = as.integer(
      any(glp1_drug == "Mounjaro", na.rm = TRUE) &
      any(glp1_drug == "Wegovy",  na.rm = TRUE) &
      match("Mounjaro", glp1_drug) < match("Wegovy", glp1_drug)
    ),

    glp1_wg_to_mj = as.integer(
      any(glp1_drug == "Wegovy",  na.rm = TRUE) &
      any(glp1_drug == "Mounjaro", na.rm = TRUE) &
      match("Wegovy", glp1_drug) < match("Mounjaro", glp1_drug)
    )
  ),
  by = patid
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Ever Mounjaro / Wegovy

# Mounjaro
data[, glp1_mounjaro_ever := as.integer(any(glp1_drug == "Mounjaro", na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = patid]

# Wegovy
data[, glp1_wegovy_ever := as.integer(any(glp1_drug == "Wegovy", na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = patid]

```

Recategorise sociodemgraphic vars to categorical vars
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Recategorise sociodemographic vars into categorical vars

#-----------------------------#
# Age (5 Level)

hist(data$age)
summary(data$age)

data[, age_cat5 := fcase(
  age >= 18 & age <= 29, "18–29",
  age >= 30 & age <= 39, "30–39",
  age >= 40 & age <= 49, "40–49",
  age >= 50 & age <= 59, "50–59",
  age >= 60 & age <= 79, "60–79",
  default = NA_character_
)]
data[, age_cat5 := factor(age_cat5, levels = c("18–29","30–39","40–49","50–59","60–79"))]

#-----------------------------#
# Ethnicity (Binary | 1 = White, 0 = Non-White)

table(data$ethnicity, useNA = "always")

data[ethnicity == "Prefer not to say", ethnicity := NA]
data[, eth_bin := fifelse(ethnicity == "White", "White", "Non-White")]

#-----------------------------#
# IMD (Named 5 level categorical)

table(data$area_imd_qt, useNA = "always")

data$area_imd_qt <- 
  factor(data$area_imd_qt, levels=c(1,2,3,4,5),
         labels=c("1 (Most Deprived)", 
                  "2", "3", "4",
                  "5 (Least Deprived)"))

#-----------------------------#
# Baseline weight (Named 5 level categorical)

# Cut with left closed, right open categories
## <90 kg     = −∞ to <90
## 90–99 kg   = ≥90 to <100
## 100–109 kg = ≥100 to <110
## 110–119 kg = ≥110 to <120
## ≥120 kg    = ≥120 to ∞

data$wt_kg_0m_cat <- cut(
  data$wt_kg_0m,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 90, 100, 110, 120, Inf), right = FALSE,
  labels = c(
    "<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg")
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create additional variables

#-----------------------------#
# BMI (using linearly interpolated weights)

# Time varying
data[, bmi := wt_kg_lin / ((ht_cm/100)^2)]

# 0 month baseline
data[, bmi_0m := wt_kg_0m / ((ht_cm/100)^2)]

# 6 month
data[, bmi_6m := wt_kg_6m / ((ht_cm/100)^2)]

#-----------------------------#
# Weight change

# Absolute change
data[, wt_change_kg_06m := wt_kg_6m - wt_kg_0m]

# Percentage change
data[, wt_change_pct_06m := 100 * (wt_kg_6m - wt_kg_0m) / wt_kg_0m]

```

HbA1c Diabetic Categories
(Use standard clinical cutoffs: https://themapleshealthcentre.co.uk/pre-diabetes-non-diabetic-hyperglycaemia/)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assign categorical values to different HbA1c levels at baseline (0 months)

# Normal: < 42, Pre-Diabetic: 42-47 (42-47.99), Diabetic: >=48
data[, hba1c_cat_0m := fifelse(
  blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m < 42, "Normal",
  fifelse(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m < 48, "Pre-Diabetic", "Diabetic")
)]

# Set order for baseline characteristics table
data[, hba1c_cat_0m := factor(
  hba1c_cat_0m,
  levels = c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic", "Normal"),
  ordered = TRUE
)]

# Tabulate
table(data$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always"))


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Check if all values were correctly assigned (0 months)

# Values that failed to classify despite being present and finite (should be empty)
data[
  is.finite(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) &
  !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) &
  is.na(hba1c_cat_0m),
  .(patid, blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m)
][order(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m)]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assign categorical values to different HbA1c levels at baseline (6 months)

# Normal: < 42, Pre-Diabetic: 42-47 (42-47.99), Diabetic: >=48
data[, hba1c_cat_6m := fifelse(
  blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m < 42, "Normal",
  fifelse(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m < 48, "Pre-Diabetic", "Diabetic")
)]

# Tabulate
table(data$hba1c_cat_6m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data$hba1c_cat_6m, useNA = "always"))


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Check if all values were correctly assigned (6 months)

# Values that failed to classify despite being present and finite (should be empty)
data[
  is.finite(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m) &
  !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m) &
  is.na(hba1c_cat_6m),
  .(patid, blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)
][order(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)]

```

Side effect severity, counts, and care package use
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create side effect severity vars (any event vars)

# Collapse severity levels
data[
  , sdeff_severity_cat3 := fifelse(
    sdeff_severity %in% c(
      "Mild side effects (easily manageable)"
      ), 
      "Mild",
    fifelse(
      sdeff_severity %in% c(
        "Moderate Side effects (somewhat manageable)"
      ),
      "Moderate",
      fifelse(
        sdeff_severity %in% c(
          "Severe side effects (challenging to manage)",
          "Severe side effects (challenging to manage, significantly interfere with daily life)",
          "Extremely severe side effects (unmanageable)",
          "Extremely severe side effects (unmanageable, impact daily life, require emergency clinical intervention)"
        ),
        "Severe",
        NA_character_
      )
    )
  )
]

# Create separate ever vars for each severity level
data_ever <- data[
  !is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3),
  .(
    sdeff_mild_ever     = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Mild")),
    sdeff_moderate_ever = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Moderate")),
    sdeff_severe_ever   = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Severe"))
  ),
  by = patid
]

# Create most severe side effect per patient using fifelse
data_mostsev <- data[
  ,
  .(
    most_severe_sdeff = fifelse(
      any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Severe", na.rm = TRUE),
      "Severe",
      fifelse(
        any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Moderate", na.rm = TRUE),
        "Moderate",
        fifelse(
          any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Mild", na.rm = TRUE),
          "Mild",
          "None"
        )
      )
    )
  ),
  by = patid
]

# Create ordered factor
data_mostsev[
  ,
  most_severe_sdeff := factor(
    most_severe_sdeff,
    levels = c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"),
    ordered = TRUE
  )
]

# Merge most severe side effect back to 6m dataset
data[
  data_mostsev, most_severe_sdeff := i.most_severe_sdeff,
  on = "patid"
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create total side-effect counts per patient (overall + severity-specific)

# Count total number of reported side-effect entries per patient (non-missing severity)
sdeff_counts <- data[
  ,
  .(
    sdeff_total_n    = sum(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3)),
    sdeff_mild_n     = sum(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Mild",     na.rm = TRUE),
    sdeff_moderate_n = sum(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Moderate", na.rm = TRUE),
    sdeff_severe_n   = sum(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Severe",   na.rm = TRUE),
    sdeff_any_ever      = as.integer(any(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3))),
    sdeff_mild_ever     = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Mild",     na.rm = TRUE)),
    sdeff_moderate_ever = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Moderate", na.rm = TRUE)),
    sdeff_severe_ever   = as.integer(any(sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Severe",   na.rm = TRUE))
  ),
  by = patid
]

# Merge back
data[sdeff_counts, `:=`(
  sdeff_total_n       = i.sdeff_total_n,
  sdeff_mild_n        = i.sdeff_mild_n,
  sdeff_moderate_n    = i.sdeff_moderate_n,
  sdeff_severe_n      = i.sdeff_severe_n,
  sdeff_any_ever      = i.sdeff_any_ever,
  sdeff_mild_ever     = i.sdeff_mild_ever,
  sdeff_moderate_ever = i.sdeff_moderate_ever,
  sdeff_severe_ever   = i.sdeff_severe_ever
), on = "patid"]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create cumulative side effect counts per patient (time-varying by glp1_idx)

# Ensure deterministic ordering within person over time
data.table::setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# Cumulative sums of side effects within person (LOCF automatically)
data[
  ,
  `:=`(
    # Sum of any severity of side effects report up to this timepoint
    sdeff_total_cum_n = cumsum(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3)),

    # Severity-specific cumulative counts
    sdeff_mild_cum_n = cumsum(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3) & sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Mild"),
    sdeff_moderate_cum_n = cumsum(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3) & sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Moderate"),
    sdeff_severe_cum_n = cumsum(!is.na(sdeff_severity_cat3) & sdeff_severity_cat3 == "Severe")
  ),
  by = patid
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create care package use vars

# Create ever var for care package use
data[
  , sdeff_carepack_ever := as.integer(any(sdeff_carepack_yn == 1, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = patid
]

```

Create wide dataset, and assign vars to factor where needed
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Only keep 1st row for wide dataset (for baseline characteristics)

data_wide <- data[, .SD[1], by = patid]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assign vars to factor where needed (for patient characteristics tables)

# Sex
data_wide$sex <- 
  factor(data_wide$sex, levels=c("F"),
         labels=c("Female"))

# Weight (baseline)
summary(data_wide$wt_kg_0m)
data_wide$wt_kg_0m_cat <- cut(
  data_wide$wt_kg_0m,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 90, 100, 110, 120, Inf), right = FALSE,
  labels = c(
    "<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg")
)

# BMI (baseline)
summary(data_wide$bmi_0m)
data_wide$bmi_0m_cat <- cut(
  data_wide$bmi_0m,
  breaks = c(
    #18.5, 
    25, 30, 35, 40, Inf),
  right  = FALSE,
  labels = c(
    #"Healthy (18.5–24.9)", *None present*
    "Overweight (25.0–29.9)",
    "Obesity I (30.0–34.9)",
    "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
    "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
  )
)

# BMI (6 month follow-up) - for use in other figures
summary(data_wide$bmi_6m)
data_wide$bmi_6m_cat <- cut(
  data_wide$bmi_6m,
  breaks = c(
    18.5, 
    25, 30, 35, 40, Inf),
  right  = FALSE,
  labels = c(
    "Healthy (18.5–24.9)",
    "Overweight (25.0–29.9)",
    "Obesity I (30.0–34.9)",
    "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
    "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
  )
)

# HbA1c categories
data_wide[, hba1c_cat_0m_lab :=
  factor(
    fcase(
      hba1c_cat_0m == "Diabetic",
      "Diabetes (≥48 mmol/mol)",
      default = NA_character_,
      hba1c_cat_0m == "Pre-Diabetic",
      "Pre-Diabetes (42–47 mmol/mol)",
      hba1c_cat_0m == "Normal",
      "Normal (<42 mmol/mol)"
    ),
    levels = c(
      "Diabetes (≥48 mmol/mol)",
      "Pre-Diabetes (42–47 mmol/mol)",
      "Normal (<42 mmol/mol)"
    )
  )
]
```

```{r}
table(data_wide$age_cat5, useNA = "always")

prop.table(table(data_wide$eth_bin, useNA = "always"))
```

################################################################################ 
# RTab1. Outlier threshold table
################################################################################ 

**Completed in a separate word document**



################################################################################ 
# RTab2. Crude paired t-test results for 6 month change in all outcomes
################################################################################ 

The following t-tests have been performed:
1. Weight change 
   - Absolute Change: wt_kg_6m - wt_kg_0m
   - Percentage Change: (wt_kg_6m - wt_kg_0m) / wt_kg_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
2. HbA1c change
   - Absolute Change: blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m - blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m - blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) / blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample, Diabetic Categories (hba1c_cat_0m)
3. Total Cholesterol change 
   - Absolute Change: blood_chol_mmoll_6m - blood_chol_mmoll_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_chol_mmoll_6m - blood_chol_mmoll_0m) / blood_chol_mmoll_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
4. HDL change 
   - Absolute Change: blood_hdl_mmoll_6m - blood_hdl_mmoll_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_hdl_mmoll_6m - blood_hdl_mmoll_0m) / blood_hdl_mmoll_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
5. LDL change 
   - Absolute Change: blood_ldl_mmoll_6m - blood_ldl_mmoll_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_ldl_mmoll_6m - blood_ldl_mmoll_0m) / blood_ldl_mmoll_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
6. Triglyceride change 
   - Absolute Change: blood_tg_mmoll_6m - blood_tg_mmoll_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_tg_mmoll_6m - blood_tg_mmoll_0m) / blood_tg_mmoll_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
7. ALT change
   - Absolute Change: blood_alt_ul_6m - blood_alt_ul_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_alt_ul_6m - blood_alt_ul_0m) / blood_alt_ul_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample
7. AST change
   - Absolute Change: blood_ast_ul_6m - blood_ast_ul_0m
   - Percentage Change: (blood_ast_ul_6m - blood_ast_ul_0m) / blood_ast_ul_0m * 100
   - By Subgroups: Full Sample

*Note that complete case analyses has been done for each separately

Create custom function for table creation
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Custom function: paired change table (mean change + 95% CI + p-value)

paired_change_table <- function(dt,
                                base_var,
                                fu_var,
                                subgroup_var = NULL,         # NULL or character vector
                                measure_label = NULL,
                                include_full_sample = TRUE,  # <-- ADD THIS
                                pct_denominator = c("baseline", "followup"),
                                digits_change = 2,
                                digits_p = 3) {

  stopifnot(data.table::is.data.table(dt))
  pct_denominator <- match.arg(pct_denominator)

  `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b

  # Keep complete pairs only
  d <- dt[
    !is.na(get(base_var)) & !is.na(get(fu_var)) &
      is.finite(get(base_var)) & is.finite(get(fu_var))
  ]
  if (nrow(d) == 0L) return(data.table::data.table())

  # Denominator for pct_total is always the complete-case N for THIS measure
  N_total <- nrow(d)

  # Helper to format p-values nicely
  fmt_p <- function(p) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(p), NA_character_,
      ifelse(
        p < 10^(-digits_p),
        paste0("<", formatC(10^(-digits_p), format = "f", digits = digits_p)),
        formatC(p, format = "f", digits = digits_p)
      )
    )
  }

  # Compute rows for one group
  compute_rows <- function(x, subgroup_level = "Full Sample") {
    base <- x[[base_var]]
    fu   <- x[[fu_var]]

    # Absolute change
    ch_abs <- fu - base
    test_abs <- stats::t.test(fu, base, paired = TRUE)

    # Percent change
    den <- if (pct_denominator == "baseline") base else fu
    ch_pct <- ifelse(is.finite(den) & den != 0, (fu - base) / den * 100, NA_real_)
    test_pct <- stats::t.test(ch_pct, mu = 0)

    out_abs <- data.table::data.table(
      measure = measure_label %||% base_var,
      subgroup = subgroup_level,
      scale = "Absolute Change",
      n = length(ch_abs),
      pct_total = 100 * length(ch_abs) / N_total,
      mean_change = mean(ch_abs, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_low  = unname(test_abs$conf.int[1]),
      ci_high = unname(test_abs$conf.int[2]),
      p_value = unname(test_abs$p.value)
    )

    out_pct <- data.table::data.table(
      measure = measure_label %||% base_var,
      subgroup = subgroup_level,
      scale = "Percent Change",
      n = sum(!is.na(ch_pct)),
      pct_total = 100 * sum(!is.na(ch_pct)) / N_total,
      mean_change = mean(ch_pct, na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_low  = unname(test_pct$conf.int[1]),
      ci_high = unname(test_pct$conf.int[2]),
      p_value = unname(test_pct$p.value)
    )

    data.table::rbindlist(list(out_abs, out_pct), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
  }

  # Always compute full sample once
  full_res <- compute_rows(d, subgroup_level = "Full Sample")
  full_res[, subgroup_type := "Overall"]

  # If no subgrouping requested
  if (is.null(subgroup_var)) {
    res <- full_res

  } else {
    subgroup_var <- as.character(subgroup_var)

    res_list <- lapply(subgroup_var, function(g) {

      dd <- data.table::copy(d)

      dd[, (g) := as.character(get(g))]
      dd[is.na(get(g)), (g) := "Missing"]

      tmp <- dd[, compute_rows(.SD, subgroup_level = get(g)[1L]), by = g]

      data.table::setnames(tmp, old = g, new = "subgroup", skip_absent = TRUE)
      tmp[, subgroup_type := g]
      tmp[]
    })

    res <- data.table::rbindlist(res_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

    if (isTRUE(include_full_sample)) {
      res <- data.table::rbindlist(list(full_res, res), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
    }
  }

  # Formatting / rounding
  res[, `:=`(
    mean_change = round(mean_change, digits_change),
    ci_low      = round(ci_low, digits_change),
    ci_high     = round(ci_high, digits_change),
    pct_total   = round(pct_total, 1),
    p_value_fmt = fmt_p(p_value)
  )]

  # Output columns
  res[, .(measure, subgroup_type, scale, subgroup, n, pct_total,
          mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value, p_value_fmt)]
}

```

Create table for all measures
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create function to build tables for all outcomes of interest (by subgroups)

build_all_change_tables <- function(data_wide) {

  out <- list(
    # 1) Weight
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "wt_kg_0m",
      fu_var   = "wt_kg_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "Weight",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 2) HbA1c (additionally by diabetic categories)
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("hba1c_cat_0m", "wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "HbA1c",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 3) Total Cholesterol
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_chol_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_chol_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "Total Cholesterol",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 4) HDL
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_hdl_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_hdl_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "HDL",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 5) LDL
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_ldl_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_ldl_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "LDL",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 6) Triglycerides
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_tg_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_tg_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "Triglycerides",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 7) ALT
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_alt_ul_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_alt_ul_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "ALT",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    ),

    # 8) AST
    paired_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_ast_ul_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_ast_ul_6m",
      subgroup_var = c("wt_kg_0m_cat", "age_cat5", "area_imd_qt", "eth_bin"),
      measure_label = "AST",
      include_full_sample = TRUE
    )
  )

  res <- data.table::rbindlist(out, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

  # Make output ordering nice
  measure_order <- c(
    "Weight", "HbA1c", "Total Cholesterol", "HDL", "LDL", "Triglycerides", "ALT", "AST"
  )
  res[, measure := factor(measure, levels = measure_order)]
  res[, scale   := factor(scale, levels = c("Absolute Change", "Percent Change"))]

  res[order(measure, subgroup, scale)]
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create and reformat final table

# Create table
rfig2_ttest_results <- build_all_change_tables(data_wide)

# Standardise subgroup label for display
rfig2_ttest_results[subgroup == "Full Sample", subgroup := "Full Sample"]

# Recode HbA1c subgroup labels with numeric cut-offs
rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("^Diab", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Basline Diabetic (≥48 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("pre", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Baseline Pre-diabetic (42–47 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("^Norm", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Baseline Normal (<42 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("^Full", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Full Sample"
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  is.na(subgroup),
  subgroup := "Baseline Missing"
]

#-----------------------------#
# Subgroup ordering (measure specific)

# HbA1c subgroup order (ONLY for HbA1c rows)
hba1c_levels <- c(
  "Full Sample",
  "Basline Diabetic (≥48 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Pre-diabetic (42–47 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Normal (<42 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Missing"
)

# Baseline weight subgroup order (ONLY for Weight rows)
wt_levels <- c(
  "Full Sample",
  "<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg",
  "Baseline Missing"
)

# Create an ordering key that depends on measure
rfig2_ttest_results[, subgroup_order := NA_integer_]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("^HbA1c", as.character(measure)),
  subgroup_order := match(as.character(subgroup), hba1c_levels)
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  grepl("^Weight", as.character(measure)),
  subgroup_order := match(as.character(subgroup), wt_levels)
]

# Put non-matching labels after, but keep stable ordering
rfig2_ttest_results[is.na(subgroup_order), subgroup_order := 999L]

# Order table using this key
data.table::setorder(rfig2_ttest_results, measure, subgroup_order, subgroup, scale)

# Drop helper col later before export
rfig2_ttest_results[, subgroup_order := NULL]

#-----------------------------#
# Relabelling

# Add units to outcome labels
rfig2_ttest_results[, measure := as.character(measure)]
rfig2_ttest_results[
  ,
  measure := fifelse(measure == "Weight",            "Weight (kg)",
             fifelse(measure == "HbA1c",             "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
             fifelse(measure == "Total Cholesterol", "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "HDL",               "HDL (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "LDL",               "LDL (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "Triglycerides",     "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "ALT",               "ALT (U/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "AST",               "AST (U/L)",
                   measure))))))))
]

# Force measure order
rfig2_ttest_results[
  ,
  measure := factor(
    measure,
    levels = c(
      "Weight (kg)",
      "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
      "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
      "HDL (mmol/L)",
      "LDL (mmol/L)",
      "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",
      "ALT (U/L)",
      "AST (U/L)"
    )
  )
]

# Force scale order
rfig2_ttest_results[
  ,
  scale := factor(
    scale,
    levels = c("Absolute Change", "Percent Change")
  )
]

# Create a display column: "Mean Change (95% CI)"
rfig2_ttest_results[
  ,
  mean_ci := sprintf("%.*f (%.*f, %.*f)",
                     2, mean_change, 2, ci_low, 2, ci_high)
]

#-----------------------------#
# Fill in subgroup_type for Full Sample rows (and any other NA subgroup_type)

# Label NAs (i.e. no subgroup) "Overall"
rfig2_ttest_results[is.na(subgroup_type), subgroup_type := "Overall"]

#-----------------------------#
# Order by subgroup_typ,  then subgroup / scale

# Choose the order you want subgroup blocks to appear in the final table
subgroup_type_levels <- c(
  "Overall",
  "wt_kg_0m_cat",
  "age_cat5",
  "area_imd_qt",
  "eth_bin",
  "hba1c_cat_0m"
)

# Assign labels to categories
subgroup_type_labels <- c(
  Overall        = "Full Sample",
  wt_kg_0m_cat   = "Baseline Weight",
  age_cat5       = "Age Group",
  area_imd_qt    = "IMD Quintile",
  eth_bin        = "Ethnicity",
  hba1c_cat_0m   = "Baseline HbA1c Category"
)

rfig2_ttest_results[
  , subgroup_type_label := subgroup_type_labels[as.character(subgroup_type)]
]

# Fallback for unexpected subgroup_type
rfig2_ttest_results[
  is.na(subgroup_type_label),
  subgroup_type_label := as.character(subgroup_type)
]

# Keep any unexpected subgroup_type values after the known ones
rfig2_ttest_results[, subgroup_type := factor(
  subgroup_type,
  levels = subgroup_type_levels
)]

#-----------------------------#
# Final ordering within subgroup blocks
# - Only hba1c_cat_0m and wt_kg_0m_cat get bespoke within-block ordering
# - Other subgroup blocks keep their existing row order

# Helper: bespoke within-block order (only filled for selected subgroup_type)
rfig2_ttest_results[, subgroup_order2 := 0L]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  subgroup_type == "hba1c_cat_0m",
  subgroup_order2 := match(as.character(subgroup), hba1c_levels)
]

rfig2_ttest_results[
  subgroup_type == "wt_kg_0m_cat",
  subgroup_order2 := match(as.character(subgroup), wt_levels)
]

# Push any unmatched/NA matches to the end within those bespoke blocks
rfig2_ttest_results[
  subgroup_type %chin% c("hba1c_cat_0m", "wt_kg_0m_cat") & is.na(subgroup_order2),
  subgroup_order2 := 999L
]

# Final row order (don’t drop subgroup_order2 until after this)
data.table::setorder(
  rfig2_ttest_results,
  measure,
  subgroup_type,
  subgroup_order2,
  scale
)

#-----------------------------#
# Set final columns (and then drop helper)

rfig2_ttest_results <- rfig2_ttest_results[, .(
  measure,                # Outcome
  subgroup_type_label,    # Display group name
  subgroup_type,          # Internal group var
  subgroup,               # Subgroup level
  scale,                  # Absolute / Percent
  n,                      # Sample size
  pct_total,              # % of complete-case N
  mean_change,            # Numeric mean
  ci_low,                 # CI lower
  ci_high,                # CI upper
  p_value,                # Numeric p
  mean_ci,                # Formatted string
  p_value_fmt             # Formatted p
)]

#--------------------------------------------------------------#
# Split table into full, absolute change, % change tables

# Split tables into full, absolute and % only tabs
rfig2_ttest_results_full <- rfig2_ttest_results
rfig2_ttest_results_abs <- rfig2_ttest_results[scale == "Absolute Change"]
rfig2_ttest_results_pct <- rfig2_ttest_results[scale == "Percent Change"]

# Remove scale for subsetted tables
rfig2_ttest_results_abs[, scale := NULL]
rfig2_ttest_results_pct[, scale := NULL]

# Rename 

rfig2_ttest_results_full
rfig2_ttest_results_abs
rfig2_ttest_results_pct

```

Export table (csv and gt html)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as csv

fwrite(
  rfig2_ttest_results,
  file = "Final Outputs/rtab2-ttest-results-raw.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as gt html table (Full Table)

# Build gt table (grouped by outcome/measure)
rfig2_ttest_results_full_html <- rfig2_ttest_results_full |>
  gt(groupname_col = "measure") |>
  cols_label(
    subgroup_type_label = "Group",
    scale       = "Measure",
    subgroup    = "Subgroup",
    n           = "N",
    pct_total   = "% N",
    mean_ci     = "Mean Change (95% CI)",
    p_value_fmt = html("p-value")
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(subgroup_type_label, scale, subgroup)) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, pct_total, mean_ci, p_value_fmt)) |>
  cols_hide(columns = c(subgroup_type, mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value)) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(pct_total), decimals = 1) |>
  #tab_header(
    #title = md("**RFig2. Paired t-test results for 6-month change in all outcomes**"),
    #subtitle = md("Complete-case paired analyses performed separately for each outcome.")
  #) |>
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Paired Change Estimate",
    columns = c(mean_ci, p_value_fmt)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    row_group.font.weight = "600",
    data_row.padding = px(4),
    row_group.padding = px(6)
  )

# Save as HTML
gtsave(
  rfig2_ttest_results_full_html,
  filename = "rtab2-ttest-results-full-html.html",
  path = "Final Outputs/"
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as gt html table (Absolute Mean Table)

# Build gt table (grouped by outcome/measure)
rfig2_ttest_results_abs_html <- rfig2_ttest_results_abs |>
  gt(groupname_col = "measure") |>
  cols_label(
    subgroup_type_label = "Group",
    #scale       = "Measure",
    subgroup    = "Subgroup",
    n           = "N",
    pct_total   = "% N",
    mean_ci     = "Mean Change (Abs, 95% CI)",
    p_value_fmt = html("p-value")
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(subgroup_type_label, subgroup)) |> # scale removed
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, pct_total, mean_ci, p_value_fmt)) |>
  cols_hide(columns = c(subgroup_type, mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value)) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(pct_total), decimals = 1) |>
  #tab_header(
    #title = md("**RFig2. Paired t-test results for 6-month change in all outcomes**"),
    #subtitle = md("Complete-case paired analyses performed separately for each outcome.")
  #) |>
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Paired Change Estimate (Abs)",
    columns = c(mean_ci, p_value_fmt)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    row_group.font.weight = "600",
    data_row.padding = px(4),
    row_group.padding = px(6)
  )

# Save as HTML
gtsave(
  rfig2_ttest_results_abs_html,
  filename = "rtab2-ttest-results-full-abs-html.html",
  path = "Final Outputs/"
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as gt html table (Percentage Mean Table)

# Build gt table (grouped by outcome/measure)
rfig2_ttest_results_pct_html <- rfig2_ttest_results_pct |>
  gt(groupname_col = "measure") |>
  cols_label(
    subgroup_type_label = "Group",
    #scale       = "Measure",
    subgroup    = "Subgroup",
    n           = "N",
    pct_total   = "% N",
    mean_ci     = "Mean Change (%, 95% CI)",
    p_value_fmt = html("p-value")
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(subgroup_type_label, subgroup)) |> # scale removed
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = c(n, pct_total, mean_ci, p_value_fmt)) |>
  cols_hide(columns = c(subgroup_type, mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value)) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(pct_total), decimals = 1) |>
  #tab_header(
    #title = md("**RFig2. Paired t-test results for 6-month change in all outcomes**"),
    #subtitle = md("Complete-case paired analyses performed separately for each outcome.")
  #) |>
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Paired Change Estimate (%)",
    columns = c(mean_ci, p_value_fmt)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    row_group.font.weight = "600",
    data_row.padding = px(4),
    row_group.padding = px(6)
  )

# Save as HTML
gtsave(
  rfig2_ttest_results_pct_html,
  filename = "rtab2-ttest-results-full-pct-html.html",
  path = "Final Outputs/"
)

```



################################################################################ 
# RTab3. Marginal adjusted mean 6 month change in all outcomes
################################################################################ 

Used the same format and variables as the paired t-test table

Create custom function for table creation
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Custom function: adjusted change table via linear regression on change score

# Outputs marginal (standardized) adjusted mean change
# (i.e. mean over model-predicted change across observed covariate distribution)
# Model: change ~ age_cat5 + eth_bin + area_imd_qt + wt_kg_0m_cat

adjusted_change_table <- function(dt,
                                  base_var,
                                  fu_var,
                                  subgroup_var = NULL,
                                  measure_label = NULL,
                                  pct_denominator = c("baseline", "followup"),
                                  adjust_vars = c("age_cat5", "eth_bin", "area_imd_qt", "wt_kg_0m_cat"),
                                  digits_change = 2,
                                  digits_p = 3) {

  stopifnot(data.table::is.data.table(dt))
  pct_denominator <- match.arg(pct_denominator)

  `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b

  fmt_p <- function(p) {
    ifelse(
      is.na(p), NA_character_,
      ifelse(p < 10^(-digits_p),
             paste0("<", formatC(10^(-digits_p), format = "f", digits = digits_p)),
             formatC(p, format = "f", digits = digits_p))
    )
  }

  # Ensure subgroup exists if requested
  d0 <- data.table::copy(dt)
  if (!is.null(subgroup_var)) {
    d0[, (subgroup_var) := as.character(get(subgroup_var))]
    d0[is.na(get(subgroup_var)), (subgroup_var) := "Missing"]
  }

  # Core complete-case for base+follow-up
  d <- d0[
    !is.na(get(base_var)) & !is.na(get(fu_var)) &
      is.finite(get(base_var)) & is.finite(get(fu_var))
  ]
  if (nrow(d) == 0L) return(data.table::data.table())

  # Require complete-case on adjustment covariates too
  keep_vars <- unique(c(base_var, fu_var, subgroup_var, adjust_vars))
  keep_vars <- keep_vars[!is.null(keep_vars)]
  d <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, ..keep_vars])]
  if (nrow(d) == 0L) return(data.table::data.table())

  # Denominator for pct_total is the complete-case N for THIS measure (abs rows)
  N_total_abs <- nrow(d)

  # Build formula
  rhs <- paste(adjust_vars, collapse = " + ")
  fml <- stats::as.formula(paste0("chg ~ ", rhs))

  #-----------------------------#
  # Helper: marginal mean + SE + CI + p for a fitted lm over rows in x
  marginal_from_lm <- function(fit, x) {
    # model matrix for these observed rows (same coding as fit)
    X <- stats::model.matrix(fit, data = x)
    xbar <- colMeans(X)

    b <- stats::coef(fit)
    V <- stats::vcov(fit)

    est <- as.numeric(crossprod(xbar, b))
    se  <- as.numeric(sqrt(drop(t(xbar) %*% V %*% xbar)))

    df    <- stats::df.residual(fit)
    tcrit <- stats::qt(0.975, df = df)

    ci <- c(est - tcrit * se, est + tcrit * se)

    tval <- if (is.finite(se) && se > 0) est / se else NA_real_
    pval <- if (!is.na(tval)) 2 * stats::pt(abs(tval), df = df, lower.tail = FALSE) else NA_real_

    list(est = est, se = se, ci = ci, p = pval)
  }

  #-----------------------------#
  # Compute rows within a given group (x is already group-restricted)
  compute_rows <- function(x, subgroup_level = "Full Sample") {

    x <- data.table::copy(x)

    base <- x[[base_var]]
    fu   <- x[[fu_var]]

    #-----------------------------#
    # Absolute change model
    x[, chg := fu - base]
    fit_abs <- stats::lm(fml, data = x)

    m_abs <- marginal_from_lm(fit_abs, x)

    out_abs <- data.table::data.table(
      measure     = measure_label %||% base_var,
      subgroup    = subgroup_level,
      scale       = "Absolute Change",
      n           = nrow(x),
      pct_total   = 100 * nrow(x) / N_total_abs,
      mean_change = m_abs$est,
      ci_low      = unname(m_abs$ci[1]),
      ci_high     = unname(m_abs$ci[2]),
      p_value     = m_abs$p
    )

    #-----------------------------#
    # Percent change model
    den <- if (pct_denominator == "baseline") base else fu
    chg_pct <- ifelse(is.finite(den) & den != 0, (fu - base) / den * 100, NA_real_)

    x_pct <- data.table::copy(x[!is.na(chg_pct)])
    if (nrow(x_pct) > 0L) {

      x_pct[, chg := chg_pct[!is.na(chg_pct)]]
      fit_pct <- stats::lm(fml, data = x_pct)

      m_pct <- marginal_from_lm(fit_pct, x_pct)

      out_pct <- data.table::data.table(
        measure     = measure_label %||% base_var,
        subgroup    = subgroup_level,
        scale       = "Percent Change",
        n           = nrow(x_pct),
        pct_total   = 100 * nrow(x_pct) / N_total_abs,
        mean_change = m_pct$est,
        ci_low      = unname(m_pct$ci[1]),
        ci_high     = unname(m_pct$ci[2]),
        p_value     = m_pct$p
      )

    } else {

      out_pct <- data.table::data.table(
        measure     = measure_label %||% base_var,
        subgroup    = subgroup_level,
        scale       = "Percent Change",
        n           = 0,
        pct_total   = 0,
        mean_change = NA_real_,
        ci_low      = NA_real_,
        ci_high     = NA_real_,
        p_value     = NA_real_
      )
    }

    data.table::rbindlist(list(out_abs, out_pct), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
  }

  # Run full sample or stratified
  if (is.null(subgroup_var)) {
    res <- compute_rows(d, subgroup_level = "Full Sample")
  } else {
    res <- d[, compute_rows(.SD, subgroup_level = get(subgroup_var)[1L]), by = subgroup_var]
    data.table::setnames(res, old = subgroup_var, new = "subgroup", skip_absent = TRUE)
  }

  # Formatting / rounding
  res[, `:=`(
    mean_change = round(mean_change, digits_change),
    ci_low      = round(ci_low, digits_change),
    ci_high     = round(ci_high, digits_change),
    pct_total   = round(pct_total, 1),
    p_value_fmt = fmt_p(p_value)
  )]

  res[, .(measure, scale, subgroup, n, pct_total, mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value, p_value_fmt)]
}

```

Create table for all measures
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create function to build adjusted tables for all outcomes of interest

build_all_adjusted_change_tables <- function(data_wide) {

  out <- list(
    # 1) Weight
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "wt_kg_0m",
      fu_var   = "wt_kg_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "Weight"
    ),
    
    # 1b) Weight (by baseline weight categories)
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "wt_kg_0m",
      fu_var   = "wt_kg_6m",
      subgroup_var = "wt_kg_0m_cat",
      measure_label = "Weight",
      # drop wt_kg_0m_cat from regression due to stratification
      adjust_vars = c("age_cat5", "eth_bin", "area_imd_qt")  
    ),

    # 2) HbA1c (full sample)
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "HbA1c"
    ),

    # 2) HbA1c (by diabetic categories)
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m",
      subgroup_var = "hba1c_cat_0m",
      measure_label = "HbA1c"
    ),

    # 3) Total Cholesterol
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_chol_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_chol_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "Total Cholesterol"
    ),

    # 4) HDL
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_hdl_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_hdl_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "HDL"
    ),

    # 5) LDL
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_ldl_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_ldl_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "LDL"
    ),

    # 6) Triglycerides
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_tg_mmoll_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_tg_mmoll_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "Triglycerides"
    ),

    # 7) ALT
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_alt_ul_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_alt_ul_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "ALT"
    ),

    # 8) AST
    adjusted_change_table(
      data_wide,
      base_var = "blood_ast_ul_0m",
      fu_var   = "blood_ast_ul_6m",
      subgroup_var = NULL,
      measure_label = "AST"
    )
  )

  res <- data.table::rbindlist(out, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

  # Make output ordering nice
  measure_order <- c(
    "Weight", "HbA1c", "Total Cholesterol", "HDL", "LDL", "Triglycerides", "ALT", "AST"
  )
  res[, measure := factor(measure, levels = measure_order)]
  res[, scale   := factor(scale, levels = c("Absolute Change", "Percent Change"))]

  res[order(measure, subgroup, scale)]
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create and reformat final table

rfig3_adjreg_results <- build_all_adjusted_change_tables(data_wide)

# Standardise subgroup label for display
rfig3_adjreg_results[subgroup == "Full Sample", subgroup := "Full Sample"]

# Recode HbA1c subgroup labels with numeric cut-offs
rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("^Diab", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Basline Diabetic (≥48 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("pre", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Baseline Pre-diabetic (42–47 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("^Norm", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Baseline Normal (<42 mmol/mol)"
]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("^Full", subgroup, ignore.case = TRUE),
  subgroup := "Full Sample"
]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  is.na(subgroup),
  subgroup := "Baseline Missing"
]

#-----------------------------#
# Subgroup ordering (measure specific)

# HbA1c subgroup order (ONLY for HbA1c rows)
hba1c_levels <- c(
  "Full Sample",
  "Basline Diabetic (≥48 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Pre-diabetic (42–47 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Normal (<42 mmol/mol)",
  "Baseline Missing"
)

# Baseline weight subgroup order (ONLY for Weight rows)
wt_levels <- c(
  "Full Sample",
  "<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg",
  "Baseline Missing"
)

# Create an ordering key that depends on measure
rfig3_adjreg_results[, subgroup_order := NA_integer_]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("^HbA1c", as.character(measure)),
  subgroup_order := match(as.character(subgroup), hba1c_levels)
]

rfig3_adjreg_results[
  grepl("^Weight", as.character(measure)),
  subgroup_order := match(as.character(subgroup), wt_levels)
]

# Put non-matching labels after, but keep stable ordering
rfig3_adjreg_results[is.na(subgroup_order), subgroup_order := 999L]

# Order table using this key
data.table::setorder(rfig3_adjreg_results, measure, subgroup_order, subgroup, scale)

# Drop helper col later before export
rfig3_adjreg_results[, subgroup_order := NULL]

#-----------------------------#
# Relabelling

# Add units to outcome labels
rfig3_adjreg_results[, measure := as.character(measure)]
rfig3_adjreg_results[
  ,
  measure := fifelse(measure == "Weight",            "Weight (kg)",
             fifelse(measure == "HbA1c",             "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
             fifelse(measure == "Total Cholesterol", "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "HDL",               "HDL (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "LDL",               "LDL (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "Triglycerides",     "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "ALT",               "ALT (U/L)",
             fifelse(measure == "AST",               "AST (U/L)",
                   measure))))))))
]

# Force measure order
rfig3_adjreg_results[
  ,
  measure := factor(
    measure,
    levels = c(
      "Weight (kg)",
      "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
      "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
      "HDL (mmol/L)",
      "LDL (mmol/L)",
      "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",
      "ALT (U/L)",
      "AST (U/L)"
    )
  )
]

# Force scale order
rfig3_adjreg_results[
  ,
  scale := factor(
    scale,
    levels = c("Absolute Change", "Percent Change")
  )
]

# Create a display column: "Adjusted Mean Change (95% CI)"
rfig3_adjreg_results[
  ,
  mean_ci := sprintf("%.*f (%.*f, %.*f)",
                     2, mean_change, 2, ci_low, 2, ci_high)
]

rfig3_adjreg_results

```

Export table (csv and gt html)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as csv

data.table::fwrite(
  rfig3_adjreg_results,
  file = "Final Outputs/rtab3-adjreg-results-raw.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table as gt html table

rfig3_adjreg_results_html <- rfig3_adjreg_results |>
  gt::gt(groupname_col = "measure") |>
  gt::cols_label(
    scale       = "Measure",
    subgroup    = "Subgroup",
    n           = "N",
    pct_total   = "% N",
    mean_ci     = "Adj. Mean Change (95% CI)",
    p_value_fmt = gt::html("p-value")
  ) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "left",
    columns = c(scale, subgroup)
  ) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(n, pct_total, mean_ci, p_value_fmt)
  ) |>
  gt::cols_hide(columns = c(mean_change, ci_low, ci_high, p_value)) |>
  gt::fmt_number(columns = c(pct_total), decimals = 1) |>
  gt::tab_spanner(
    label = "Marginal Adjusted Change Estimate",
    columns = c(mean_ci, p_value_fmt)
  ) |>
  gt::tab_options(
    table.font.size = gt::px(12),
    row_group.font.weight = "600",
    data_row.padding = gt::px(4),
    row_group.padding = gt::px(6)
  )

gt::gtsave(
  rfig3_adjreg_results_html,
  filename = "rtab3-adjreg-results-html.html",
  path = "Final Outputs/"
)

```



################################################################################ 
# RTab4. Misc estimates for write-up
################################################################################ 

Main paper stats
```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# Recruitment window (registration date range)

summary(data_wide$reg_date)

#-----------------------------#
# GLP-1 Start Date

summary(data_wide$glp1_st_date)

#-----------------------------#
# Average baseline weight and BMI

summary(data_wide$wt_kg_0m)
print(sum_stats(data_wide$wt_kg_0m))

summary(data_wide$bmi_0m)
print(sum_stats(data_wide$bmi_0m))

#-----------------------------#
# Average 6m weight

summary(data_wide$wt_kg_6m)
print(sum_stats(data_wide$wt_kg_6m))

#-----------------------------#
# Baseline Age range

summary(data_wide$age)

#-----------------------------#
# Baseline glp1 (extract directly, as first row is glp1_idx == 0)

table(data_wide$glp1_drug, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_drug, useNA = "always"))*100

#-----------------------------#
# Mounjaro ever vs Wegovy ever

table(data_wide$glp1_mounjaro_ever, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_mounjaro_ever, useNA = "always"))*100

table(data_wide$glp1_wegovy_ever, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_wegovy_ever, useNA = "always"))*100

#-----------------------------#
# Any glp1 switching

# Any switch
table(data_wide$glp1_ever_switch, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_ever_switch, useNA = "always"))*100

# Any Mounjaro to Wegovy
table(data_wide$glp1_mj_to_wg, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_mj_to_wg, useNA = "always"))*100

# Any Wegovy to Mounjaro
table(data_wide$glp1_wg_to_mj, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_wg_to_mj, useNA = "always"))*100

# Extract Wegovy to Mounjaro patients for manual check
data_patchk_wg2mj <- data[glp1_wg_to_mj == 1, 
                          .(patid, glp1_idx, mounjaro_mg_locf, wegovy_mg_locf)]
length(unique(data_patchk_wg2mj$patid))
unique(data_patchk_wg2mj$patid)

#-----------------------------#
# GLP1 type at baseline

table(data_wide$glp1_drug_locf_0m)

#-----------------------------#
# GLP1 type at 6m

table(data_wide$glp1_drug_locf_6m)

#-----------------------------#
# Dosage at baseline

table(data_wide$glp1_dose)
prop.table(table(data_wide$glp1_dose, useNA = "always")) * 100

data_wegovy_bl <- data_wide[glp1_drug_locf_0m == "Wegovy"]
table(data_wegovy_bl$glp1_dose, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wegovy_bl$glp1_dose, useNA = "always")) * 100

data_mounjaro_bl <- data_wide[glp1_drug_locf_0m == "Mounjaro"]
table(data_mounjaro_bl$glp1_dose, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_mounjaro_bl$glp1_dose, useNA = "always")) * 100

#-----------------------------#
# Sum of participants who gained and lost weight

# Lost weight
sum(data_wide$wt_change_kg_06m < 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# Gained weight / stayed same
sum(data_wide$wt_change_kg_06m >= 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# Proportion who lost weight
sum(data_wide$wt_change_kg_06m < 0, na.rm = TRUE) / 
  sum(!is.na(data_wide$wt_change_kg_06m), na.rm = TRUE) * 100

#-----------------------------#
# HbA1c categories by age

# Full sample
table(data_wide$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")

# 50+ T2D
data_50 <- data_wide[age >= 50]
table(data_50$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_50$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")) * 100

# 18-29 T2D
data_1829 <- data_wide[age <= 29]
table(data_1829$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_1829$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")) * 100

#-----------------------------#
# Mean BMI

summary(data_wide$bmi_0m)
print(sum_stats(data_wide$bmi_0m))

summary(data_wide$bmi_6m)
print(sum_stats(data_wide$bmi_6m))

#-----------------------------#
# Side effect severity among participants with any side effects

data_any_sdeff <- data_wide[sdeff_any_ever == 1]

# Max severity
table(data_any_sdeff$most_severe_sdeff, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_any_sdeff$most_severe_sdeff, useNA = "always")) * 100

```

```{r}
head(data_check_noblood0m)

```

Weight change and BMI complete case sample results
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Restrict sample to complete case at 6 months (weight)

data_wt_6m_cc <- data_wide[!is.na(wt_change_kg_06m)]
length(unique(data_wt_6m_cc$patid))

#-----------------------------#
# Baseline and 6m weight

summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_0m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_0m))

summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_6m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_6m))

#-----------------------------#
# Average weight loss 3m-0m

# Distribution
summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_kg_03m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_kg_03m))

summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_03m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_03m))

# t-test
## kg
stats::t.test(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_3m, data_wt_6m_cc$wt_kg_0m, paired = TRUE)
## %
stats::t.test(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_03m, paired = FALSE)

#-----------------------------#
# Average weight loss 6m-0m

# Distribution
summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_kg_06m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_kg_06m))

summary(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m))

# Weight loss targets
mean(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m <= -5) * 100
mean(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m <= -10) * 100
mean(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m <= -15) * 100
mean(data_wt_6m_cc$wt_change_pct_06m <= -20) * 100

#-----------------------------#
# % of total 6m loss achieved by 3m (patient-level proportion)

# Keep only those with BOTH 3m and 6m change observed
data_wt_3m6m_cc <- data_wt_6m_cc[!is.na(wt_change_kg_03m)]

# Convert to positive "loss" amounts
# (wt_change_kg_* are negative for loss, so multiply by -1)
data_wt_3m6m_cc[
  ,
  `:=`(
    loss_3m = -wt_change_kg_03m,
    loss_6m = -wt_change_kg_06m
  )
]

# Individual proportion: fraction of 6m loss achieved by 3m
# Exclude those with no 6m loss or weight gain at 6m (loss_6m <= 0)
data_wt_3m6m_cc[
  ,
  prop_3m_of_6m := fifelse(loss_6m > 0, loss_3m / loss_6m, NA_real_)
]


# Summary
summary(data_wt_3m6m_cc$prop_3m_of_6m)
print(sum_stats(data_wt_3m6m_cc$prop_3m_of_6m))

# Final proportion
mean(data_wt_3m6m_cc$prop_3m_of_6m, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

# Ready-to-paste sentence numbers (rounded)
sprintf(
  "On average, %.1f%% of total 6-month weight loss was achieved by 3 months.",
  mean(data_wt_3m6m_cc$prop_3m_of_6m, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Restrict sample to complete case at 6 months (BMI)

data_bmi_6m_cc <- data_wide[!is.na(bmi_0m) & !is.na(bmi_6m)]
length(unique(data_bmi_6m_cc$patid))

#-----------------------------#
# Baseline and 6m weight

summary(data_bmi_6m_cc$bmi_0m)
print(sum_stats(data_bmi_6m_cc$bmi_0m))

summary(data_bmi_6m_cc$bmi_6m)
print(sum_stats(data_bmi_6m_cc$bmi_6m))

```
