```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# HbA1c

# Select complete case sample
data_hba1c_06m_cc <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)
]
length(unique(data_hba1c_06m_cc$patid))

# HbA1c
print(sum_stats(data_hba1c_06m_cc$blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m))
print(sum_stats(data_hba1c_06m_cc$blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m))

# HbA1c categorical
table(data_hba1c_06m_cc$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_hba1c_06m_cc$hba1c_cat_0m, useNA = "always"))*100

```

Treatment discontinuation and side effect counts
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Side effects among attrited patients

# Count of attrited patients
table(data_wide$attr_ever_yn, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_wide$attr_ever_yn, useNA = "always"))

# Subset attrited patients
data_attr_ever <- data_wide[attr_ever_yn == TRUE]
length(unique(data_attr_ever$patid))

# Check ever side effect
table(data_attr_ever$sdeff_any_ever, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_attr_ever$sdeff_any_ever, useNA = "always")) * 100

# Check total side effect count
table(data_attr_ever$sdeff_total_n, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_attr_ever$sdeff_total_n, useNA = "always")) * 100

# Check max severity
table(data_attr_ever$most_severe_sdeff, useNA = "always")
prop.table(table(data_attr_ever$most_severe_sdeff, useNA = "always")) * 100


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Side effect symptom counts

# Vector of symptom columns
symptom_cols <- c(
  "sdeff_symptom_abdpain",
  "sdeff_symptom_acidref",
  "sdeff_symptom_bloat",
  "sdeff_symptom_constip",
  "sdeff_symptom_diarr",
  "sdeff_symptom_dizzy",
  "sdeff_symptom_flat",
  "sdeff_symptom_hairloss",
  "sdeff_symptom_headache",
  "sdeff_symptom_injsite",
  "sdeff_symptom_nausea",
  "sdeff_symptom_other",
  "sdeff_symptom_palpit",
  "sdeff_symptom_rash",
  "sdeff_symptom_selfharm",
  "sdeff_symptom_tired",
  "sdeff_symptom_visual",
  "sdeff_symptom_vomit"
)

# Create summary table
symptom_summary <- data[
  ,
  lapply(.SD, function(x) sum(x == 1, na.rm = TRUE)),
  .SDcols = symptom_cols
]

# Convert to long format
symptom_summary <- melt(
  symptom_summary,
  measure.vars = symptom_cols,
  variable.name = "symptom",
  value.name = "n_total"
)

symptom_summary


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Treatment discontinuation at 6 months by care package use

#-----------------------------#
# Manually check summary stats

# Create subset who experienced any side effects
data_any_sdeff <- data_wide[sdeff_any_ever == TRUE]
length(unique(data_any_sdeff$patid))

# Further subset to any use of care package
data_any_sdeff_use <- data_any_sdeff[sdeff_carepack_ever == 1]
data_any_sdeff_nouse <- data_any_sdeff[sdeff_carepack_ever == 0]
length(unique(data_any_sdeff_use$patid))
length(unique(data_any_sdeff_nouse$patid))

# Check proportions of attrition
table(data_any_sdeff_use$attr_ever_yn, useNA = "always")
table(data_any_sdeff_nouse$attr_ever_yn, useNA = "always")

#-----------------------------#
# Perform two-proportion z-test

# Summarise by care package use (drop NAs to avoid mis-ordering / extra row)
tab_sdeff_carepack <- data_any_sdeff[
  !is.na(sdeff_carepack_ever) & !is.na(attr_ever_yn),
  .(
    n_total = uniqueN(patid),
    n_drop  = uniqueN(patid[attr_ever_yn == 1L])
  ),
  by = sdeff_carepack_ever
]

# Order so 0 = No Use, 1 = Use
setorder(tab_sdeff_carepack, sdeff_carepack_ever)

# Extract counts
x <- tab_sdeff_carepack$n_drop
n <- tab_sdeff_carepack$n_total

# Proportions
p_sdeff_carepack <- x / n

# Two-proportion z-test via prop.test (no Yates correction)
ztest_sdeff_carepack <- prop.test(x, n, correct = FALSE)

# Print clean result
cat(sprintf(
  "Dropout was %.1f%% (%d/%d) in the no care package group vs %.1f%% (%d/%d) in the care package group (two-proportion z-test, p = %.3f).\n",
  100 * p_sdeff_carepack[1], x[1], n[1],
  100 * p_sdeff_carepack[2], x[2], n[2],
  ztest_sdeff_carepack$p.value
))

```

BMI category transition tables
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create transition matrix table for BMI categories

#-----------------------------#
# NAs included

# Note: "Healthy (18.5–24.9)" does not exist in bmi_0m_cat (baseline), but it DOES
# exist in bmi_6m_cat (6m). We therefore include it in the expected column order
# so the cast table always has a consistent set of columns.
cat_order <- c(
  "Healthy (18.5–24.9)",
  "Overweight (25.0–29.9)",
  "Obesity I (30.0–34.9)",
  "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
  "Obesity III (≥40.0)",
  "Missing"
)

tab_bmi_trans_na_incl <- data_wide[
  ,
  .N,
  by = .(
    bmi_0m_cat = factor(fcoalesce(as.character(bmi_0m_cat), "Missing"), levels = cat_order),
    bmi_6m_cat = factor(fcoalesce(as.character(bmi_6m_cat), "Missing"), levels = cat_order)
  )
]

tab_bmi_trans_na_incl <- dcast(
  tab_bmi_trans_na_incl,
  bmi_0m_cat ~ bmi_6m_cat,
  value.var = "N",
  fill = 0
)

# Ensure all expected columns exist (dcast won't create absent levels)
missing_cols <- setdiff(cat_order, names(tab_bmi_trans_na_incl))
if (length(missing_cols)) tab_bmi_trans_na_incl[, (missing_cols) := 0L]

setcolorder(tab_bmi_trans_na_incl, c("bmi_0m_cat", cat_order))

# Add row total
tab_bmi_trans_na_incl[, row_total := rowSums(.SD), .SDcols = cat_order]

# Convert count columns to character so assignment of strings is legal
tab_bmi_trans_na_incl[, (cat_order) := lapply(.SD, as.character), .SDcols = cat_order]

# Now overwrite with "n (row%)"
tab_bmi_trans_na_incl[
  row_total > 0,
  (cat_order) := Map(
    function(x) sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", as.integer(x), 100 * as.integer(x) / row_total),
    .SD
  ),
  .SDcols = cat_order
]

tab_bmi_trans_na_incl

fwrite(
  tab_bmi_trans_na_incl,
  file = "rtab4-tab-bmi-trans-na-incl.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

#-----------------------------#
# NAs excluded (skipped counts of missing values)

# Redefine order with missing label removed
cat_order_cc <- c(
  "Healthy (18.5–24.9)",
  "Overweight (25.0–29.9)",
  "Obesity I (30.0–34.9)",
  "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
  "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
)

# Only keep complete case sample here
tab_bmi_trans_na_excl <- data_wide[
  !is.na(bmi_0m_cat) & !is.na(bmi_6m_cat),
  .N,
  by = .(
    bmi_0m_cat = factor(as.character(bmi_0m_cat), levels = cat_order_cc),
    bmi_6m_cat = factor(as.character(bmi_6m_cat), levels = cat_order_cc)
  )
]

tab_bmi_trans_na_excl <- dcast(
  tab_bmi_trans_na_excl,
  bmi_0m_cat ~ bmi_6m_cat,
  value.var = "N",
  fill = 0
)

# Ensure all expected columns exist (dcast won't create absent levels)
missing_cols_cc <- setdiff(cat_order_cc, names(tab_bmi_trans_na_excl))
if (length(missing_cols_cc)) tab_bmi_trans_na_excl[, (missing_cols_cc) := 0L]

setcolorder(tab_bmi_trans_na_excl, c("bmi_0m_cat", cat_order_cc))

# Add row total
tab_bmi_trans_na_excl[, row_total := rowSums(.SD), .SDcols = cat_order_cc]

# Convert count columns to character so assignment of strings is legal
tab_bmi_trans_na_excl[, (cat_order_cc) := lapply(.SD, as.character), .SDcols = cat_order_cc]

# Now overwrite with "n (row%)"
tab_bmi_trans_na_excl[
  row_total > 0,
  (cat_order_cc) := Map(
    function(x) sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", as.integer(x), 100 * as.integer(x) / row_total),
    .SD
  ),
  .SDcols = cat_order_cc
]

tab_bmi_trans_na_excl

fwrite(
  tab_bmi_trans_na_excl,
  file = "rtab4-tab-bmi-trans-na-excl.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

```


################################################################################ 
# RTab5. Characeristics Tables for Patients with Missing Data
################################################################################ 

Patient characteristics table for individuals with missing weight change
(Appear similar to full sample)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create baseline characteristics table

#-----------------------------#
# Restrict to patients iwth missing weight change

data_missing_wtchange <- data_wide[is.na(wt_change_kg_06m)]

#-----------------------------#
# Assign baseline weight (categorical) as strata

strata <- c(
  list(Total = data_missing_wtchange),
  split(data_missing_wtchange, data_missing_wtchange$wt_kg_0m_cat, drop = TRUE)
)

#-----------------------------#
# Assign labels to variables

labels <- list(
  variables = list(
    # Sex
    sex                     = "Sex",
    
    # Age
    age_cat5                = "Age",

    # Physical Measures (skip weight)
    bmi_0m_cat              = "BMI (kg/m\u00B2)",

    # Lipids
    blood_chol_mmoll_0m      = "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_hdl_mmoll_0m       = "HDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_ldl_mmoll_0m       = "LDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_tg_mmoll_0m        = "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",

    # Glycemic Measure
    blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m   = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    
    # Sociodemographics
    eth_bin                 = "Ethnicity",
    area_imd_qt             = "Index of Multiple Deprivation (Quintile)"
  ),

  # Column group header (matches the blog example layout)
  groups = list("", "Baseline Weight")
)

groupspan <- c(1, length(strata) - 1)

#-----------------------------#
# Create table of baseline characteristics

rtab5_desctab_missing_wtchange <- table1(
  x           = strata,
  labels      = labels,
  groupspan   = groupspan,
  # Override median to show IQR instead of Min/Max
  render.continuous = c(
    "Mean (SD)"        = "mean (sd)",
    "Median (IQR)"     = "q50 (q25, q75)",
    "Range [Min, Max]" = "min, max"
  )
  #footnote    = footnote,
  #render.continuous  = my.render.cont,
  #render.categorical = my.render.cat
)

rtab5_desctab_missing_wtchange


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table
## Easiest way is the export through RStudio viewer (save as webpage)


```

Patient characteristics table for individuals with missing hba1c change
(Appear similar to full sample)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create baseline characteristics table

#-----------------------------#
# Restrict to patients iwth missing weight change

data_missing_hba1cchange <- data_wide[is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) | 
                                     is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)]

#-----------------------------#
# Assign baseline weight (categorical) as strata

strata <- c(
  list(Total = data_missing_hba1cchange),
  split(data_missing_hba1cchange, data_missing_hba1cchange$wt_kg_0m_cat, drop = TRUE)
)

#-----------------------------#
# Assign labels to variables

labels <- list(
  variables = list(
    # Sex
    sex                     = "Sex",
    
    # Age
    age_cat5                = "Age",

    # Physical Measures (skip weight)
    bmi_0m_cat              = "BMI (kg/m\u00B2)",

    # Lipids
    blood_chol_mmoll_0m      = "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_hdl_mmoll_0m       = "HDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_ldl_mmoll_0m       = "LDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_tg_mmoll_0m        = "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",

    # Glycemic Measure
    blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m   = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    
    # Sociodemographics
    eth_bin                 = "Ethnicity",
    area_imd_qt             = "Index of Multiple Deprivation (Quintile)"
  ),

  # Column group header (matches the blog example layout)
  groups = list("", "Baseline Weight")
)

groupspan <- c(1, length(strata) - 1)

#-----------------------------#
# Create table of baseline characteristics

rtab5_desctab_missing_hba1cchange <- table1(
  x           = strata,
  labels      = labels,
  groupspan   = groupspan,
  # Override median to show IQR instead of Min/Max
  render.continuous = c(
    "Mean (SD)"        = "mean (sd)",
    "Median (IQR)"     = "q50 (q25, q75)",
    "Range [Min, Max]" = "min, max"
  )
  #footnote    = footnote,
  #render.continuous  = my.render.cont,
  #render.categorical = my.render.cat
)

rtab5_desctab_missing_hba1cchange


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table
## Easiest way is the export through RStudio viewer (save as webpage)


```

(Appear similar to full sample)

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#

data_missing_cholchange <- data_wide[is.na(blood_chol_mmoll_0m) | 
                                     is.na(blood_chol_mmoll_6m)]

#-----------------------------#
```

################################################################################ 
# MFig1. CONSORT style diagram
################################################################################ 

**Completed in a separate powerpoint document**



################################################################################ 
# MTab1. Patient characteristics table (by weight)
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create baseline characteristics table

#-----------------------------#
# Assign baseline weight (categorical) as strata

strata <- c(
  list(Total = data_wide),
  split(data_wide, data_wide$wt_kg_0m_cat, drop = TRUE)
)

#-----------------------------#
# Assign labels to variables

labels <- list(
  variables = list(
    # Sex
    sex                     = "Sex",
    
    # Age
    age_cat5                = "Age",

    # Physical Measures (skip weight)
    bmi_0m_cat              = "BMI (kg/m\u00B2)",

    # Lipids
    blood_chol_mmoll_0m      = "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_hdl_mmoll_0m       = "HDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_ldl_mmoll_0m       = "LDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_tg_mmoll_0m        = "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",

    # Glycemic Measure
    blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m   = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    hba1c_cat_0m_lab         = "HbA1c Category",
    
    # Sociodemographics
    eth_bin                 = "Ethnicity",
    area_imd_qt             = "Index of Multiple Deprivation (Quintile)"
  ),

  # Column group header (matches the blog example layout)
  groups = list("", "Baseline Weight")
)

groupspan <- c(1, length(strata) - 1)

#-----------------------------#
# Define render settings (For setting digits and choosing mean/median if desired)

#my.render.cont <- function(x) {
#  s <- stats.apply.rounding(stats.default(x), digits = 0)  # change digits here
#  c("",
#    "Mean (SD)" = sprintf("%s (\u00B1 %s)", s$MEAN, s$SD)
#  )
#}

#my.render.cat <- function(x) {
#  c("",
#    sapply(stats.default(x), function(y) with(y,
#      sprintf("%d (%0.0f %%)", FREQ, PCT)
#    ))
#  )
#}

#-----------------------------#
# Create table of baseline characteristics

mtab1_descstat_wt <- table1(
  x           = strata,
  labels      = labels,
  groupspan   = groupspan,
  # Override median to show IQR instead of Min/Max
  render.continuous = c(
    "Mean (SD)"        = "mean (sd)",
    "Median (IQR)"     = "q50 (q25, q75)",
    "Range [Min, Max]" = "min, max"
  )
  #footnote    = footnote,
  #render.continuous  = my.render.cont,
  #render.categorical = my.render.cat
)

mtab1_descstat_wt


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table
## Easiest way is the export through RStudio viewer (save as webpage)
## Then print as PDF through a browswer or Adobe Acrobat

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig2. Weight change trajectory and waterfall plots (main)
################################################################################ 

Data Setup
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Data setup

# Create last week index with a valid weight per patient
# Note that linearly interpolated weight was used
data[, last_wt_glp1_idx := {
  idx <- glp1_idx[!is.na(wt_kg_lin) & is.finite(wt_kg_lin)]
  if (length(idx)) max(idx) else NA_integer_
}, by = patid]

# Check patients without valid last_wt_glp1_idx
data[, .N, by = .(is_na = is.na(last_wt_glp1_idx))][order(is_na)]
data[is.na(last_wt_glp1_idx), .(patid, n_rows = .N), by = patid]

# Keep only rows from glp1_idx == 0 up to that last valid week
data_ribplot <- data[
  !is.na(last_wt_glp1_idx) & glp1_idx >= 0L & glp1_idx <= last_wt_glp1_idx
]

# Keep only rows up to glp1_idx == 26 (6 months of follow-up)
data_ribplot <- data_ribplot[glp1_idx <= 26]

# Check patient length
length(unique(data_ribplot$patid))

```

Summary Table for Subtitles - Calculate mean change + counts
```{r}
# Restrict to complete case sample
data_wt <- data_wide[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_06m) &
  !is.na(wt_change_pct_06m),
  .(patid, wt_change_kg_06m, wt_change_pct_06m)
]

# Create weight change summary table (to add as subtitles)
tab_wt_change_sum <- data_wt[, {
  
  n_total <- .N
  n_loss  <- sum(wt_change_kg_06m < 0)
  n_noloss <- n_total - n_loss
  
  .(
    N = n_total,
    
    # Absolute change
    mean_kg   = mean(wt_change_kg_06m),
    median_kg = median(wt_change_kg_06m),
    iqr_kg_l  = quantile(wt_change_kg_06m, 0.25),
    iqr_kg_u  = quantile(wt_change_kg_06m, 0.75),
    
    # Percentage change
    mean_pct   = mean(wt_change_pct_06m),
    median_pct = median(wt_change_pct_06m),
    iqr_pct_l  = quantile(wt_change_pct_06m, 0.25),
    iqr_pct_u  = quantile(wt_change_pct_06m, 0.75),
    
    # Counts
    n_loss     = n_loss,
    pct_loss   = 100 * n_loss / n_total,
    n_noloss   = n_noloss,
    pct_noloss = 100 * n_noloss / n_total
  )
}]

tab_wt_change_sum

# Create subtitle (absolute)
wt_change_subtitle_kg <- tab_wt_change_sum[, sprintf(
  "Participants achieving weight loss at six months: %d/%d (%.1f%%)",
  n_loss, N, pct_loss
)]

# Create subtitle (percentage)
wt_change_subtitle_pct <- tab_wt_change_sum[, sprintf(
  "Participants achieving weight loss at six months: %d/%d (%.1f%%)",
  n_loss, N, pct_loss
)]

```

a) Quantile Ribbon Plot in kg (wk 0-26 - 6 month fu)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create quantile ribbon plot (kg)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot data prep

# Calculate quantiles by week (mean + percentile ribbon) + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_kg <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_kg_timevar),
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_kg_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    p05 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p10 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p25 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p50 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p75 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p90 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p95 = quantile(wt_change_kg_timevar, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = glp1_idx
][order(glp1_idx)]

print(data_ribplot_qt_kg)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig2a_ribplot_kg_main <- ggplot(data_ribplot_qt_kg, aes(x = glp1_idx)) +

  # Central 90% (p05–p95)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95, fill = "Central 90%"),
    alpha = 0.10
  ) +
  # Central 80% (p10–p90)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p10, ymax = p90, fill = "Central 80%"),
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  # Central 50% (p25–p75)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p25, ymax = p75, fill = "Central 50%"),
    alpha = 0.30
  ) +

  # Mean line (mapped to fill so it shares the same legend)
  geom_line(
    aes(y = mean, fill = "Mean"),
    colour = "#0B2A5B",
    linewidth = 1.2,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Percentile Range",
    breaks = c("Central 90%", "Central 80%", "Central 50%", "Mean"),
    values = c(
      "Central 90%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Central 80%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Central 50%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Mean"      = NA
    )
  ) +

  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(
        alpha    = c(0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 1),
        linetype = c(0, 0, 0, 1),
        colour   = c("#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B")
      )
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Weight Change (kg)",
    title = "Absolute Weight Change Over 6 Months",
    subtitle = wt_change_subtitle_kg
  ) +

  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.key.height = unit(1.1, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.6, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
      ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
      by = 2
    )
  )

plot(mfig2a_ribplot_kg_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Patient count table data prep

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks in main plot) 
x_breaks <- seq( 
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  by = 2 
)

# Use LOCF drug + numeric LOCF dose so every week reflects "current" prescribed dose
data_ribplot_ptcount <- data_ribplot[
  glp1_idx %in% x_breaks &
    !is.na(glp1_drug_locf) &
    attr_status == 0L,   # keep consistent with LOCF construction (pre-attrition)
  .(
    n_mounjaro      = uniqueN(patid[glp1_drug_locf == "Mounjaro"]),
    mean_mounjaro_mg = mean(mounjaro_mg_locf_num[glp1_drug_locf == "Mounjaro"], na.rm = TRUE),

    n_wegovy        = uniqueN(patid[glp1_drug_locf == "Wegovy"]),
    mean_wegovy_mg   = mean(wegovy_mg_locf_num[glp1_drug_locf == "Wegovy"], na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = glp1_idx
][order(glp1_idx)]

# Format for printing in the table
data_ribplot_ptcount_long <- melt(
  data_ribplot_ptcount,
  id.vars = "glp1_idx",
  measure.vars = c("n_mounjaro", "mean_mounjaro_mg", "n_wegovy", "mean_wegovy_mg"),
  variable.name = "metric",
  value.name = "value"
)

data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, metric_lab := fcase(
  metric == "n_mounjaro",       "Tirzepatide\n(No. of patients)",
  metric == "mean_mounjaro_mg", "Tirzepatide\n(Mean dose, mg)",
  metric == "n_wegovy",         "Semaglutide\n(No. of patients)",
  metric == "mean_wegovy_mg",   "Semaglutide\n(Mean dose, mg)",
  default = metric
)]

# Control row ordering in the table
data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, metric_lab := factor(
  metric_lab,
  levels = c("Tirzepatide\n(No. of patients)", 
             "Tirzepatide\n(Mean dose, mg)", 
             "Semaglutide\n(No. of patients)", 
             "Semaglutide\n(Mean dose, mg)")
)]

# Display formatting: integers for N, 1dp for mg
data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, label := fifelse(
  metric %chin% c("n_mounjaro", "n_wegovy"),
  formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","),
  fifelse(
    metric %chin% c("mean_mounjaro_mg", "mean_wegovy_mg") & is.finite(value),
    formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 1),
    ""
  )
)]

#-------------------------------#
# Patient count + mean dose table (stratified by glp-1 type)

# Create patient count table
mfig2a_ribplot_kg_ptcount <- ggplot(data_ribplot_ptcount_long, 
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = metric_lab, label = label)) +
  geom_text(size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(data_ribplot_ptcount_long$metric_lab))) +
  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = seq_along(levels(data_ribplot_ptcount_long$metric_lab)) + 0.5,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.margin  = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5, l = 5.5)
  ) +
  ggtitle("No. of Patients & Mean Prescribed Dose")

plot(mfig2a_ribplot_kg_ptcount)

#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig2a_ribplot_kg <- mfig2a_ribplot_kg_main / mfig2a_ribplot_kg_ptcount + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig2a_ribplot_kg)

```

b) Quantile Ribbon Plot in percentage (wk 0-26 - 6 month fu)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create quantile ribbon plot (%)

# Calculate quantiles by week (mean + percentile ribbon) + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_pct <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_pct_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_pct_timevar),
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_pct_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    p05 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p10 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p25 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p50 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p75 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p90 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    p95 = quantile(wt_change_pct_timevar, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = glp1_idx
][order(glp1_idx)]

# *Limit rows between 0-26*
data_ribplot_qt_pct <- data_ribplot_qt_pct[glp1_idx <= 26]

print(data_ribplot_qt_pct)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig2b_ribplot_pct_main <- ggplot(data_ribplot_qt_pct, aes(x = glp1_idx)) +

  # Central 90% (p05–p95)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p05, ymax = p95, fill = "Central 90%"),
    alpha = 0.10
  ) +
  # Central 80% (p10–p90)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p10, ymax = p90, fill = "Central 80%"),
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  # Central 50% (p25–p75)
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = p25, ymax = p75, fill = "Central 50%"),
    alpha = 0.30
  ) +

  # Mean line (mapped to fill so it shares the same legend)
  geom_line(
    aes(y = mean, fill = "Mean"),
    colour = "#0B2A5B",
    linewidth = 1.2,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "Percentile Range",
    breaks = c("Central 90%", "Central 80%", "Central 50%", "Mean"),
    values = c(
      "Central 90%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Central 80%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Central 50%" = "#0B2A5B",
      "Mean"      = NA
    )
  ) +

  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(
        alpha    = c(0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 1),
        linetype = c(0, 0, 0, 1),
        colour   = c("#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B", "#0B2A5B")
      )
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Weight Change (%)",
    title = "Percentage Weight Change Over 6 Months",
    subtitle = wt_change_subtitle_pct
  ) +

  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.key.height = unit(1.1, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.6, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_pct$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
      ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_pct$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
      by = 2
    )
  )

plot(mfig2b_ribplot_pct_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Patient count table data prep

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks in main plot) 
x_breaks <- seq( 
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_pct$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_pct$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)), 
  by = 2 
)

# Use LOCF drug + numeric LOCF dose so every week reflects "current" prescribed dose
data_ribplot_ptcount <- data_ribplot[
  glp1_idx %in% x_breaks &
    !is.na(glp1_drug_locf) &
    attr_status == 0L,   # keep consistent with LOCF construction (pre-attrition)
  .(
    n_mounjaro      = uniqueN(patid[glp1_drug_locf == "Mounjaro"]),
    mean_mounjaro_mg = mean(mounjaro_mg_locf_num[glp1_drug_locf == "Mounjaro"], na.rm = TRUE),

    n_wegovy        = uniqueN(patid[glp1_drug_locf == "Wegovy"]),
    mean_wegovy_mg   = mean(wegovy_mg_locf_num[glp1_drug_locf == "Wegovy"], na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = glp1_idx
][order(glp1_idx)]

# Format for printing in the table
data_ribplot_ptcount_long <- melt(
  data_ribplot_ptcount,
  id.vars = "glp1_idx",
  measure.vars = c("n_mounjaro", "mean_mounjaro_mg", "n_wegovy", "mean_wegovy_mg"),
  variable.name = "metric",
  value.name = "value"
)

data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, metric_lab := fcase(
  metric == "n_mounjaro",       "Tirzepatide\n(No. of patients)",
  metric == "mean_mounjaro_mg", "Tirzepatide\n(Mean dose, mg)",
  metric == "n_wegovy",         "Semaglutide\n(No. of patients)",
  metric == "mean_wegovy_mg",   "Semaglutide\n(Mean dose, mg)",
  default = metric
)]

# Control row ordering in the table
data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, metric_lab := factor(
  metric_lab,
  levels = c("Tirzepatide\n(No. of patients)", 
             "Tirzepatide\n(Mean dose, mg)", 
             "Semaglutide\n(No. of patients)", 
             "Semaglutide\n(Mean dose, mg)")
)]

# Display formatting: integers for N, 1dp for mg
data_ribplot_ptcount_long[, label := fifelse(
  metric %chin% c("n_mounjaro", "n_wegovy"),
  formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","),
  fifelse(
    metric %chin% c("mean_mounjaro_mg", "mean_wegovy_mg") & is.finite(value),
    formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 1),
    ""
  )
)]

#-------------------------------#
# Patient count + mean dose table (stratified by glp-1 type)

# Create patient count table
mfig2b_ribplot_pct_ptcount <- ggplot(data_ribplot_ptcount_long, 
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = metric_lab, label = label)) +
  geom_text(size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(data_ribplot_ptcount_long$metric_lab))) +
  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = seq_along(levels(data_ribplot_ptcount_long$metric_lab)) + 0.5,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.margin  = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5, l = 5.5)
  ) +
  ggtitle("No. of Patients & Mean Prescribed Dose")

plot(mfig2b_ribplot_pct_ptcount)

#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig2b_ribplot_pct <- mfig2b_ribplot_pct_main / mfig2b_ribplot_pct_ptcount + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig2b_ribplot_pct)

```

c) Waterfall Plot for Weight Change in kg (wk 0-26 - 6 month fu)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m weight change (kg)

# Build change table (complete cases for weight only)
data_wf_wt_kg <- data_wide[
  !is.na(wt_kg_0m) & !is.na(wt_kg_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = wt_kg_0m, val_6m = wt_kg_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_wt_kg, val_change)
data_wf_wt_kg[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_wt_kg[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Weight Loss", "Weight Gain or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_wt_kg[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot (absolute change)

mfig2c_wf_wt_kg <- ggplot(data_wf_wt_kg, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Weight Loss"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Weight Gain or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by Weight Change)",
    y = "Weight Change (kg)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Absolute Weight Change Distribution at 6 Months"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig2c_wf_wt_kg)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig2c_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "Weight (kg)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig2c_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig2c_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig2c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig2c_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig2c_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig2c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig2c_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig2c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig2c_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig2c_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig2c_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig2c_wf_wt_kg_tt <-
  mfig2c_wf_wt_kg /
  wrap_elements(mfig2c_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig2c_wf_wt_kg_tt

```

d) Waterfall Plot for Weight Change in % (wk 0-26 - 6 month fu)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m weight change (%)

# Build change table (complete cases for weight only)
data_wf_wt_pct <- data_wide[
  !is.na(wt_kg_0m) & !is.na(wt_kg_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = wt_kg_0m, val_6m = wt_kg_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := 100 * (val_6m - val_0m) / val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_wt_pct, val_change)
data_wf_wt_pct[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_wt_pct[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Weight Loss", "Weight Gain or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_wt_pct[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot (percentage change)

mfig2d_wf_wt_pct <- ggplot(data_wf_wt_pct, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c(
    "Weight Loss"              = "#0B2A5B",
    "Weight Gain or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by Weight Change)",
    y = "Weight Change (%)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Percentage Weight Change Distribution at 6 Months"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig2d_wf_wt_pct)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig2d_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "Weight (kg)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Percent Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig2d_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig2d_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig2d_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig2d_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig2d_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig2d_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig2d_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig2d_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig2d_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig2d_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig2d_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig2d_wf_wt_pct_tt <-
  mfig2d_wf_wt_pct /
  wrap_elements(mfig2d_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig2d_wf_wt_pct_tt

```

e) Combine into one plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine four plots

## Plot needs to be separately mapped in left and right columns due to spacing issues

left_col <- wrap_elements(mfig2a_ribplot_kg) /
            wrap_elements(mfig2b_ribplot_pct)

right_col <- wrap_elements(mfig2c_wf_wt_kg_tt) /
             wrap_elements(mfig2d_wf_wt_pct_tt)

mfig2_wt_change <-
  (left_col | right_col) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &   # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 0.955)      # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )

plot(mfig2_wt_change)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# PNG
ggsave(
  mfig2_wt_change, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig2-wt-change.png",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  mfig2_wt_change, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig2-wt-change.svg",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  mfig2_wt_change, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig2-wt-change.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig3. Weight change trajectory plots (subgroup stratified)
################################################################################ 

a) Age Group Stratified
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Partial F-test for p-value calculation

# Subset to complete case dataset (for explanatory var)
data_wide_agefull <- data_wide[!is.na(age_cat5)]

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_age_unadj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ 1, data = data_wide_agefull)
summary(lm_wt_age_unadj)

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_age_adj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ age_cat5, data = data_wide_agefull)
summary(lm_wt_age_adj)

# Partial F-test (nested model comparison)
# H0: all age_cat5 coefficients = 0  (i.e., age_cat5 adds no explanatory power)
anova(lm_wt_age_unadj, lm_wt_age_adj)

# Extract patial F-test p-value
pf_age <- anova(lm_wt_age_unadj, lm_wt_age_adj)$`Pr(>F)`[2]
pf_age

# Format p-value with custom function
format_pval(pf_age)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean line plot for 5 age groups

# Calculate mean by week + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_kg_age <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_kg_timevar) &
    !is.na(age_cat5) & !is.na(glp1_idx) &
    glp1_idx >= 0 & glp1_idx <= 26,
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_kg_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, age_cat5)
][order(glp1_idx, age_cat5)]

# *Limit rows between 0-26*
data_ribplot_qt_kg_age <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_age[glp1_idx <= 26]

print(data_ribplot_qt_kg_age)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig3a_age_main <- ggplot(
  data_ribplot_qt_kg_age,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean, colour = age_cat5, group = age_cat5)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Mean Weight Change (kg)",  # add separately in full figure
    title = "Stratified by Age Group",
    subtitle = paste0("P for difference between age groups at 6 months: ", format_pval(pf_age)),
    colour = "Age Group"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    #axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal_custom_5)

plot(mfig3a_age_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Risk table

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks)
x_breaks <- seq(
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg_age$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg_age$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = 2
)

# Create risk dataframe
risk_df_age <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_age[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]

# Create risk table
mfig3a_age_risk <- ggplot(
  risk_df_age,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = age_cat5, label = n)
) +
  # numbers
  geom_text(size = 3.5) +

  # row separators
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = seq_along(levels(risk_df_age$age_cat5)) + 0.5,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(risk_df_age$age_cat5))) +

  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0,
      size = 11,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
      )
  ) +
  
  labs(title = "Number of Patients")

plot(mfig3a_age_risk)


#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig3a_age <- mfig3a_age_main / mfig3a_age_risk + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig3a_age)

```

b) IMD Quintiles Stratified
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Partial F-test for p-value calculation

# Subset to complete case dataset (for explanatory var)
data_wide_imdfull <- data_wide[!is.na(area_imd_qt)]

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_imd_unadj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ 1, data = data_wide_imdfull)
summary(lm_wt_imd_unadj)

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_imd_adj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ area_imd_qt, data = data_wide_imdfull)
summary(lm_wt_imd_adj)

# Partial F-test (nested model comparison)
# H0: all age_cat5 coefficients = 0  (i.e., age_cat5 adds no explanatory power)
anova(lm_wt_imd_unadj, lm_wt_imd_adj)

# Extract patial F-test p-value
pf_imd <- anova(lm_wt_imd_unadj, lm_wt_imd_adj)$`Pr(>F)`[2]
pf_imd

# Format p-value with custom function
format_pval(pf_imd)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean line plot for 5 IMD groups

# Calculate means by week + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_kg_timevar) &
    !is.na(area_imd_qt) & !is.na(glp1_idx) &
    glp1_idx >= 0 & glp1_idx <= 26,
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_kg_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, area_imd_qt)
][order(glp1_idx, area_imd_qt)]

# *Limit rows between 0-26*
data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd[glp1_idx <= 26]

print(data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig3b_imd_main <- ggplot(
  data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean, colour = area_imd_qt, group = area_imd_qt)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Mean Weight Change (kg)",  # add separately in full figure
    title = "Stratified by IMD Quintiles",
    subtitle = paste0("P for difference between IMD quintiles at 6 months: ", format_pval(pf_imd)),
    colour = "IMD Quintile"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    #axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal_custom_5)

plot(mfig3b_imd_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Risk table

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks)
x_breaks <- seq(
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = 2
)

# Create risk dataframe
risk_df_imd <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_imd[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]

# Create risk table
mfig3b_imd_risk <- ggplot(
  risk_df_imd,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = area_imd_qt, label = n)
) +
  # numbers
  geom_text(size = 3.5) +

  # row separators
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = seq_along(levels(risk_df_imd$area_imd_qt)) + 0.5,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  scale_y_discrete(
  limits = rev(levels(risk_df_imd$area_imd_qt)),
  labels = c(
    "1 (Most Depr.)",
    "2",
    "3",
    "4",
    "5 (Least Depr.)"
  )
) +

  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0,
      size = 11,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
      )
  ) +
  
  labs(title = "Number of Patients")

plot(mfig3b_imd_risk)


#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig3b_imd <- mfig3b_imd_main / mfig3b_imd_risk + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig3b_imd)

```

c) Ethnicity Stratified
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Partial F-test for p-value calculation

# Subset to complete case dataset (for explanatory var)
data_wide_ethfull <- data_wide[!is.na(eth_bin)]

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_eth_unadj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ 1, data = data_wide_ethfull)
summary(lm_wt_eth_unadj)

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_eth_adj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ eth_bin, data = data_wide_ethfull)
summary(lm_wt_eth_adj)

# Partial F-test (nested model comparison)
# H0: all age_cat5 coefficients = 0  (i.e., age_cat5 adds no explanatory power)
anova(lm_wt_eth_unadj, lm_wt_eth_adj)

# Extract patial F-test p-value
pf_eth <- anova(lm_wt_eth_unadj, lm_wt_eth_adj)$`Pr(>F)`[2]
pf_eth

# Format p-value with custom function
format_pval(pf_eth)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean line plot for 2 ethnicity groups

# Calculate means by week + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_kg_timevar) &
    !is.na(eth_bin) & !is.na(glp1_idx) &
    glp1_idx >= 0 & glp1_idx <= 26,
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_kg_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, eth_bin)
][order(glp1_idx, eth_bin)]

# *Limit rows between 0-26*
data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth[glp1_idx <= 26]

print(data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig3c_eth_main <- ggplot(
  data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean, colour = eth_bin, group = eth_bin)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Mean Weight Change (kg)",  # add separately in full figure
    title = "Stratified by Ethnicity",
    subtitle = paste0("P for difference between ethnicity groups at 6 months: ", format_pval(pf_eth)),
    colour = "Ethnicity"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    #axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal_custom_5)

plot(mfig3c_eth_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Risk table

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks)
x_breaks <- seq(
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = 2
)

# Create risk dataframe
risk_df_eth <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_eth[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]

# Make sure eth_bin is a factor in desired order
risk_df_eth[, eth_bin := factor(eth_bin, levels = rev(unique(eth_bin)))]

# y positions (1..K) and separators at +0.5
levs <- levels(risk_df_eth$eth_bin)
sep_df <- data.frame(y = seq_along(levs) + 0.5)

# Create risk table
mfig3c_eth_risk <- ggplot(
  risk_df_eth,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = eth_bin, label = n)
) +
  
  # numbers
  geom_text(size = 3.5) +
  
  # row separators
  geom_hline(
    data = sep_df,
    aes(yintercept = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
    plot.margin  = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 5))
  ) +
  labs(title = "Number of Patients")

plot(mfig3c_eth_risk)


#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig3c_eth <- mfig3c_eth_main / mfig3c_eth_risk + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig3c_eth)

```

d) Starting Weight Group Stratified
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Partial F-test for p-value calculation

# Subset to complete case dataset (for explanatory var)
data_wide_wtgrpfull <- data_wide[!is.na(wt_kg_0m_cat)]

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_wtgrp_unadj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ 1, data = data_wide_wtgrpfull)
summary(lm_wt_wtgrp_unadj)

# Unadjusted model
lm_wt_wtgrp_adj <- lm(wt_change_kg_06m ~ wt_kg_0m_cat, data = data_wide_wtgrpfull)
summary(lm_wt_wtgrp_adj)

# Partial F-test (nested model comparison)
# H0: all age_cat5 coefficients = 0  (i.e., age_cat5 adds no explanatory power)
anova(lm_wt_wtgrp_unadj, lm_wt_wtgrp_adj)

# Extract patial F-test p-value
pf_wtgrp <- anova(lm_wt_wtgrp_unadj, lm_wt_wtgrp_adj)$`Pr(>F)`[2]
pf_wtgrp

# Format p-value with custom function
format_pval(pf_wtgrp)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean line plot for 5 baseline weight groups

# Calculate mean by week + N patients contributing
data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp <- data_ribplot[
  !is.na(wt_change_kg_timevar) & is.finite(wt_change_kg_timevar) &
    !is.na(wt_kg_0m_cat) & !is.na(glp1_idx) &
    glp1_idx >= 0 & glp1_idx <= 26,
  .(
    mean = mean(wt_change_kg_timevar, na.rm = TRUE),
    n   = uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, wt_kg_0m_cat)
][order(glp1_idx, wt_kg_0m_cat)]

# *Limit rows between 0-26*
data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp[glp1_idx <= 26]

print(data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp)

#-------------------------------#
# Main plot (graded quantile ribbons)

# Create main plot
mfig3d_wtgrp_main <- ggplot(
  data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean, colour = wt_kg_0m_cat, group = wt_kg_0m_cat)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(
    x = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    y = "Mean Weight Change (kg)",  # add separately in full figure
    title = "Stratified by Baseline Weight",
    subtitle = paste0("P for difference between baseline weight groups at 6 months: ", format_pval(pf_wtgrp)),
    colour = "Baseline Weight"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    #axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal_custom_5)

plot(mfig3d_wtgrp_main)

#-------------------------------#
# Risk table

# Choose which weeks to show in the risk table (match x breaks)
x_breaks <- seq(
  floor(min(data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  ceiling(max(data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp$glp1_idx, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = 2
)

# Create risk dataframe
risk_df_wtgrp <- data_ribplot_qt_kg_wtgrp[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]

# Create risk table
mfig3d_wtgrp_risk <- ggplot(
  risk_df_wtgrp,
  aes(x = glp1_idx, y = wt_kg_0m_cat, label = n)
) +
  # numbers
  geom_text(size = 3.5) +

  # row separators
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = seq_along(levels(risk_df_wtgrp$wt_kg_0m_cat)) + 0.5,
    colour = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(risk_df_wtgrp$wt_kg_0m_cat))) +

  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0,
      size = 11,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
      )
  ) +
  
  labs(title = "Number of Patients")

plot(mfig3d_wtgrp_risk)


#-------------------------------#
# Full plot

mfig3d_wtgrp <- mfig3d_wtgrp_main / mfig3d_wtgrp_risk + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1)) # set ratio of top vs bottom plot

plot(mfig3d_wtgrp)

```

e) Combine into one plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine three plots

# Build main plot
## In following order: tl wtgrp, tr age, bl imd, br eth
mfig3_stratplot <-
  (wrap_elements(mfig3d_wtgrp) | wrap_elements(mfig3a_age)) /
  plot_spacer() /
  (wrap_elements(mfig3b_imd)   | wrap_elements(mfig3c_eth)) +
  plot_layout(
    heights = c(1, 0.05, 1) # middle number for middle gap
  ) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16)
    )
  )  +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &   # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 0.955)      # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )

# Add y axis label (removed now that alphabetic annotations are used in nature formatting)
#mfig3_stratplot <- ggdraw(mfig3_stratplot_core) +
#  draw_label(
#    "Mean Weight Change (kg)",
#    angle = 90,
#    x = 0.01, y = 0.6, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
#    size = 12
#  )

plot(mfig3_stratplot)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# PNG
ggsave(
  mfig3_stratplot, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig3-stratplot.png",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  mfig3_stratplot, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig3-stratplot.svg",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  mfig3_stratplot, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig3-stratplot.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig4a. HbA1c change figure (diabetic evolution)
################################################################################ 

a) Waterfall Plot for HbA1c Change (Absolute)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m HbA1c change (Absolute)

# Build change table (complete cases for HbA1c only)
data_wf_hba1c_abs <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m, val_6m = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_hba1c_abs, val_change)
data_wf_hba1c_abs[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_hba1c_abs[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_hba1c_abs[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot (absolute change)

mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs <- ggplot(data_wf_hba1c_abs, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by HbA1c Change)",
    y = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    title = "Change in HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    fill = NULL,
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4a_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HbA1c (mmol/mol)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4a_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4a_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4a_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4a_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4a_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs_tt <-
  mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs /
  wrap_elements(mfig4a_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs_tt

```

b) Waterfall Plot for HbA1c Change (Percentage)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m HbA1c change (Percentage)

# Build change table (complete cases for HbA1c only)
data_wf_hba1c_pct <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m, val_6m = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := 100 * (val_6m - val_0m) / val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_hba1c_pct, val_change)
data_wf_hba1c_pct[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_hba1c_pct[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_hba1c_pct[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot (percentage change)

mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct <- ggplot(data_wf_hba1c_pct, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by HbA1c Change)",
    y = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    title = "Change in HbA1c (%)",
    fill = NULL,
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4b_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HbA1c (mmol/mol)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Percent Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4b_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4b_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4b_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4b_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4b_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct_tt <-
  mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct /
  wrap_elements(mfig4b_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct_tt

```

c) Box and whisker plot for HbA1c Change (Diabetic)
```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# Data Prep (Convert to long)

box_long <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & !is.na(hba1c_cat_6m) &
    hba1c_cat_0m == "Diabetic",
  .(
    patid,
    `Baseline` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m,
    `6 months` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m
  )
][
  , melt(.SD,
         id.vars       = "patid",
         variable.name = "time",
         value.name    = "hba1c")
]

#-----------------------------#
# Automated title

# % among those with complete-case HbA1c at 0m+6m:
title_box_diab <- make_box_title(
   dt = data_wide,
   subgroup_filter = quote(!is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & hba1c_cat_0m == "Diabetic" &
                             !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   title_prefix = "Diabetic HbA1c at Baseline",
   denom_filter = quote(!is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   digits_pct = 1
 )

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4c_box_diab <- ggplot(
  box_long,
  aes(x = time, y = hba1c, fill = time)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.65,
    outlier.alpha = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    aes(group = 1),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Baseline" = "#0B2A5B",
      "6 months" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Follow-up Period",
    y = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = title_box_diab
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "plain"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )

plot(mfig4c_box_diab)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4c_tt_diab <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HbA1c (mmol/mol)" &
    subgroup == "Basline Diabetic (≥48 mmol/mol)" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4c_tt_diab <- tableGrob(
  mfig4c_tt_diab,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4c_tt_diab <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4c_tt_diab,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4c_tt_diab)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4c_tt_diab <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4c_tt_diab,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4c_tt_diab <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4c_tt_diab,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4c_tt_diab)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4c_tt_diab$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4c_box_diab_tt <-
  mfig4c_box_diab /
  wrap_elements(mfig4c_tt_diab) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4c_box_diab_tt

```

d) Box and whisker plot for HbA1c Change (Pre-Diabetic)
```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# Data Prep (Convert to long)

box_long <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & !is.na(hba1c_cat_6m) &
  hba1c_cat_0m == "Pre-Diabetic",
  .(
    patid,
    `Baseline` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m,
    `6 months` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m
  )
][
  , melt(.SD,
         id.vars       = "patid",
         variable.name = "time",
         value.name    = "hba1c")
]

#-----------------------------#
# Automated title

# % among those with complete-case HbA1c at 0m+6m:
title_box_pdiab <- make_box_title(
   dt = data_wide,
   subgroup_filter = quote(!is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & hba1c_cat_0m == "Pre-Diabetic" &
                             !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   title_prefix = "Pre-Diabetic HbA1c at Baseline",
   denom_filter = quote(!is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   digits_pct = 1
 )

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4d_box_pdiab <- ggplot(
  box_long,
  aes(x = time, y = hba1c, fill = time)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.65,
    outlier.alpha = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    aes(group = 1),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Baseline" = "#0B2A5B",
      "6 months" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Follow-up Period",
    y = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = title_box_pdiab
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "plain"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )

plot(mfig4d_box_pdiab)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4d_tt_pdiab <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HbA1c (mmol/mol)" &
    subgroup == "Baseline Pre-diabetic (42–47 mmol/mol)" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4d_tt_pdiab <- tableGrob(
  mfig4d_tt_pdiab,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4d_tt_pdiab <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4d_tt_pdiab,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4d_tt_pdiab)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4d_tt_pdiab <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4d_tt_pdiab,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4d_tt_pdiab <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4d_tt_pdiab,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4d_tt_pdiab)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4d_tt_pdiab$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4d_box_pdiab_tt <-
  mfig4d_box_pdiab /
  wrap_elements(mfig4d_tt_pdiab) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4d_box_pdiab_tt

```

e) Box and whisker plot for HbA1c Change (Normal)
```{r}
#-----------------------------#
# Data Prep (Convert to long)

box_long <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & !is.na(hba1c_cat_6m) &
  hba1c_cat_0m == "Normal",
  .(
    patid,
    `Baseline` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m,
    `6 months` = blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m
  )
][
  , melt(.SD,
         id.vars       = "patid",
         variable.name = "time",
         value.name    = "hba1c")
]

#-----------------------------#
# Automated title

# % among those with complete-case HbA1c at 0m+6m:
title_box_norm <- make_box_title(
   dt = data_wide,
   subgroup_filter = quote(!is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & hba1c_cat_0m == "Normal" &
                             !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   title_prefix = "Normal HbA1c at Baseline",
   denom_filter = quote(!is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m) & !is.na(blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m)),
   digits_pct = 1
 )

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4e_box_norm <- ggplot(
  box_long,
  aes(x = time, y = hba1c, fill = time)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.65,
    outlier.alpha = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "line",
    aes(group = 1),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Baseline" = "#0B2A5B",
      "6 months" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Follow-up Period",
    y = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    fill = NULL,
    title = title_box_norm
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "plain"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )

plot(mfig4e_box_norm)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4e_tt_norm <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HbA1c (mmol/mol)" &
    subgroup == "Baseline Normal (<42 mmol/mol)" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4e_tt_norm <- tableGrob(
  mfig4e_tt_norm,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4e_tt_norm <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4e_tt_norm,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4e_tt_norm)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4e_tt_norm <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4e_tt_norm,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4e_tt_norm <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4e_tt_norm,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4e_tt_norm)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4e_tt_norm$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4e_box_norm_tt <-
  mfig4e_box_norm /
  wrap_elements(mfig4e_tt_norm) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4e_box_norm_tt

```

f) Combine into one plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine five plots

mfig4_hba1c <- 
  (mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs_tt | mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct_tt) /
  plot_spacer() /
  (mfig4c_box_diab_tt | mfig4d_box_pdiab_tt | mfig4e_box_norm_tt) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(
      #title = "Change in HbA1c Levels from Baseline to 6 Month Follow-up",
```
