```{r}
      plot.title = element_text(
        size = 16,
        margin = margin(b = 14)
      )
    )
  ) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.05, 1))

plot(mfig4_hba1c)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

ggsave(
  mfig4_hba1c, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig4extra-hba1c.png",
  width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig4b. Biochemistry lipid change waterfall plots
################################################################################ 

a) Waterfall Plot for total cholesterol change (Absolute)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m TC change (Absolute)

# Build change table (complete cases for TC only)
data_wf_tc_abs <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_chol_mmoll_0m) & !is.na(blood_chol_mmoll_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_chol_mmoll_0m, val_6m = blood_chol_mmoll_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_tc_abs, val_change)
data_wf_tc_abs[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_tc_abs[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_tc_abs[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4a_wf_tc_abs <- ggplot(data_wf_tc_abs, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by Total Cholesterol Change)",
    y = "Change in Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    title = "Change in Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    fill = NULL,
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig4a_wf_tc_abs)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4a_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4a_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4a_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4a_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4a_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4a_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4a_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4a_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4a_wf_tc_abs_tt <-
  mfig4a_wf_tc_abs /
  wrap_elements(mfig4a_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4a_wf_tc_abs_tt

```

b) Waterfall Plot for HDL change (Absolute)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m HDL change (Absolute)

# Build change table (complete cases for HDL only)
data_wf_hdl_abs <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_hdl_mmoll_0m) & !is.na(blood_hdl_mmoll_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_hdl_mmoll_0m, val_6m = blood_hdl_mmoll_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_hdl_abs, val_change)
data_wf_hdl_abs[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_hdl_abs[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_hdl_abs[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs <- ggplot(data_wf_hdl_abs, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by HDL Change)",
    y = "Change in HDL (mmol/L)",
    title = "Change in HDL (mmol/L)",
    fill = NULL,
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4b_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "HDL (mmol/L)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4b_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4b_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4b_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4b_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4b_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4b_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4b_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs_tt <-
  mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs /
  wrap_elements(mfig4b_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs_tt

```

c) Waterfall Plot for LDL change (Absolute)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m LDL change (Absolute)

# Build change table (complete cases for LDL only)
data_wf_ldl_abs <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_ldl_mmoll_0m) & !is.na(blood_ldl_mmoll_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_ldl_mmoll_0m, val_6m = blood_ldl_mmoll_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_ldl_abs, val_change)
data_wf_ldl_abs[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_ldl_abs[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +
wf_boundary_x <- data_wf_ldl_abs[val_change < 0, max(order_id, na.rm = TRUE)]
wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs <- ggplot(data_wf_ldl_abs, aes(x = order_id, y = val_change, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),
        rng[2]
      )
    },
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Patients (Ordered by LDL Change)",
    y = "Change in LDL (mmol/L)",
    title = "Change in LDL (mmol/L)",
    fill = NULL,
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

plot(mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs)

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4c_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "LDL (mmol/L)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4c_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4c_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
mfig4c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4c_tt,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4c_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4c_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4c_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4c_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4c_tt)
)

# Change grob table width
mfig4c_tt$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs_tt <-
  mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs /
  wrap_elements(mfig4c_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs_tt

```

d) Waterfall Plot for Triglycerides change (Absolute)
```{r}
#----------------------------------------#
# Waterfall plot: 6m-0m Triglycerides change ()

# Build change table (complete cases for Triglycerides only)
data_wf_tg_abs <- data_wide[
  !is.na(blood_tg_mmoll_0m) & !is.na(blood_tg_mmoll_6m),
  .(patid, val_0m = blood_tg_mmoll_0m, val_6m = blood_tg_mmoll_6m)
][
  is.finite(val_0m) & is.finite(val_6m)
][
  , val_change := val_6m - val_0m
][
  is.finite(val_change)
]

# Order patients from most decrease to most increase
setorder(data_wf_tg_abs, val_change)
data_wf_tg_abs[, order_id := seq_len(.N)]
data_wf_tg_abs[, direction := fifelse(val_change < 0, "Decrease", "Increase or No Change")]

# Boundary where change switches from - to +

wf_boundary_x <- if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x)) wf_boundary_x + 0.5 else NA_real_

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

  geom_col(width = 1, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  {if (is.finite(wf_boundary_x))
    geom_vline(xintercept = wf_boundary_x, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, colour = "black")} +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Decrease"              = "#0B2A5B",
      "Increase or No Change" = "#04BFAF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = function(x) {

      c(
        rng[1],
        round(rng[2] * 0.25),
        round(rng[2] * 0.50),
        round(rng[2] * 0.75),

  ) +

    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
mfig4d_tt <- rfig2_ttest_results[
  measure == "Triglycerides (mmol/L)" &
    subgroup == "Full Sample" & 
    scale == "Absolute Change",
  .(
    N = n,
    `Mean Change` = sprintf("%.2f", mean_change),
    `95% CI` = paste0(
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_low),
      " to ",
      sprintf("%.2f", ci_high)
    ),
    `P Value` = format_pval(p_value)
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
mfig4d_tt <- tableGrob(
  mfig4d_tt,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),

    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),

)

# Add header line to grob table

  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4d_tt)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Within-Person Mean Change Summary",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
mfig4d_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  mfig4d_tt,
  heights = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
mfig4d_tt <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  mfig4d_tt,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(mfig4d_tt)
)

# Change grob table width

  c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.25),  # tweak as needed
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

mfig4d_wf_tg_abs_tt <-
  mfig4d_wf_tg_abs /
  wrap_elements(mfig4d_tt) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))

mfig4d_wf_tg_abs_tt

```

e) Combine into one plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine 4 plots

mfig4_lipid <-
  (mfig4a_wf_tc_abs_tt | mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs_tt) /
  plot_spacer() /
  (mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs_tt | mfig4d_wf_tg_abs_tt) +
  plot_layout(
    heights = c(1, 0.05, 1) # middle number for middle gap
  ) +
  plot_annotation(
    #title = "Change in Lipid Profiles from Baseline to 6 Month Follow-up",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, margin = margin(b = 14)))
  )

plot(mfig4_lipid)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

ggsave(
  mfig4_lipid, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig4bextra-lipid.png",
  width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

```

f) HDL change subgroup table (for checking only)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create HDL subgroup table for checking

# Calculate HDL change
data_wide[, hdl_change_06m := blood_hdl_mmoll_6m - blood_hdl_mmoll_0m]

# 1) Stratified by age_cat5
hdl_by_age <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hdl_change_06m),
  .(
    n          = .N,
    mean_hdl   = mean(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_hdl     = sd(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_hdl = median(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_l      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_u      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = age_cat5
][order(age_cat5)]

# 2) Stratified by wt_kg_0m_cat
hdl_by_wt <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hdl_change_06m),
  .(
    n          = .N,
    mean_hdl   = mean(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_hdl     = sd(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_hdl = median(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_l      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_u      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = wt_kg_0m_cat
][order(wt_kg_0m_cat)]

# 3) Stratified by area_imd_qt
hdl_by_imd <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hdl_change_06m),
  .(
    n          = .N,
    mean_hdl   = mean(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_hdl     = sd(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_hdl = median(hdl_change_06m, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_l      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_u      = quantile(hdl_change_06m, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = area_imd_qt
][order(area_imd_qt)]

# Assign stratification var name
hdl_by_age[, strat_var := "Age"]
hdl_by_wt[,  strat_var := "Baseline weight"]
hdl_by_imd[, strat_var := "IMD"]

# Harmonise column names
setnames(hdl_by_age, "age_cat5",     "stratum")
setnames(hdl_by_wt,  "wt_kg_0m_cat",  "stratum")
setnames(hdl_by_imd, "area_imd_qt",   "stratum")

# Row bind
mfig4_hdl_subgrp_check <- rbindlist(
  list(hdl_by_age, hdl_by_wt, hdl_by_imd),
  use.names = TRUE,
  fill = TRUE
)

# Move strat_var to the left
setcolorder(
  mfig4_hdl_subgrp_check,
  c("strat_var", "stratum",
    setdiff(names(mfig4_hdl_subgrp_check), c("strat_var", "stratum")))
)

mfig4_hdl_subgrp_check


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table

fwrite(
  mfig4_hdl_subgrp_check,
  file = "Final Outputs/mfig4extra-tab-hdl-subgrp-check.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig4c. HbA1c and Lipids Combined
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine plots

#-----------------------------#
# Create helper function for wrapping subplots

# Treat any pre-combined patchwork (plot/table) as a single panel

## Original function
#W <- function(x) wrap_elements(full = x)

## Added padding
W <- function(x,
              pad_t = 3, pad_r = 3, pad_b = 3, pad_l = 3,
              pad_unit = "mm") {

  # Make x a single patchwork element
  p <- wrap_elements(full = x)

  # Convert to grid units
  top    <- unit(pad_t, pad_unit)
  right  <- unit(pad_r, pad_unit)
  bottom <- unit(pad_b, pad_unit)
  left   <- unit(pad_l, pad_unit)

  # Create a blank "canvas" and inset the plot inside it, leaving padding
  plot_spacer() +
    inset_element(
      p,
      left   = left,
      bottom = bottom,
      right  = unit(1, "npc") - right,
      top    = unit(1, "npc") - top
    )
}

#-----------------------------#
# Combine subplots into final plot

# Inner rows (wrapped)
tc        <- W(mfig4a_wf_tc_abs_tt)
hdl       <- W(mfig4b_wf_hdl_abs_tt)
ldl       <- W(mfig4c_wf_ldl_abs_tt)
tg        <- W(mfig4d_wf_tg_abs_tt)
hba1c_abs <- W(mfig4a_wf_hba1c_abs_tt)
hba1c_pct <- W(mfig4b_wf_hba1c_pct_tt)

# 3 boxplots (each wrapped)
box_row <- W(mfig4c_box_diab_tt) | W(mfig4d_box_pdiab_tt) | W(mfig4e_box_norm_tt)

top_2col <- (tc | hdl) / (ldl | tg) / (hba1c_abs | hba1c_pct)

mfig4_combined <-
  (top_2col / box_row) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 0.95)
  )

plot(mfig4_combined)

# PNG
ggsave(
  mfig4_combined,
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig4-hba1c-lipids.png",
  width = 18, height = 20, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  mfig4_combined,
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig4-hba1c-lipids.svg",
  width = 18, height = 20, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  mfig4_combined,
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig4-hba1c-lipids.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 18, height = 20, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# MFig5. Dosing schedule (mounjaro only) with side effect and attrition counts
################################################################################ 

Data Prep for dose plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create dosing schedule plot dataset

#-----------------------------#
# Dataset creation

# Check dosing categories
table(data$mounjaro_mg, useNA = "always")
table(data$wegovy_mg, useNA = "always")

# Keep relevant columns
data_dose <- data[, .(
  patid, glp1_idx, attr_status,
  glp1_drug_locf, mounjaro_mg_locf_num, wegovy_mg_locf_num
)]

# Unify prescription columns into plot dataset
rx_long <- rbindlist(list(

  data_dose[!is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
       .(
         patid,
         glp1_idx,
         glp1_drug_locf = "Mounjaro",
         dose = mounjaro_mg_locf_num
       )],

  data_dose[!is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
       .(
         patid,
         glp1_idx,
         glp1_drug_locf = "Wegovy",
         dose = wegovy_mg_locf_num
       )]

), use.names = TRUE)

# Ensure correct temporal ordering
setorder(rx_long, patid, glp1_idx)

#-----------------------------#
# Collapse weekly dose to monthly dose

# Set month weeks (13 wks = 3 months, 26 weks = 6 months)
month_weeks <- c(0, 4, 8, 13, 17, 21, 26)

# Create monthly snapshots for mounjaro
mounjaro_monthly <- data_dose[
  glp1_idx %in% month_weeks & !is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
  .(patid, month = glp1_idx, drug = "Mounjaro", dose_mg = mounjaro_mg_locf_num)
]

# Create monthly snapshots for wegovy
wegovy_monthly <- data_dose[
  glp1_idx %in% month_weeks & !is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
  .(patid, month = glp1_idx, drug = "Wegovy", dose_mg = wegovy_mg_locf_num)
]

# Combine into monthly dose dataset
dose_monthly <- rbindlist(list(mounjaro_monthly, wegovy_monthly), use.names = TRUE)
```

[Main] Plot stacked bar chart for dose (mounjaro)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create dosing schedule stacked bar chart

#-----------------------------#
# Create % distribution dataset

# % of patients at each dose per month by mounjaro and wegovy
dose_monthly_pct <- dose_monthly[
  ,
  .N,
  by = .(drug, month, dose_mg)
][
  ,
  pct := 100 * N / sum(N),
  by = .(drug, month)
]

# Relabel months
month_labs <- c(
  "0"  = "Baseline",
  "4"  = "1 Month",
  "8"  = "2 Months",
  "13" = "3 Months",
  "17" = "4 Months",
  "21" = "5 Months",
  "26" = "6 Months"
)
dose_monthly_pct[, month_lab := factor(month, levels = month_weeks, labels = month_labs)]

# Make dose a factor so stacking order is stable (low to high)
dose_monthly_pct[, dose_mg := factor(dose_mg, levels = sort(unique(dose_mg)))]

# Create a combined fill label
dose_monthly_pct[, dose_drug := paste0(as.character(dose_mg), " mg")]

#-----------------------------#
# Split tables by drug

# Only keep mounjaro dosing information
dose_mounjaro <- dose_monthly_pct[drug == "Mounjaro"]

# Ensure stable stacking order within each drug
dose_mounjaro[, dose_mg := factor(dose_mg, levels = sort(unique(dose_mg)))]

# As dose_drug already encodes dose, dose_mg can be used directly for fill
dose_mounjaro[, dose_lab := paste0(as.character(dose_mg), " mg")]

dose_mounjaro[, dose_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = unique(dose_lab[order(as.numeric(as.character(dose_mg)))]))]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Plot stacked bar chart (mounjaro)

p_mounjaro <- ggplot(
  dose_mounjaro,
  aes(x = month_lab, y = pct, fill = dose_lab)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Tirzepatide Dose from Baseline to 6 Month Follow-up",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Patients (%)",
    fill = "Dose"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.06))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe2_6) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p_mounjaro


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create patient count table (mounjaro)

# Choose the month order from existing factor
month_levels <- levels(dose_mounjaro$month_lab)

# Aggregate counts by month x dose (you already have N, but sum defensively)
tab_ptcount_mounjaro <- dose_mounjaro[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(month_lab, dose_lab)
]

# Ensure stable ordering
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels)]
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, dose_lab  := factor(dose_lab,  levels = levels(dose_mounjaro$dose_lab))]

# add a Total N row
tab_total_mounjaro <- dose_mounjaro[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = month_lab
][
  , dose_lab := "Total"
]

tab_ptcount_mounjaro <- rbind(tab_total_mounjaro, tab_ptcount_mounjaro, fill = TRUE)

# Label formatting
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, label := fifelse(is.na(N), "", formatC(N, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","))]

# For plotting as a grid of text, we want rows = dose, cols = month
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, row_lab := as.character(dose_lab)]
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, row_lab := factor(row_lab, levels = c("Total", levels(dose_mounjaro$dose_lab)))]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean dose and range table (mounjaro)

# Add month_lab onto the patient-level monthly dataset
dose_monthly[, month_lab := factor(month, levels = month_weeks, labels = month_labs)]

# Choose same month ordering as the plots
month_levels_m <- levels(dose_mounjaro$month_lab)

#-----------------------------#
# Mounjaro: mean + range dose per month

tab_meandose_mounjaro <- dose_monthly[drug == "Mounjaro",
  .(
    mean_dose = mean(dose_mg, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_dose  = min(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE),
    max_dose  = max(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = month_lab
]

tab_meandose_mounjaro[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels_m)]

# Create two display rows
tab_meandose_mounjaro_long <- rbindlist(list(

  tab_meandose_mounjaro[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Mean dose (mg)",
      label = ifelse(is.na(mean_dose), "", sprintf("%.1f", mean_dose))
    )
  ],

  tab_meandose_mounjaro[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Range (mg)",
      label = ifelse(
        is.na(min_dose) | is.na(max_dose),
        "",
        sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", min_dose, max_dose)
      )
    )
  ]

))

tab_meandose_mounjaro_long[
  ,
  row_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = c("Mean dose (mg)", "Range (mg)"))
]

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine plots

# Assign shared axis labels as grobs
x_lab <- wrap_elements(
  full = textGrob(
    "Months Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    gp = gpar(fontsize = 11)
  )
)

# Create mean dose table block (mounjaro)
p_mounjaro_meandose <- make_count_table(
  tab_meandose_mounjaro_long,
  title = "Dose Summary by Month"
) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create patient count table block (mounjaro)
p_mounjaro_counts <- make_count_table(
  tab_ptcount_mounjaro,
  title = "Patient Counts by Dose"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create internal padding within blocks (three layer limitation)
pad_tbl <- theme(
  plot.margin = margin(t = 6, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)
)

p_mounjaro_meandose <- p_mounjaro_meandose + pad_tbl
p_mounjaro_counts   <- p_mounjaro_counts   + pad_tbl

# Assign blocks
mfig5_dose_mounjaro <- p_mounjaro / p_mounjaro_meandose / p_mounjaro_counts +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.10, 0.25))


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Print plot (for reference)

ggsave(
  mfig5_dose_mounjaro, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5extra-dose-mounjaro.png",
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```

Data prep for cumulative attrition proportion by side effect count (time varying)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Attrition proportion within subgroup by timepoint (glp1_idx)

data.table::setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# Use valid follow-up only
dt_fu <- data[fu_valid == TRUE]

# Define subgroup at each timepoint from cumulative side effect count
dt_fu[
  ,
  sdeff_cum_band := fcase(
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 0L, "0",
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 1L, "1",
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 2L, "2",
    sdeff_total_cum_n >= 3L, "3+",
    default = NA_character_
  )
]

dt_fu[, sdeff_cum_band := factor(sdeff_cum_band, levels = c("0","1","2","3+"))]

# Build long attrition table: at each week, within each subgroup band
tab_attr_sdeff <- dt_fu[
  !is.na(sdeff_cum_band),
  .(
    n_total        = uniqueN(patid),
    n_attrited     = uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 1L]),
    n_not_attrited = uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 0L]),
    pct_attrited   = 100 * uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 1L]) / uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, sdeff_cum_band)
][order(glp1_idx, sdeff_cum_band)]

# Create numerator/denominator string
tab_attr_sdeff[, frac := sprintf("%d/%d", n_attrited, n_total)]
tab_attr_sdeff[, frac_pct := sprintf("%d/%d\n%.1f%%", n_attrited, n_total, pct_attrited)]

tab_attr_sdeff[]

```

[Attrition Care Package] Plot attrition stratified by side effect care package use 
(Restrict to only patients with side effects)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Attrition at 6m, comparing:

## Any side effects up to that point  vs  No side effects up to that point
## (regardless of counts)
## Only including indivdiuals with any side effect up to that point

tab_attr_carepack_given_sdeff_6m <- data[
  glp1_idx == 26L & sdeff_total_cum_n > 0,
  .(
    patid,
    glp1_idx,
    carepack_lab = factor(
      fifelse(is.na(sdeff_carepack_ever) | sdeff_carepack_ever == 0L,
              "No Use", "Use"),
      levels = c("No Use", "Use")
    ),
    attr_by_time = as.integer(attr_status == 1L)
  )
][
  ,
  .(
    n_total      = .N,
    n_attrited   = sum(attr_by_time, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_attrited = 100 * sum(attr_by_time, na.rm = TRUE) / .N
  ),
  by = carepack_lab
][
  ,
  `:=`(
    frac  = sprintf("%d/%d (%.1f%%)", n_attrited, n_total, pct_attrited),
    lab_y = pct_attrited + 0.4
  )
]

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

# Add p-value from RFig4 section
pval_plot <- ifelse(
  ztest_sdeff_carepack$p.value < 0.001,
  "P for difference in proportions < 0.001",
  paste0("P for difference in proportions = ", sprintf("%.3f", ztest_sdeff_carepack$p.value))
)

mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar <-
  ggplot(tab_attr_carepack_given_sdeff_6m,
         aes(x = carepack_lab, y = pct_attrited, fill = carepack_lab)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = frac, y = lab_y), size = 3.5, vjust = 0) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste0(
      "Treatment Discontinuation at 6 Months by Care Package Use\n",
      "(Among Patients with Side Effects, ",
      pval_plot, ")"
    ),
    x = "Care Package Use",
    y = "Treatment Discontinuation (%)"
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.95),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Print plot (for reference)

ggsave(
  mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar,
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5extra-sdeff-carepack-bar.png",
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```

[Counts and Severity Proportions] Side effect counts and severity
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Fig. Side effect distribution in total sample:

# Left = side effect count (sdeff_total_n)
# Right = max severity (none/mild/moderate/severe)

# Total N (unique patients)
N_total <- data_wide[, uniqueN(patid)]

#-----------------------------#
# Side effect count (0,1,2,3+)

data_wide[
  ,
  sdeff_total_cat := fcase(
    is.na(sdeff_total_n) | sdeff_total_n == 0L, "0",
    sdeff_total_n == 1L, "1",
    sdeff_total_n == 2L, "2",
    sdeff_total_n >= 3L, "3+",
    default = NA_character_
  )
]

tab_count <- data_wide[
  !is.na(sdeff_total_cat),
  .(n_cat = uniqueN(patid)),
  by = .(cat = sdeff_total_cat)
][
  ,
  block := "Count"
][
  ,
  .(block, cat, n_cat)
]

#-----------------------------#
# Max Severity (None/Mild/Moderate/Severe)

tab_sev <- data_wide[
  ,
  .(n_cat = uniqueN(patid)),
  by = .(cat = most_severe_sdeff)
][
  ,
  `:=`(
    block = "Severity",
    cat = factor(
      cat,
      levels = c("None","Mild","Moderate","Severe")
    )
  )
][
  ,
  .(block, cat, n_cat)
]

#-----------------------------#
# Combine + proportions + labels

# Row bind list
tab_plot <- rbindlist(list(tab_count, tab_sev), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Create derived totals and proportions for plotting
tab_plot[, n_total := N_total]
tab_plot[, pct := 100 * n_cat / n_total]
tab_plot[, frac_pct := sprintf("%d/%d\n(%.1f%%)", n_cat, n_total, pct)]

# Ordered categories for stable x positions
count_levels <- c("0","1","2","3+")
sev_levels   <- c("None","Mild","Moderate","Severe")

tab_plot[
  block == "Count",
  cat := factor(as.character(cat), levels = count_levels)
]
tab_plot[
  block == "Severity",
  cat := factor(as.character(cat), levels = sev_levels)
]

#-----------------------------#
# Spacing (robust): explicit positions per block

within_step <- 1.0
between_gap <- 0.5

count_levels <- c("0","1","2","3+")
sev_levels   <- c("None","Mild","Moderate","Severe")

# Make sure cat is character before mapping
tab_plot[, cat_chr := as.character(cat)]

# Block index (left vs right)
tab_plot[, block_idx := fifelse(block == "Count", 1L, 2L)]

# Explicit cat positions (0..3) by block
pos_count <- setNames(0:3, count_levels)
pos_sev   <- setNames(0:3, sev_levels)

tab_plot[
  ,
  cat_pos := fifelse(
    block == "Count",
    unname(pos_count[cat_chr]),
    unname(pos_sev[cat_chr])
  )
]

# Final x positions
tab_plot[
  ,
  x_pos := (block_idx - 1) * (within_step * 4 + between_gap) + cat_pos * within_step
]

# Tick labels
x_ticks <- unique(
  tab_plot[
    order(block_idx, cat_pos),
    .(x_pos, x_lab = paste0(cat_chr))
  ]
)

# Labels above bars
tab_plot[, lab_y := pct + 1.2]
y_max <- min(100, ceiling(max(tab_plot$pct, na.rm = TRUE) / 5) * 5 + 8)

#-----------------------------#
# Plot

mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar <-
  ggplot(tab_plot, aes(x = x_pos, y = pct, fill = block)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = frac_pct, y = lab_y),
    size = 3,
    vjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = x_ticks$x_pos,
    labels = x_ticks$x_lab,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.06, 0.06))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x),
    limits = c(0, y_max),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Side Effect Burden by Total Counts & Maximum Severity",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of Total Sample (%)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.95),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Print plot (for reference)

ggsave(
  mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar,
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5extra-sdeff-dist-bar.png",
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```

d) Combine all plots
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assemble with an extra bottom row + extra left column

# Add tags manually to each plot
p_mounjaro_tag <- p_mounjaro +
  labs(tag = "a") +
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.995)   # position for "a" ONLY
  )

mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar_tag <- mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar +
  labs(tag = "b") +
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.99)
  )

mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar_tag <- mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar +
  labs(tag = "c") +
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.99)
  )

# Rebuild left stack with tagged top plot
mfig5_dose_mounjaro_tag <- p_mounjaro_tag / p_mounjaro_meandose / p_mounjaro_counts +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.10, 0.25))

# Combined side effect distribution + care package use on right
## Plot spacers added to top and bottom so that spacing matches dosage plot
mfig5_right <- (
    plot_spacer() /
    mfig5_sdeff_dist_bar_tag /
    mfig5_attr_sdeff_carepack_bar_tag /
    plot_spacer()
  ) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(0.001, 1, 1, 0.001)) 

# Combine
mfig5_dose_attr <- (wrap_elements(mfig5_dose_mounjaro_tag) | mfig5_right) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.35, 1))  # left vs right widths

mfig5_dose_attr


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# PNG
ggsave(
  mfig5_dose_attr, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5-dose-attr.png",
  width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  mfig5_dose_attr, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5-dose-attr.svg",
  width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  mfig5_dose_attr, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/mfig5-dose-attr.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# STab1. Patient characteristics table (by age)
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create baseline characteristics table

#-----------------------------#
# Assign age (categorical) as strata

strata <- c(
  list(Total = data_wide),
  split(data_wide, data_wide$age_cat5, drop = TRUE)
)

#-----------------------------#
# Assign labels to variables

labels <- list(
  variables = list(
    # Sex*Age
    sex                     = "Sex",

    # Physical Measures
    wt_kg_0m_cat            = "Weight (kg)",
    bmi_0m_cat              = "BMI (kg/m\u00B2)",

    # Lipids
    blood_chol_mmoll_0m      = "Total Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_hdl_mmoll_0m       = "HDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_ldl_mmoll_0m       = "LDL Cholesterol (mmol/L)",
    blood_tg_mmoll_0m        = "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",

    # Glycemic Measure
    blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m   = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)",
    hba1c_cat_0m_lab         = "HbA1c Category",
    
    # Sociodemographics
    eth_bin                 = "Ethnicity",
    area_imd_qt             = "Index of Multiple Deprivation (Quintile)"
  ),

  # Column group header (matches the blog example layout)
  groups = list("", "Age Group")
)

groupspan <- c(1, length(strata) - 1)

#-----------------------------#
# Define render settings (For setting digits and choosing mean/median if desired)

#my.render.cont <- function(x) {
#  s <- stats.apply.rounding(stats.default(x), digits = 0)  # change digits here
#  c("",
#    "Mean (SD)" = sprintf("%s (\u00B1 %s)", s$MEAN, s$SD)
#  )
#}

#my.render.cat <- function(x) {
#  c("",
#    sapply(stats.default(x), function(y) with(y,
#      sprintf("%d (%0.0f %%)", FREQ, PCT)
#    ))
#  )
#}

#-----------------------------#
# Create table of baseline characteristics

stab1_descstat_age <- table1(
  x           = strata,
  labels      = labels,
  groupspan   = groupspan,
  render.continuous = c(
    "Mean (SD)"        = "mean (sd)",
    "Median (IQR)"     = "q50 (q25, q75)",
    "Range [Min, Max]" = "min, max"
  )
  #footnote    = footnote,
  #render.continuous  = my.render.cont,
  #render.categorical = my.render.cat
)

stab1_descstat_age


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table
## Easiest way is the export through RStudio viewer (save as webpage)
## Then print as PDF through a browswer or Adobe Acrobat

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig1. Weight and BMI change (absolute and percentage) by subgroup heatmap
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Copy dataset

data_hm <- copy(data_wide)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Factor ordering (so panels/axes match layout)

data_hm[, age_cat5 := factor(
  age_cat5,
  levels = c("18–29","30–39","40–49","50–59","60–79")
)]

data_hm[, area_imd_qt := factor(
  area_imd_qt,
  levels = c(
    "1 (Most Deprived)",
    "2", "3", "4",
    "5 (Least Deprived)"
  ),
  labels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5")
)]

# Reduce baseline weight categories from 5 to 3 due to small cell counts
data_hm[, wt_kg_0m_cat3 := factor(
  fifelse(
    wt_kg_0m_cat == "<90 kg", "<90 kg",
    fifelse(
      wt_kg_0m_cat %in% c("90–99 kg", "100–109 kg"), "90–109 kg",
      "≥110 kg"
    )
  ),
  levels = c("<90 kg", "90–109 kg", "≥110 kg")
)]

# Reduce baseline BMI categories from 5 to 3 due to small cell counts
data_hm[, bmi_0m_cat3 := factor(
  fifelse(
    bmi_0m_cat %in% c("Overweight (25.0–29.9)", 
                      "Obesity I (30.0–34.9)"),
    "Overweight / Obesity I (25.0–34.9)",
    fifelse(
      bmi_0m_cat == "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
      "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
      "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
    )
  ),
  levels = c(
    "Overweight / Obesity I (25.0–34.9)",
    "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
    "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
  )
)]



#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Collapse to cell means: (0m weight / BMI panel) x (IMD row) x (Age col)

#-----------------------------#
# Weight mean change columns 

hm_kg_abs <- data_hm[!is.na(wt_change_kg_06m),
  .(value = mean(wt_change_kg_06m, na.rm = TRUE), n = .N),
  by = .(wt_kg_0m_cat3, area_imd_qt, age_cat5)
]

hm_kg_pct <- data_hm[!is.na(wt_change_pct_06m),
  .(value = mean(wt_change_pct_06m, na.rm = TRUE), n = .N),
  by = .(wt_kg_0m_cat3, area_imd_qt, age_cat5)
]

#-----------------------------#
# BMI mean change columns

# Absolute BMI change (6m - 0m)
hm_bmi_abs <- data_hm[!is.na(bmi_0m) & !is.na(bmi_6m),
  .(value = mean(bmi_6m - bmi_0m, na.rm = TRUE), n = .N),
  by = .(bmi_0m_cat3, area_imd_qt, age_cat5)
]

# Percent BMI change ((6m - 0m) / 0m * 100)
hm_bmi_pct <- data_hm[!is.na(bmi_0m) & !is.na(bmi_6m) & !is.na(bmi_0m) & bmi_0m != 0,
  .(value = mean(((bmi_6m - bmi_0m) / bmi_0m) * 100, na.rm = TRUE), n = .N),
  by = .(bmi_0m_cat3, area_imd_qt, age_cat5)
]

#-----------------------------#
# Create weight grid

# Complete grid built from data_hm levels
wt_grid <- CJ(
  wt_kg_0m_cat3   = levels(data_hm$wt_kg_0m_cat3),
  area_imd_qt = levels(data_hm$area_imd_qt),
  age_cat5    = levels(data_hm$age_cat5),
  unique = TRUE
)

# Make join types match
wt_grid[, `:=`(
  wt_kg_0m_cat3 = factor(wt_kg_0m_cat3, levels = levels(data_hm$wt_kg_0m_cat3)),
  area_imd_qt = factor(area_imd_qt, levels = levels(data_hm$area_imd_qt)),
  age_cat5 = factor(age_cat5, levels = levels(data_hm$age_cat5))
)]

# Join full grid
hm_kg_abs  <- merge(wt_grid, hm_kg_abs,  by = c("wt_kg_0m_cat3","area_imd_qt","age_cat5"), all.x = TRUE)
hm_kg_pct <- merge(wt_grid, hm_kg_pct, by = c("wt_kg_0m_cat3","area_imd_qt","age_cat5"), all.x = TRUE)

#-----------------------------#
# Create BMI grid

# Complete grid built from data_hm levels
bmi_grid <- CJ(
  bmi_0m_cat3   = levels(data_hm$bmi_0m_cat3),
  area_imd_qt = levels(data_hm$area_imd_qt),
  age_cat5    = levels(data_hm$age_cat5),
  unique = TRUE
)

# Make join types match
bmi_grid[, `:=`(
  bmi_0m_cat3 = factor(bmi_0m_cat3, levels = levels(data_hm$bmi_0m_cat3)),
  area_imd_qt = factor(area_imd_qt, levels = levels(data_hm$area_imd_qt)),
  age_cat5 = factor(age_cat5, levels = levels(data_hm$age_cat5))
)]

# Merge BMI summaries onto the same grid
hm_bmi_abs <- merge(bmi_grid, hm_bmi_abs, by = c("bmi_0m_cat3","area_imd_qt","age_cat5"), all.x = TRUE)
hm_bmi_pct <- merge(bmi_grid, hm_bmi_pct, by = c("bmi_0m_cat3","area_imd_qt","age_cat5"), all.x = TRUE)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot function (tile + value labels), faceted by baseline group

# Create custom function to plot heatmap (with default set to baseline weight)
plot_heatmap <- function(
  dt, title, legend_title,
  digits = 1,
  facet_var = "wt_kg_0m_cat3",
  facet_levels = NULL,
  facet_prefix = "Baseline Weight\n"
) {

  dt_plot <- copy(dt)

  # Ensure correct vertical ordering (facet group ordering)
  if (!is.null(facet_levels)) {
    dt_plot[, (facet_var) := factor(as.character(get(facet_var)), levels = facet_levels)]
  }

  # Facet labels: e.g. "Baseline Weight\n<90 kg" OR "Baseline BMI\nObesity II (35.0–39.9)"
  facet_lab_var <- paste0(facet_var, "_lab")
  dt_plot[, (facet_lab_var) := factor(
    get(facet_var),
    levels = levels(get(facet_var)),
    labels = paste0(facet_prefix, levels(get(facet_var)))
  )]

  # Tile labels (append * if n < 20)
  dt_plot[, label := ifelse(
    is.na(value),
    "",
    paste0(
      sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f"), value),
      ifelse(n < 20, "*", "")
    )
  )]

  # Text colour change (black and white depending on colour darkness)
  mid_val <- quantile(dt_plot$value, 0.85, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7) # set white to start at 85%
  dt_plot[, label_col := ifelse(
    is.na(value),
    NA_character_,
    ifelse(value <= mid_val, "white", "black") # set white first then black if needed
  )]

  ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = age_cat5, y = area_imd_qt, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4, na.rm = FALSE) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = label, colour = label_col),
      size = 4,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradient(
      low = "#002F6C",
      high = "#E6F2F1",
      name = legend_title,
      oob = squish
    ) +
    scale_colour_identity() +
    guides(
      fill = guide_colorbar(
        title.position = "top",
        title.hjust = 0.5,
        barwidth  = unit(5, "cm"),
        barheight = unit(0.5, "cm")
      )
    ) +
    coord_equal(ratio = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(
      stats::as.formula(paste0("~ ", facet_lab_var)),
      ncol = 1,
      strip.position = "top"
    ) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Age Group (Years)",
      y = "IMD Quintiles\n(1 = Most Deprived, 5 = Least Deprived)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),

      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13),
      axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)),

      strip.background = element_blank(),
      strip.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain", hjust = 0, vjust = 1),
      strip.placement = "outside",

      axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(3, "pt"),

      panel.spacing.y = unit(0.6, "lines"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),

      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.box      = "vertical",
      legend.box.just = "center",

      legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.text = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
    )
}


# Create weight kg plot
sfig1_hm_kg <- plot_heatmap(
  hm_kg_abs, "Absolute Weight Change (kg)", "Mean Change (kg)", digits = 1,
  facet_var = "wt_kg_0m_cat3",
  facet_levels = c("<90 kg", "90–109 kg", "≥110 kg"),
  facet_prefix = "Baseline Weight\n"
)

# Create weight % plot
sfig1_hm_kg_pct <- plot_heatmap(
  hm_kg_pct, "Percentage Weight Change (%)", "Mean Change (%)", digits = 1,
  facet_var = "wt_kg_0m_cat3",
  facet_levels = c("<90 kg", "90–109 kg", "≥110 kg"),
  facet_prefix = "Baseline Weight\n"
)

# Create BMI abs plot
sfig1_hm_bmi_abs <- plot_heatmap(
  hm_bmi_abs, "Absolute BMI Change", "Mean Change (BMI)", digits = 1,
  facet_var = "bmi_0m_cat3",
  facet_levels = c(
    "Overweight / Obesity I (25.0–34.9)",
    "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
    "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
  ),
  facet_prefix = "Baseline BMI\n"
)

# Create BMI % plot
sfig1_hm_bmi_pct <- plot_heatmap(
  hm_bmi_pct, "Percentage BMI Change (%)", "Mean Change (%)", digits = 1,
  facet_var = "bmi_0m_cat3",
  facet_levels = c(
    "Overweight / Obesity I (25.0–34.9)",
    "Obesity II (35.0–39.9)",
    "Obesity III (≥40.0)"
  ),
  facet_prefix = "Baseline BMI\n"
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Print side by side layout for heatmap
# (adds two BMI panels to the right of the existing two)

sfig1_hm <-
  (sfig1_hm_kg | sfig1_hm_kg_pct | sfig1_hm_bmi_abs | sfig1_hm_bmi_pct) +
  plot_layout(guides = "keep") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &  # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 0.995)    # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )

sfig1_hm


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# Main Plot
## PNG
ggsave(
  sfig1_hm, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig1-heatmap.png",
  width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300
)
## SVG
ggsave(
  sfig1_hm, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig1-heatmap.svg",
  width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300
)
## PDF
ggsave(
  sfig1_hm, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig1-heatmap.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

# Weight Change Plot Tables
fwrite(
  hm_kg_abs,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig1extra-heatmap-numtab-hmkg.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

fwrite(
  hm_kg_pct,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig1extra-heatmap-numtab-hmpct.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

# BMI Change Plot tables
fwrite(
  hm_bmi_abs,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig1extra-heatmap-numtab-hmbmiabs.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

fwrite(
  hm_bmi_pct,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig1extra-heatmap-numtab-hmbmipct.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig2. Biochemistry and weight change relationship forest plot
################################################################################ 

Run linear regression models and export outputs
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Run adjusted linear models for 6m change (6m - 0m)

# Define outcomes to model (follow-up variable name - baseline variable name)
outcomes <- list(
  blood_chol_mmoll     = c("blood_chol_mmoll_6m",      "blood_chol_mmoll_0m"),
  blood_hdl_mmoll      = c("blood_hdl_mmoll_6m",       "blood_hdl_mmoll_0m"),
  blood_ldl_mmoll      = c("blood_ldl_mmoll_6m",       "blood_ldl_mmoll_0m"),
  blood_tg_mmoll       = c("blood_tg_mmoll_6m",        "blood_tg_mmoll_0m"),
  blood_hba1c_mmolmol  = c("blood_hba1c_mmolmol_6m",   "blood_hba1c_mmolmol_0m")
)

# Define covariates
adjust_vars <- c("age_cat5", "eth_bin", "area_imd_qt", "wt_kg_0m_cat")

# Formatting controls
digits_est <- 3
digits_p   <- 3

# Helper to format p-values nicely
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p), NA_character_,
    ifelse(p < 10^(-digits_p),
           paste0("<", formatC(10^(-digits_p), format = "f", digits = digits_p)),
           formatC(p, format = "f", digits = digits_p))
  )
}

# Helper function: pull the wt_change coefficient row
## A scale function has been added to change 1kg change as exposure to 10kg
extract_wt_coef <- function(fit, term = "wt_change_kg_06m", scale = 1) {
  sm <- summary(fit)
  coefs <- sm$coefficients
  if (!term %in% rownames(coefs)) return(NULL)

  est <- unname(coefs[term, "Estimate"]) * scale
  se  <- unname(coefs[term, "Std. Error"]) * scale
  t   <- unname(coefs[term, "t value"])
  p   <- unname(coefs[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])

  ci <- tryCatch(stats::confint(fit, level = 0.95)[term, ] * scale,
                 error = function(e) c(NA_real_, NA_real_))

  list(
    estimate = est,
    se       = se,
    t_value  = t,
    p_value  = p,
    ci_low   = unname(ci[1]),
    ci_high  = unname(ci[2])
  )
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Fit models and store them (for checking)

sfig2_bio_wt_models <- setNames(vector("list", length(outcomes)), names(outcomes))

# Loop over outcomes, create change vars, fit models, extract wt_change coefficient
sfig2_bio_wt_tab <- rbindlist(lapply(names(outcomes), function(out_nm) {

  fu0 <- outcomes[[out_nm]][1]
  bl0 <- outcomes[[out_nm]][2]
  chg <- paste0(out_nm, "_change_06m")

  # Create change variable: 6m - 0m
  data_wide[, (chg) := get(fu0) - get(bl0)]

  # Model: change ~ wt_change + covariates
  fml <- as.formula(
    paste0(chg, " ~ wt_change_kg_06m + ", paste(adjust_vars, collapse = " + "))
  )

  fit <- lm(fml, data = data_wide)

  # Store model for checking
  sfig2_bio_wt_models[[out_nm]] <<- fit

  # Extract wt_change term
  res <- extract_wt_coef(fit, term = "wt_change_kg_06m", scale = 10)
  if (is.null(res)) {
    return(data.table(
      outcome = out_nm,
      outcome_change_var = chg,
      n = stats::nobs(fit),
      estimate = NA_character_,
      se       = NA_character_,
      t_value  = NA_character_,
      p_value  = NA_character_,
      ci_low   = NA_character_,
      ci_high  = NA_character_,
      estimate_ci = NA_character_
    ))
  }

  # Format numbers
  est_f <- formatC(res$estimate, format = "f", digits = digits_est)
  se_f  <- formatC(res$se,       format = "f", digits = digits_est)
  t_f   <- formatC(res$t_value,  format = "f", digits = digits_est)
  lo_f  <- formatC(res$ci_low,   format = "f", digits = digits_est)
  hi_f  <- formatC(res$ci_high,  format = "f", digits = digits_est)
  p_f   <- fmt_p(res$p_value)

  data.table(
    outcome = out_nm,
    outcome_change_var = chg,
    n = stats::nobs(fit),
    estimate = est_f,
    se       = se_f,
    t_value  = t_f,
    p_value  = p_f,
    ci_low   = lo_f,
    ci_high  = hi_f,
    estimate_ci = paste0(est_f, " (", lo_f, ", ", hi_f, ")")
  )
}), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# View outputs
sfig2_bio_wt_tab

# Check models for confirmation
lapply(sfig2_bio_wt_models, broom::tidy)

```

Convert table into forest plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Convert table into forest plot

# Define outcome labels for plotting
outcome_labs <- c(
  blood_chol_mmoll     = "Total cholesterol (mmol/L)",
  blood_hdl_mmoll      = "HDL cholesterol (mmol/L)",
  blood_ldl_mmoll      = "LDL cholesterol (mmol/L)",
  blood_tg_mmoll       = "Triglycerides (mmol/L)",
  blood_hba1c_mmolmol  = "HbA1c (mmol/mol)"
)

sfig2_bio_wt_tab[, outcome_label := fifelse(outcome %chin% names(outcome_labs),
                                    outcome_labs[outcome],
                                    outcome)]

# Keep a raw table with numeric columns for plotting
fplot_tab_raw <- copy(sfig2_bio_wt_tab)

# Build the display table used by forestploter (with a blank CI column)
fplot_tab <- copy(fplot_tab_raw)

fplot_tab[, ` ` := strrep(" ", 75)]     # forestploter draws here
fplot_tab[, N   := fifelse(is.na(estimate), "", as.character(n))]

# Ensure character columns are not NA
fplot_tab[is.na(estimate_ci), estimate_ci := ""]
fplot_tab[is.na(p_value),     p_value     := ""]

fplot_tab <- fplot_tab[, .(
  Outcome        = outcome_label,
  ` `,                                 # CI column
  N,
  `Estimate (95% CI)` = estimate_ci,
  `P Value`       = p_value
)]

#-----------------------------#
# Insert spacer rows (blank lines) to increase vertical spacing

# Add a spacer after every plotted row EXCEPT the last
keep_idx <- which(!is.na(fplot_tab_raw$estimate))

# Indices to insert: after each kept row except the last
insert_after <- keep_idx[-length(keep_idx)]

# Expanded indices: original row, then (if needed) spacer
expanded_idx <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(fplot_tab_raw)), function(i) {
  if (i %in% insert_after) c(i, NA_integer_) else i
}))

# Expand numeric raw table (for est/lower/upper alignment)
fplot_tab_raw_sp <- fplot_tab_raw[expanded_idx]
fplot_tab_raw_sp[is.na(expanded_idx), `:=`(
  estimate = NA_real_,
  ci_low   = NA_real_,
  ci_high  = NA_real_
)]

# Expand display table (table rows)
fplot_tab_sp <- fplot_tab[expanded_idx]
fplot_tab_sp[is.na(expanded_idx), `:=`(
  Outcome = "",
  ` `     = "",   # CI column
  N       = "",
  `Estimate (95% CI)` = "",
  `P Value` = ""
)]

# Overwrite objects used downstream
fplot_tab_raw <- fplot_tab_raw_sp
fplot_tab     <- fplot_tab_sp

#-----------------------------#
# Add spacing under header (prepend one blank spacer row)

# Prepend one blank row to the numeric table (keeps est/lower/upper aligned)
blank_raw <- fplot_tab_raw[0][1]
blank_raw[1, `:=`(
  estimate = NA_real_,
  ci_low   = NA_real_,
  ci_high  = NA_real_
)]
fplot_tab_raw <- rbind(blank_raw, fplot_tab_raw, fill = TRUE)

# Prepend one blank row to the display table
blank_disp <- fplot_tab[0][1]
blank_disp[1, `:=`(
  Outcome = "",
  ` `     = "",
  N       = "",
  `Estimate (95% CI)` = "",
  `P Value` = ""
)]
fplot_tab <- rbind(blank_disp, fplot_tab, fill = TRUE)

#-----------------------------#
# Setup theme and formatting

# Define theme
tm <- forest_theme(
  base_size = 12,
  core = list(
    padding   = unit(c(10, 6.5), "mm"),
    bg_params = list(fill = "white")
  ),
  arrow_type = "closed",
  arrow_label_just = "end",
  xlab_margin = unit(c(4, 0, 0, 0), "mm")
)

# X-axis limits/ticks based on numeric CI ranges
x_min <- as.numeric(min(fplot_tab_raw$ci_low,  na.rm = TRUE))
x_max <- as.numeric(max(fplot_tab_raw$ci_high, na.rm = TRUE))
pad   <- 0.10 * (x_max - x_min)
xlim  <- c(x_min - pad, x_max + pad)
ticks_at <- pretty(xlim, n = 4)
ticks_at <- ticks_at[ticks_at >= xlim[1] & ticks_at <= xlim[2]]

# Forest plot (ref_line = 0 for LM coefficient null)
sfig2_bio_wt_fplot <- forest(
  data       = fplot_tab,
  est        = as.numeric(fplot_tab_raw$estimate),
  lower      = as.numeric(fplot_tab_raw$ci_low),
  upper      = as.numeric(fplot_tab_raw$ci_high),
  ci_column  = which(names(fplot_tab) == " "),
  ref_line   = 0,
  xlab       = "Estimated outcome change per 10 kg change in weight",
  xlim       = xlim,
  ticks_at   = ticks_at,
  arrow_lab = c(
    "Outcome changes opposite\nto weight change",
    "Outcome changes in same\ndirection as weight change"
  ),
  theme      = tm
)

#-----------------------------#
# Reduce spacing under header (shrink first body row slightly)

gt <- sfig2_bio_wt_fplot

# Find the first body row (row just under column headers)
header_row <- max(gt$layout$t[grepl("colhead", gt$layout$name)])
first_body_row <- header_row + 1

# Reduce its height slightly
gt$heights[first_body_row] <- gt$heights[first_body_row] - unit(2, "mm")

# Overwrite plot object
sfig2_bio_wt_fplot <- gt

#-----------------------------#
# Render

plot(sfig2_bio_wt_fplot)

```

Export outputs
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export output as csv

fwrite(
  sfig2_bio_wt_tab,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig2extra-bio-wt-tab-raw.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export output as forest plot

# Get forest plot width and height 
# Note: manually changing width and height will only expand canvas
fplot_wh <- get_wh(plot = sfig2_bio_wt_fplot, unit = "in")

# Define base file path once
base_path <- "Final Outputs/sfig2-bio-wt-lm-forestplot"

# PNG
png(
  paste0(base_path, ".png"),
  res   = 300,
  width = fplot_wh[1],
  height = fplot_wh[2],
  units = "in"
)
sfig2_bio_wt_fplot
dev.off()

# SVG
svg(
  paste0(base_path, ".svg"),
  width  = fplot_wh[1],
  height = fplot_wh[2]
)
sfig2_bio_wt_fplot
dev.off()

# PDF
pdf(
  paste0(base_path, ".pdf"),
  width  = fplot_wh[1],
  height = fplot_wh[2]
)
sfig2_bio_wt_fplot
dev.off()

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig3. KM plot for attrition with mutually adjusted cox regression output
################################################################################ 

The plot will be artificially low before week 8 as we defined attrition as no subsequent prescription past 9 weeks.

Cox regression and output extraction
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create km plot dataset

# Extract relevant columns
data_attr_km <- data[
  ,.(
    patid, wk_idx, wk_st_date, wk_end_date, glp1_st_wk, glp1_st_date, glp1_idx,
    attr_date, attr_wk, attr_ever_yn, attr_status,
    age_cat5, sex, eth_bin, area_imd_qt, wt_kg_0m_cat
  )
]

# Convert to wide
data_attr_km <- data_attr_km[, .SD[1], by = patid]

# Assign 26 to NA in attr_glp1_idx (max follow-up)
data_attr_km[is.na(attr_wk) & attr_status == FALSE, attr_wk := 26]

# Check distribution of attrition
summary(data_attr_km$attr_date)
table(data_attr_km$attr_wk, useNA = "always")


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Perform cox regression

# Lock factor order and set reference groups
data_attr_km[, age_cat5 := factor(
  age_cat5,
  levels = c("18–29", "30–39", "40–49", "50–59", "60–79")
)]
data_attr_km[, age_cat5 := relevel(age_cat5, ref = "18–29")]

data_attr_km[, area_imd_qt := factor(
  area_imd_qt,
  levels = c("1 (Most Deprived)", "2", "3", "4", "5 (Least Deprived)")
)]
data_attr_km[, area_imd_qt := relevel(area_imd_qt, ref = "1 (Most Deprived)")]

data_attr_km[, eth_bin := factor(
  eth_bin,
  levels = c("White", "Non-White")
)]
data_attr_km[, eth_bin := relevel(eth_bin, ref = "White")]

data_attr_km[, wt_kg_0m_cat := factor(
  wt_kg_0m_cat,
  levels = c("<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg")
)]
data_attr_km[, wt_kg_0m_cat := relevel(wt_kg_0m_cat, ref = "≥120 kg")]

# Fit Cox model
cox_attr <- coxph(
  Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ age_cat5 + eth_bin + area_imd_qt + wt_kg_0m_cat,
  data = data_attr_km
)

# Proportional hazards test using scaled Schoenfeld residuals
zph_attr <- cox.zph(cox_attr, transform = "km")  # "km" is a common choice
zph_attr


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Extract outputs into tables

# Helper function: extract and format HR table from cox model
cox_hr_dt <- function(fit, digits = 2, p_digits = 3) {
  s <- summary(fit)

  dt <- as.data.table(s$coefficients, keep.rownames = "term")
  ci <- as.data.table(s$conf.int, keep.rownames = "term")

  # Merge coefficient table + CI table
  out <- merge(
    dt[, .(term,
           beta = coef,
           se   = `se(coef)`,
           z    = z,
           p_value = `Pr(>|z|)`)],
    ci[, .(term,
           hr = `exp(coef)`,
           ci_low = `lower .95`,
           ci_high = `upper .95`)],
    by = "term",
    all = TRUE
  )

  # Format strings for display under KM curves
  out[, hr_ci := sprintf(
    paste0("%.", digits, "f (%.", digits, "f–%.", digits, "f)"),
    hr, ci_low, ci_high
  )]

  out[, p_value_fmt := fifelse(
    is.na(p_value), NA_character_,
    fifelse(p_value < 10^(-p_digits), paste0("<", formatC(10^(-p_digits), format = "f", digits = p_digits)),
            formatC(p_value, format = "f", digits = p_digits))
  )]

  # Keep clean columns
  out[, .(term, hr, ci_low, ci_high, hr_ci, p_value, p_value_fmt)]
}

# Extract full cox regression results
hr_all <- cox_hr_dt(cox_attr)

# Split into panel tables + pretty subgroup labels
hr_age <- hr_all[grepl("^age_cat5", term)]
hr_age[, subgroup := sub("^age_cat5", "", term)]
hr_age <- hr_age[, .(subgroup, hr_ci, p_value_fmt)]

hr_imd <- hr_all[grepl("^area_imd_qt", term)]
hr_imd[, subgroup := sub("^area_imd_qt", "", term)]
hr_imd <- hr_imd[, .(subgroup, hr_ci, p_value_fmt)]

hr_eth <- hr_all[grepl("^eth_bin", term)]
hr_eth[, subgroup := sub("^eth_bin", "", term)]
hr_eth <- hr_eth[, .(subgroup, hr_ci, p_value_fmt)]

hr_wt <- hr_all[grepl("^wt_kg_0m_cat", term)]
hr_wt[, subgroup := sub("^wt_kg_0m_cat", "", term)]
hr_wt <- hr_wt[, .(subgroup, hr_ci, p_value_fmt)]

# Helper function: add reference rows to tables
add_ref_row <- function(dt, ref_label) {
  rbind(
    data.table(subgroup = paste0(ref_label), hr_ci = "1.00 (ref)", p_value_fmt = NA_character_),
    dt
  )
}

# Assign reference rows to all tables
hr_age <- add_ref_row(hr_age, "18–29")
hr_imd <- add_ref_row(hr_imd, "1 (Most Deprived)")
hr_eth <- add_ref_row(hr_eth, "White")
hr_wt  <- add_ref_row(hr_wt,  "≥120 kg")

# Order tables manually (to match KM strata order)
## Age
hr_age[, subgroup := factor(
  subgroup,
  levels = c("18–29", "30–39", "40–49", "50–59", "60–79")
)]
setorder(hr_age, subgroup)
hr_age[, subgroup := as.character(subgroup)]
## IMD
hr_imd[, subgroup := factor(
  subgroup,
  levels = c("1 (Most Deprived)", "2", "3", "4", "5 (Least Deprived)")
)]
setorder(hr_imd, subgroup)
hr_imd[, subgroup := as.character(subgroup)]
## Ethnicity
hr_eth[, subgroup := factor(
  subgroup,
  levels = c("White", "Non-White")
)]
setorder(hr_eth, subgroup)
hr_eth[, subgroup := as.character(subgroup)]
## Baseline weight
hr_wt[, subgroup := factor(
  subgroup,
  levels = c("<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg")
)]
setorder(hr_wt, subgroup)
hr_wt[, subgroup := as.character(subgroup)]

# Blank out NA values in reference categories
hr_age[is.na(p_value_fmt), p_value_fmt := ""]
hr_imd[is.na(p_value_fmt), p_value_fmt := ""]
hr_eth[is.na(p_value_fmt), p_value_fmt := ""]
hr_wt [is.na(p_value_fmt), p_value_fmt := ""]

# Check tables
hr_age
hr_imd
hr_eth
hr_wt

```

a) Full sample
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create km plot

# Fit regression
km_attr_full <- survfit(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ 1, data = data_attr_km)
summary(km_attr_full)

# Build the exact palette vector (same colours for plot + risk table)
full_cols <- "#0B2A5B"

# Plot
## No 95% CI necessary as we are just plotting it
sfig3a_km_attr_full <- ggsurvplot(
  km_attr_full,
  data                = data_attr_km,
  fun                 = "event",
  conf.int            = FALSE,
  #conf.int.alpha      = 0.25,
  conf.int.col        = full_cols,
  conf.int.fill       = full_cols,
  censor              = TRUE,
  risk.table          = TRUE,
  risk.table.height   = 0.08,
  #risk.table.fontsize = 3.2,
  risk.table.y.text.col = FALSE,
  xlab                = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
  ylab                = "Proportion of Patients (%)",
  break.time.by       = 2,
  palette             = full_cols
)

# Edit plot styling
sfig3a_km_attr_full$plot <- sfig3a_km_attr_full$plot +
  labs(title = "Full Study Sample") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, margin = margin(b = 18)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 14)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 14)),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 0.10))

# Edit risk table styling
sfig3a_km_attr_full$table <- sfig3a_km_attr_full$table +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +  # prevent table clipping on the right
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 2, b = 0, l = 2),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x  = element_blank(),
    # keep space, but hide strata axis
    axis.text.y  = element_text(colour = "transparent"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "transparent"),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

print(sfig3a_km_attr_full)

```

b) Age Group Stratified
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create km plot

# Lock the strata order
data_attr_km[
  ,
  age_cat5 := factor(
    age_cat5,
    levels = c("18–29", "30–39", "40–49", "50–59", "60–79")
  )
]

# Fit regression
km_attr_age <- survfit(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ age_cat5, data = data_attr_km)
summary(km_attr_age)

# Check strata names (for legend label order)
names(km_attr_age$strata)

# Build the exact palette vector (same colours for plot + risk table)
age_cols <- as.character(pal_custom_5)

# Plot
sfig3b_km_attr_age <- ggsurvplot(
  km_attr_age,
  data                = data_attr_km,
  fun                 = "event",
  conf.int            = FALSE,
  censor              = TRUE,
  risk.table          = FALSE,
  xlab                = "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation",
  ylab                = "Proportion of Patients (%)",
  break.time.by       = 2,
  legend.labs         = c("18–29", "30–39", "40–49", "50–59", "60–79"),
  palette             = age_cols
)

# Edit plot styling
sfig3b_km_attr_age$plot <- sfig3b_km_attr_age$plot +
  labs(title = "Stratified by Age Group") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, margin = margin(b = 18)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 14)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 14)),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 0.20))

#-----------------------------#
# Table

# Create table
sfig3b_hr_age <- hr_age[
  ,
  .(
    `Age Group` = subgroup,
    `HR (95% CI)` = hr_ci,
    `P Value` = p_value_fmt
  )
]

# Convert to grob table
sfig3b_hr_age <- tableGrob(
  sfig3b_hr_age,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    base_size = 11,

    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    ),

    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
      bg_params = list(col = NA)
    )
  )
)

# Add header line to grob table
sfig3b_hr_age <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  sfig3b_hr_age,
  grobs = grid::segmentsGrob(
    x0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    x1 = unit(1, "npc"),
    y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
    y1 = unit(0, "npc"),
    gp  = gpar(col = "grey60", lwd = 0.8)
  ),
  t = 1, b = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(sfig3b_hr_age)
)

# Create title grob
title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "Mutually Adjusted Hazard Ratios",
  gp   = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11.5, fontface = "plain"),
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  x    = grid::unit(0, "npc"),
  y    = grid::unit(0.5, "npc")
)

# Create small row above the table and insert title
sfig3b_hr_age <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  sfig3b_hr_age,
  heights = grid::unit(1.1, "lines"),
  pos = 0
)
sfig3b_hr_age <- gtable::gtable_add_grob(
  sfig3b_hr_age,
  grobs = title_grob,
  t = 1, l = 1, r = ncol(sfig3b_hr_age)
)

# Add a small spacer row between title and table
sfig3b_hr_age <- gtable::gtable_add_rows(
  sfig3b_hr_age,
  heights = grid::unit(0.6, "lines"),
  pos = 1
)

# Change grob table width
sfig3b_hr_age$widths <- grid::unit(
  c(0.28, 0.44, 0.28),
  "npc"
)

#-----------------------------#
# Stack plot and table

sfig3b_km_attr_age_hr <-
  sfig3b_km_attr_age$plot /
  patchwork::plot_spacer() /
  wrap_elements(sfig3b_hr_age) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(2, 0.1, 1))

sfig3b_km_attr_age_hr

```

```{r}
sfig3e_km_attr_wtgrp_hr

```

f) Combine into full plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine 5 plots

# helper: take either a ggsurvplot (has $plot and $table)
# OR a patchwork/ggplot object (already stacked), and add a top spacer.
km_stack <- function(g,
                     heights = c(0.82, 0.18),
                     top_gap = 0.06,
                     bottom_gap = 0) {

  # Case 1: ggsurvplot object
  if (is.list(g) && all(c("plot", "table") %in% names(g))) {

    panels  <- list(
      patchwork::plot_spacer(),
      g$plot,
      g$table
    )
    layout_heights <- c(top_gap, heights)

  } else {

    # Case 2: already a ggplot/patchwork object (e.g., plot / HR table)
    panels  <- list(
      patchwork::plot_spacer(),
      g
    )
    layout_heights <- c(top_gap, 1)
  }

  # Optional bottom spacer (used to add extra whitespace below selected panels)
  if (bottom_gap > 0) {
    panels <- c(panels, list(patchwork::plot_spacer()))
    layout_heights <- c(layout_heights, bottom_gap)
  }

  Reduce(`/`, panels) +
    patchwork::plot_layout(heights = layout_heights)
}

# Build each panel (set top_gap to control gap above title)
p1 <- km_stack(sfig3a_km_attr_full,      heights = c(0.70, 0.10), top_gap = 0.00)
p2 <- km_stack(sfig3e_km_attr_wtgrp_hr,  heights = c(0.70, 0.30), top_gap = 0.06)
p3 <- km_stack(sfig3b_km_attr_age_hr,    heights = c(0.70, 0.30), top_gap = 0.06)
p4 <- km_stack(sfig3c_km_attr_imd_hr,    heights = c(0.70, 0.30), top_gap = 0.06, bottom_gap = 0.06)
p5 <- km_stack(sfig3d_km_attr_eth_hr,    heights = c(0.70, 0.30), top_gap = 0.06, bottom_gap = 0.06)

# Wrap each panel so it behaves as a single unit for alphabetic labeling
p1w <- wrap_elements(p1)
p2w <- wrap_elements(p2)
p3w <- wrap_elements(p3)
p4w <- wrap_elements(p4)
p5w <- wrap_elements(p5)

# Add manual tags + per-panel label position control 
## (sizing is different across panels hence the need to control these separately)
p1w_tag <- p1w + labs(tag = "a") +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
        plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.935))

p2w_tag <- p2w + labs(tag = "b") +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
        plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.908))

p3w_tag <- p3w + labs(tag = "c") +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
        plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.908))

p4w_tag <- p4w + labs(tag = "d") +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
        plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.908))

p5w_tag <- p5w + labs(tag = "e") +
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
        plot.tag.position = c(0.02, 0.908))

design <- "
AA
BC
DE
"

sfig3_km_attr <-
  wrap_plots(list(p1w_tag, p2w_tag, p3w_tag, p4w_tag, p5w_tag)) +
  plot_layout(
    design  = design,
    heights = c(0.70, 1, 1) # shrink row AA
  ) &
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 10, l = 20, unit = "pt")
  )

sfig3_km_attr


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# Define base file path once
base_path <- "Final Outputs/sfig3-km-attrt"

# PNG
png(
  paste0(base_path, ".png"),
  res   = 300,
  width = 16,
  height = 23,
  units = "in"
)
sfig3_km_attr
dev.off()

# SVG
svg(
  paste0(base_path, ".svg"),
  width = 16,
  height = 23,
)
sfig3_km_attr
dev.off()

# PDF
pdf(
  paste0(base_path, ".pdf"),
  width = 16,
  height = 23,
)
sfig3_km_attr
dev.off()

```



################################################################################
