# SFig4. Side effect counts and severity by subgroups
################################################################################ 

This figure shows, within each subgroup category, the number of patients across side-effect count
bands (0 / 1 / 2 / 3+) and, within each count band, the breakdown of worst side-effect severity
(No side effects / Mild / Moderate / Severe). 

Labels above bars show total patients in each (subgroup level × count band) group 
and the proportion of the subgroup they represent (n / N_subgroup).

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Extract patient-level dataset (one row per patient) with required variables

data_sdeff_sub <- data[
  , .(
    patid,

    # Side effect summary / severity
    sdeff_total_n,
    most_severe_sdeff,

    # Subgroups
    age_cat5,
    eth_bin,
    area_imd_qt,
    wt_kg_0m_cat
  )
]

# Collapse to one row per patient
data_sdeff_sub <- data_sdeff_sub[, .SD[1], by = patid]

# Treat missing side-effect counts as zero
data_sdeff_sub[is.na(sdeff_total_n), sdeff_total_n := 0L]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Factor ordering for subgroups

data_sdeff_sub[, age_cat5 := factor(
  age_cat5,
  levels = c("18–29","30–39","40–49","50–59","60–79")
)]

data_sdeff_sub[, area_imd_qt := factor(
  area_imd_qt,
  levels = c(
    "1 (Most Deprived)",
    "2", "3", "4",
    "5 (Least Deprived)"
  ),
  labels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5")
)]

data_sdeff_sub[, wt_kg_0m_cat := factor(
  wt_kg_0m_cat,
  levels = c("<90 kg", "90–99 kg", "100–109 kg", "110–119 kg", "≥120 kg")
)]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Worst side-effect severity label (include "No Side Effects")

sev_levels <- c("No Side Effects", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")

data_sdeff_sub[
  ,
  worst_sev := factor(
    data.table::fcase(
      is.na(most_severe_sdeff) | most_severe_sdeff %chin% c("None", "No Side Effects", "No side effect"),
        "No Side Effects",
      most_severe_sdeff == "Mild",
        "Mild",
      most_severe_sdeff == "Moderate",
        "Moderate",
      most_severe_sdeff == "Severe",
        "Severe",
      default = as.character(most_severe_sdeff)
    ),
    levels = sev_levels
  )
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Side-effect count band (0 / 1 / 2 / 3+)

count_levels4 <- c("0", "1", "2", "3+")

data_sdeff_sub[
  ,
  sdeff_count_cat4 := factor(
    data.table::fcase(
      sdeff_total_n <= 0, "0",
      sdeff_total_n == 1, "1",
      sdeff_total_n == 2, "2",
      default = "3+"
    ),
    levels = count_levels4
  )
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Build long dataset: one row per patient per subgroup type

dt_long <- rbindlist(list(
  data_sdeff_sub[!is.na(age_cat5),
    .(patid, sdeff_count_cat4, worst_sev,
      subgroup = "Age", subgroup_level = age_cat5)
  ],
  data_sdeff_sub[!is.na(eth_bin),
    .(patid, sdeff_count_cat4, worst_sev,
      subgroup = "Ethnicity", subgroup_level = eth_bin)
  ],
  data_sdeff_sub[!is.na(area_imd_qt),
    .(patid, sdeff_count_cat4, worst_sev,
      subgroup = "IMD Quintiles", subgroup_level = area_imd_qt)
  ],
  data_sdeff_sub[!is.na(wt_kg_0m_cat),
    .(patid, sdeff_count_cat4, worst_sev,
      subgroup = "Baseline Weight", subgroup_level = wt_kg_0m_cat)
  ]
), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Stable facet order
dt_long[, subgroup := factor(subgroup, levels = c("Age", "IMD Quintiles", "Ethnicity", "Baseline Weight"))]

# Stable ordering within facets
dt_long[subgroup == "Age",
        subgroup_level := factor(as.character(subgroup_level), levels = levels(data_sdeff_sub$age_cat5))]

dt_long[subgroup == "IMD Quintiles",
        subgroup_level := factor(as.character(subgroup_level), levels = levels(data_sdeff_sub$area_imd_qt))]

dt_long[subgroup == "Baseline Weight",
        subgroup_level := factor(as.character(subgroup_level), levels = levels(data_sdeff_sub$wt_kg_0m_cat))]

if (is.factor(data_sdeff_sub$eth_bin)) {
  dt_long[subgroup == "Ethnicity",
          subgroup_level := factor(as.character(subgroup_level), levels = levels(data_sdeff_sub$eth_bin))]
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Summarise to COUNTS (stack segments = counts by worst severity)
# Bar total = total patients in (subgroup × subgroup_level × count band)

dt_sum <- dt_long[
  ,
  .(n = uniqueN(patid)),
  by = .(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4, worst_sev)
]

# Denominator for the label: total N in each subgroup_level (across all count bands)
dt_subN <- dt_long[
  ,
  .(N_subgrp = uniqueN(patid)),
  by = .(subgroup, subgroup_level)
]

# Bar totals (across severity) for labels
dt_barN <- dt_sum[
  ,
  .(n_bar = sum(n)),
  by = .(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4)
]

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Merge denominators and build label like old Fig6: n / N with %
dt_lab <- merge(dt_barN, dt_subN, by = c("subgroup", "subgroup_level"), all.x = TRUE)
dt_lab[
  ,
  pct_subgrp := n_bar / N_subgrp
]
# 2-line label: n/N on row 1, x% on row 2
dt_lab[
  ,
  lab := paste0(
    n_bar, "/", N_subgrp, "\n",
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * pct_subgrp), "%"
  )
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combined x label: subgroup level + count band (nested look)
# Note: make x key UNIQUE across facets by including subgroup
# Order: subgroup level then side-effect count (18–29 0, 18–29 1, 18–29 2, 18–29 3+, then 30–39 0, ...)

dt_sum[
  ,
  x2 := interaction(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4, sep = "  |  ", drop = TRUE)
]

dt_sum[
  ,
  x2_lab := paste0(as.character(subgroup_level), "\n", as.character(sdeff_count_cat4))
]

# Explicit factor ordering for x within each subgroup facet
dt_sum[
  ,
  x2 := factor(
    x2,
    levels = dt_sum[order(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4), unique(x2)]
  )
]

# Match x keys for label table (and keep the same ordering)
dt_lab[
  ,
  x2 := interaction(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4, sep = "  |  ", drop = TRUE)
]
dt_lab[
  ,
  x2 := factor(
    x2,
    levels = levels(dt_sum$x2)
  )
]

dt_lab[
  ,
  x2_lab := paste0(as.character(subgroup_level), "\n", as.character(sdeff_count_cat4))
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Convert stacked COUNTS to proportions of the subgroup (bar height = n/N_subgrp)

dt_sum <- merge(dt_sum, dt_subN, by = c("subgroup", "subgroup_level"), all.x = TRUE)
dt_sum[
  ,
  prop_subgrp := n / N_subgrp
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Label position = top of stacked bar (total proportion)
# (use a separate data frame so we label each bar once)

dt_y_top <- dt_sum[
  ,
  .(y_top = sum(prop_subgrp)),
  by = .(subgroup, subgroup_level, sdeff_count_cat4, x2)
]

dt_lab_pos <- merge(
  dt_lab,
  dt_y_top,
  by = c("subgroup", "subgroup_level", "sdeff_count_cat4", "x2"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

# Named x-label mapping (required for scale_x_discrete)
x_lab_map <- unique(dt_sum[, .(x2, x2_lab)])
x_lab_map <- setNames(x_lab_map$x2_lab, as.character(x_lab_map$x2))


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Custom x spacing: narrow within subgroup level, wider between subgroup levels

within_step <- 1.0
between_gap <- 1.2

# Smaller between-level gap just for Ethnicity (reduces White vs Non-White spacing)
between_gap_eth <- 0.6

count_pos <- setNames(seq_along(count_levels4) - 1, count_levels4)

dt_sum[
  ,
  lvl_idx := as.integer(factor(subgroup_level, levels = unique(subgroup_level))),
  by = subgroup
]

dt_sum[
  ,
  gap_use := fifelse(subgroup == "Ethnicity", between_gap_eth, between_gap)
]

dt_sum[
  ,
  x_pos := (lvl_idx - 1) * (within_step * length(count_levels4) + gap_use) +
    unname(count_pos[as.character(sdeff_count_cat4)]) * within_step
]

dt_lab_pos[
  ,
  lvl_idx := as.integer(factor(subgroup_level, levels = unique(subgroup_level))),
  by = subgroup
]

dt_lab_pos[
  ,
  gap_use := fifelse(subgroup == "Ethnicity", between_gap_eth, between_gap)
]

dt_lab_pos[
  ,
  x_pos := (lvl_idx - 1) * (within_step * length(count_levels4) + gap_use) +
    unname(count_pos[as.character(sdeff_count_cat4)]) * within_step
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Make x positions unique across subgroup facets (prevents duplicated tick labels)

# width of one subgroup panel's x-range (in the same units as x_pos)
panel_width <- (max(dt_sum$lvl_idx, na.rm = TRUE) - 1) *
  (within_step * length(count_levels4) + between_gap) +
  (max(unname(count_pos), na.rm = TRUE) * within_step) +
  between_gap

# subgroup index (stable because subgroup is already a factor with fixed levels)
dt_sum[, sub_idx := as.integer(subgroup)]
dt_lab_pos[, sub_idx := as.integer(subgroup)]

# shift each subgroup's x positions by a constant offset
dt_sum[, x_pos := x_pos + (sub_idx - 1) * panel_width]
dt_lab_pos[, x_pos := x_pos + (sub_idx - 1) * panel_width]

# recompute ticks after shifting
x_ticks <- unique(dt_sum[, .(x_pos, x2_lab)])


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Plot: stacked PROPORTION bars, facetted by subgroup type (stack facets vertically)
# Labels above bars: n/N_subgroup (pct)

# Assign palettes
pal_sev4 <- pal_custom_5[1:4]
names(pal_sev4) <- sev_levels

# Assign facet alphabetical labels (one per panel)
dt_tags <- data.table(
  subgroup = factor(
    c("Age", "IMD Quintiles", "Ethnicity", "Baseline Weight"),
    levels = levels(dt_long$subgroup)  # uses your stable facet order
  ),
  tag = c("a", "b", "c", "d")
)

sfig4_sdeff_subgrp <-
  ggplot(dt_sum, aes(x = x_pos, y = prop_subgrp, fill = worst_sev)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    data = dt_lab_pos,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_top, label = lab),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = -0.35,
    size = 2.7,
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ subgroup, ncol = 1, scales = "free_x") + # stack facets vertically

  # --- add panel letters ---
  geom_text(
    data = dt_tags,
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = tag),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 5,
    hjust = 3,   # nudge left
    vjust = -0.6 # nudge up
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  # -------------------------

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = x_ticks$x_pos,
    labels = x_ticks$x2_lab,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Subgroup Level and Side Effect Count",
    y = "Proportion of Subgroup",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "plain", size = 11, hjust = 0),
    strip.placement  = "outside",
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 7, lineheight = 0.95),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    plot.margin      = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 12)  # gives left room for tags if needed
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = pal_sev4,
    name   = "Maximum Side Effect Severity:"
  )


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export figure

# PNG
ggsave(
  sfig4_sdeff_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig4-sdeff-subgrp.png",
  width = 16, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  sfig4_sdeff_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig4-sdeff-subgrp.svg",
  width = 16, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  sfig4_sdeff_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig4-sdeff-subgrp.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 16, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig5. Weight loss at time of discontinuation vs continued patients by subgroups
################################################################################ 

Data Prep
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Assign 6 month weight change vars (attrited vs completed patients)

## The total count will be lower than the full study sample
## Some participants who completed follow-up (no drug disc) have incomplete 6m weight data
## This is a complete case analysis, hence the lower sample size

data_attr_wt <- copy(data_wide)

# Check for discontinued patients with no data_attr_wt (should be 0)
data_attr_wt_chk <- data_attr_wt[attr_ever_yn == 1 & is.na(attr_wt)]

# Treatment status factor
data_attr_wt[
  ,
  treat_status := factor(
    fifelse(attr_ever_yn == 1L, "Discontinued", "On Treatment"),
    levels = c("On Treatment", "Discontinued")
  )
]

# Absolute weight loss (kg)
data_attr_wt[
  ,
  wt_loss_abs := fifelse(
    attr_ever_yn == 1L,
    attr_wt - wt_kg_0m,
    wt_kg_6m - wt_kg_0m
  )
]

# Percent weight loss (%)
data_attr_wt[, wt_loss_pct := 100 * wt_loss_abs / wt_kg_0m]

# Remove missing values
data_attr_wt <- data_attr_wt[!is.na(wt_loss_abs) & !is.na(wt_loss_pct)]

#-----------------------------#
# Summary stats for mean weight loss stratified by discontinuation

data_discontfu <- data_attr_wt[attr_ever_yn == 1]
data_completefu <- data_attr_wt[attr_ever_yn == 0]

# Mean weight loss in discontinued
sum_stats(data_discontfu$wt_loss_abs)
sum_stats(data_discontfu$wt_loss_pct)

# Mean weight loss in completed
sum_stats(data_completefu$wt_loss_abs)
sum_stats(data_completefu$wt_loss_pct)

# Weight loss targets for discontinued participants
mean(data_discontfu$wt_loss_pct <= -5) * 100
mean(data_discontfu$wt_loss_pct <= -10) * 100
mean(data_discontfu$wt_loss_pct <= -15) * 100
mean(data_discontfu$wt_loss_pct <= -20) * 100

```

a) Overall absolute weight loss at 6 months
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Overall absolute weight loss at 6m

sfig5a_wtloss_attr <-
  ggplot(
    data_attr_wt,
    aes(x = treat_status, y = wt_loss_abs, fill = treat_status)
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.65,
    outlier.alpha = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(NA, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight Loss (kg) by 6 Months",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Weight Loss (kg)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "plain", size = 12),
    legend.position  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

sfig5a_wtloss_attr

```

b) Overall % weight loss at 6m
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Overall percentage weight loss at 6m

sfig5b_wtloss_attr <-
  ggplot(
    data_attr_wt,
    aes(x = treat_status, y = wt_loss_pct, fill = treat_status)
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.65,
    outlier.alpha = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(NA, NA),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight Loss (%) by 6 Months",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Weight Loss (%)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "plain", size = 12),
    legend.position  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

sfig5b_wtloss_attr

```

c) Weight loss thresholds stratified by attrition
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Weight loss by clinical thresholds

#-----------------------------#
# Data Prep (note that weight loss is negative for loss)

thresholds <- c(5, 10, 15)

tab_thresh <- rbindlist(lapply(thresholds, function(t) {

  cutpoint <- -t  # e.g., t=10 => wt_loss_pct <= -10 means achieved ≥10% loss

  data_attr_wt[
    ,
    .(
      n_total    = .N,
      n_achieved = sum(wt_loss_pct <= cutpoint, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    by = treat_status
  ][
    ,
    `:=`(
      threshold = paste0("≥", t, "%"),
      pct  = 100 * n_achieved / n_total,
      frac = sprintf("%d/%d\n%.1f%%", n_achieved, n_total, 100 * n_achieved / n_total)
    )
  ]
}))

tab_thresh[
  ,
  threshold := factor(threshold, levels = paste0("≥", thresholds, "%"))
]

#-----------------------------#
# Plotting

# Custom x spacing (tight within threshold, gap between thresholds)

status_levels <- levels(tab_thresh$treat_status)
within_step  <- 0.9
between_gap  <- 0.5

status_pos <- setNames(seq_along(status_levels) - 1, status_levels)

tab_thresh[
  ,
  thresh_idx := as.integer(threshold)
]

tab_thresh[
  ,
  x_pos := (thresh_idx - 1) * (within_step * length(status_levels) + between_gap) +
    unname(status_pos[as.character(treat_status)]) * within_step
]

x_ticks <- unique(
  tab_thresh[
    order(thresh_idx, treat_status),
    .(x_pos, x_lab = paste0(as.character(treat_status), "\n", as.character(threshold)))
  ]
)

tab_thresh[, lab_y := pct + 1.5]

sfig5c_wtloss_attr <-
  ggplot(tab_thresh, aes(x = x_pos, y = pct, fill = treat_status)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(aes(label = frac, y = lab_y), size = 3, vjust = 0) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = x_ticks$x_pos,
    labels = x_ticks$x_lab,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.10))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight Loss Threshold Achievement by 6 Months",
    x = "Treatment Status by Threshold",
    y = "Patients Achieving Threshold (%)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.95),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

sfig5c_wtloss_attr

```

d) Combine plots
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine panels

# Top row: (a) absolute kg, (b) percent
# Bottom row: (c) thresholds spanning full width


sfig5_wtloss_attr <-
  (sfig5a_wtloss_attr | sfig5b_wtloss_attr) /
  sfig5c_wtloss_attr +
  plot_layout(
    widths  = c(1, 1),
    heights = c(1, 1.25)
  ) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &   # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0, 0.985)      # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )

sfig5_wtloss_attr


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export

# PNG
ggsave(
  sfig5_wtloss_attr,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig5-wtloss-attr.png",
  width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  sfig5_wtloss_attr,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig5-wtloss-attr.svg",
  width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  sfig5_wtloss_attr,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig5-wtloss-attr.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig6. Average dosage trajectories subgroup stratified
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mean dose trajectory plots (subgroup stratified) — 4 panels total

# Original Layout: 2 columns (Mounjaro | Wegovy) x 4 rows (WtGrp, Age, IMD, Eth)
# Each panel = main mean-dose line plot + risk table underneath (like Fig4)

# Wegovy has now been removed, hence 2 top 2 bottom layout instead


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build weekly dose-long with subgroup vars (same as before)

dose_weekly <- data[, .(
  patid, glp1_idx,
  mounjaro_mg_locf_num,
  wegovy_mg_locf_num,
  age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat
)]

dose_long <- rbindlist(list(
  dose_weekly[!is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
    .(patid, glp1_idx, drug = "Mounjaro", dose_mg = mounjaro_mg_locf_num,
      age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat)
  ],
  dose_weekly[!is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
    .(patid, glp1_idx, drug = "Wegovy", dose_mg = wegovy_mg_locf_num,
      age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat)
  ]
), use.names = TRUE)

setorder(dose_long, drug, glp1_idx)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions (p-value at week 26 via partial F-test + panel builder)

get_pal <- function(k) {
  if (!exists("pal_custom_5")) stop("pal_custom_5 not found.")
  pal <- pal_custom_5
  if (length(pal) < k) pal <- rep(pal, length.out = k)
  pal[seq_len(k)]
}

pf_dose_26 <- function(dt, subgroup_var) {
  d26 <- dt[glp1_idx == 26 & !is.na(get(subgroup_var)) & is.finite(dose_mg)]
  if (nrow(d26) < 3) return(NA_real_)
  if (uniqueN(d26[[subgroup_var]]) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  lm0 <- lm(dose_mg ~ 1, data = d26)
  lm1 <- lm(as.formula(paste0("dose_mg ~ ", subgroup_var)), data = d26)
  as.numeric(anova(lm0, lm1)$`Pr(>F)`[2])
}

make_dose_panel <- function(dt, subgroup_var, subgroup_lab, pal_vec,
                            panel_title, show_x = FALSE) {

  # Preserve factor ordering if present
  if (is.factor(dt[[subgroup_var]])) {
    levs <- levels(dt[[subgroup_var]])
  } else {
    levs <- sort(unique(dt[[subgroup_var]]))
  }
  dt[, (subgroup_var) := factor(get(subgroup_var), levels = levs)]

  # Summaries
  summ <- dt[
    !is.na(get(subgroup_var)) & is.finite(dose_mg),
    .(mean_dose = mean(dose_mg, na.rm = TRUE), n = uniqueN(patid)),
    by = .(glp1_idx, grp = get(subgroup_var))
  ][order(glp1_idx, grp)]

  # p-value
  p <- pf_dose_26(dt, subgroup_var)

  # Main plot
  p_main <- ggplot(
    summ,
    aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean_dose, colour = grp, group = grp)
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
    labs(
      x = if (show_x) "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation" else NULL,
      y = "Mean Dose (mg)",
      title = panel_title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "P for difference between ", tolower(subgroup_lab), " groups at 6 months: ",
        ifelse(is.na(p), "NA", format_pval(p))
      ),
      colour = subgroup_lab
    ) +
    fig_theme +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.4)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal_vec)

  # Risk table
  x_breaks <- seq(0, 26, by = 2)
  risk_df  <- summ[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]
  
  levs2  <- levels(risk_df$grp)
  sep_df <- data.frame(y = seq_along(levs2) + 0.5)
  
  p_risk <- ggplot(
    risk_df,
    aes(x = glp1_idx, y = grp, label = n)
  ) +
    geom_text(size = 3.5) +
    geom_hline(
      data = sep_df,
      aes(yintercept = y),
      colour = "grey85",
      linewidth = 0.3
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
    scale_y_discrete(
      limits = rev(levs2),
      labels = if (subgroup_var == "area_imd_qt") {
        c("1 (Most Deprived)" = "1 (Most Depr.)",
          "2" = "2",
          "3" = "3",
          "4" = "4",
          "5 (Least Deprived)" = "5 (Least Depr.)")
      } else {
        waiver()
      }
    ) +
    theme_void(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
      plot.margin  = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 5))
    ) +
    labs(title = "Number of Patients")

  p_main / p_risk + plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1))
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build the 8 panels (4 rows x 2 cols)

build_4_for_drug <- function(drug_name, show_x_last_row = TRUE) {
  dt <- dose_long[drug == drug_name]

  # palettes sized to each subgroup
  pal_wtgrp <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$wt_kg_0m_cat))))
  pal_age   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$age_cat5))))
  pal_imd   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$area_imd_qt))))
  pal_eth   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$eth_bin))))

  p1 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(wt_kg_0m_cat)],
    "wt_kg_0m_cat", "Baseline Weight", pal_wtgrp,
    panel_title = paste0(
      #drug_name,  # drug name removed as only mounjaro showed (add ": " if needed)
      "Stratified by Baseline Weight"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p2 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(age_cat5)],
    "age_cat5", "Age Group", pal_age,
    panel_title = paste0(
      #drug_name,  # drug name removed as only mounjaro showed (add ": " if needed)
      "Stratified by Age Group"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p3 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(area_imd_qt)],
    "area_imd_qt", "IMD Quintile", pal_imd,
    panel_title = paste0(
      #drug_name,  # drug name removed as only mounjaro showed (add ": " if needed)
      "Stratified by IMD Quintiles"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p4 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(eth_bin)],
    "eth_bin", "Ethnicity", pal_eth,
    panel_title = paste0(
      #drug_name,  # drug name removed as only mounjaro showed (add ": " if needed)
      "Stratified by Ethnicity"),
    show_x = show_x_last_row
  )

  list(wtgrp = p1, age = p2, imd = p3, eth = p4)
}

m_pan <- build_4_for_drug("Mounjaro", show_x_last_row = TRUE)
w_pan <- build_4_for_drug("Wegovy",   show_x_last_row = TRUE)

# Reference code for plotting mounjaro AND wegovy (now retired)
# Row-wise (2 columns, 4 rows): (M | W) stacked vertically
#sfig6_dose_subgrp_core <-
#  (m_pan$wtgrp | w_pan$wtgrp) /
#  (m_pan$age   | w_pan$age)   /
#  (m_pan$imd   | w_pan$imd)   /
#  (m_pan$eth   | w_pan$eth) +
#  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1, 1)) +
#  plot_annotation(
#    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16))
#  )

# 2x2 grid (Mounjaro ONLY)
sfig6_dose_subgrp <-
  ((wrap_elements(m_pan$wtgrp) | wrap_elements(m_pan$age)) /
   (wrap_elements(m_pan$imd)   | wrap_elements(m_pan$eth))) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &  # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.tag.position = c(0.05, 0.955)    # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )

# Shared y-axis label like Fig4 (removed now due to alphabetical labels)
#sfig6_dose_subgrp <- ggdraw(sfig6_dose_subgrp_core) +
#  draw_label(
#    "Mean Dose (mg)",
#    angle = 90,
#    x = 0.01, y = 0.5, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
#    size = 12
#  )

#plot(sfig6_dose_subgrp_core)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export

# PNG
ggsave(
  sfig6_dose_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig6-dose-subgrp.png",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  sfig6_dose_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig6-dose-subgrp.svg",
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  sfig6_dose_subgrp,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig6-dose-subgrp.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# SFig7. Sankey plot of HbA1c diabetes evolution
################################################################################ 

Reference Table for Diabetic Group Transitions
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create transition matrix table for HbA1c diabetic categories

#-----------------------------#
# NAs included

cat_order <- c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic", "Normal", "Missing")

sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl <- data_wide[
  ,
  .N,
  by = .(
    hba1c_cat_0m = factor(fcoalesce(as.character(hba1c_cat_0m), "Missing"), levels = cat_order),
    hba1c_cat_6m = factor(fcoalesce(as.character(hba1c_cat_6m), "Missing"), levels = cat_order)
  )
]

sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl <- dcast(
  sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl,
  hba1c_cat_0m ~ hba1c_cat_6m,
  value.var = "N",
  fill = 0
)

setcolorder(sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl, c("hba1c_cat_0m", cat_order))

# Add row total
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl[, row_total := rowSums(.SD), .SDcols = cat_order]

# Convert count columns to character so assignment of strings is legal
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl[, (cat_order) := lapply(.SD, as.character), .SDcols = cat_order]

# Now overwrite with "n (row%)"
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl[
  row_total > 0,
  (cat_order) := Map(
    function(x) sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", as.integer(x), 100 * as.integer(x) / row_total),
    .SD
  ),
  .SDcols = cat_order
]

sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl

#-----------------------------#
# NAs excluded (skipped counts of missing values)

# Redefine order with missing label removed
cat_order_cc <- c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic", "Normal")

# Only keep complete case sample here
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl <- data_wide[
  !is.na(hba1c_cat_0m) & !is.na(hba1c_cat_6m),
  .N,
  by = .(
    hba1c_cat_0m = factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_0m), levels = cat_order_cc),
    hba1c_cat_6m = factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_6m), levels = cat_order_cc)
  )
]

sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl <- dcast(
  sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl,
  hba1c_cat_0m ~ hba1c_cat_6m,
  value.var = "N",
  fill = 0
)

setcolorder(sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl, c("hba1c_cat_0m", cat_order_cc))

# Add row total
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl[, row_total := rowSums(.SD), .SDcols = cat_order_cc]

# Convert count columns to character so assignment of strings is legal
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl[, (cat_order_cc) := lapply(.SD, as.character), .SDcols = cat_order_cc]

# Now overwrite with "n (row%)"
sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl[
  row_total > 0,
  (cat_order_cc) := Map(
    function(x) sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", as.integer(x), 100 * as.integer(x) / row_total),
    .SD
  ),
  .SDcols = cat_order_cc
]

sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

# Table with NAs included
fwrite(
  sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_incl,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig7extra-tab-hba1c-diab-trans-na-incl.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

# Table with NAs excluded
fwrite(
  sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl,
  file = "Final Outputs/sfig7extra-tab-hba1c-diab-trans-na-excl.csv",
  bom = TRUE, quote = TRUE
)

```

Sankey plot
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Data Prep

# Copy dataset
tab_sankey <- copy(sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl)

# Pivot wide to long
links <- melt(
  tab_sankey,
  id.vars = "hba1c_cat_0m",
  measure.vars = c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic", "Normal"),
  variable.name = "hba1c_cat_6m",
  value.name = "cell"
)

# Extract counts + set ordering (keep ALL 3 levels)
lvl_hba1c <- c("Normal", "Pre-Diabetic", "Diabetic")

links[
  ,
  `:=`(
    n = as.integer(sub("^([0-9]+).*", "\\1", cell)),
    hba1c_cat_0m = factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_0m), levels = lvl_hba1c),
    hba1c_cat_6m = factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_6m), levels = lvl_hba1c)
  )
]

# Remove empty / zero flows
links <- links[!is.na(n) & n > 0]

# Restrict to Diabetes or Pre-Diabetes at baseline
links_sub <- links[hba1c_cat_0m %in% c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic")]
links_sub[, hba1c_cat_0m := factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_0m), levels = lvl_hba1c)]
links_sub[, hba1c_cat_6m := factor(as.character(hba1c_cat_6m), levels = lvl_hba1c)]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Sankey (alluvial) plot

sfig7_sankey_diab_evol <-
  ggplot(
    links_sub,
    aes(axis1 = hba1c_cat_0m, axis2 = hba1c_cat_6m, y = n)
  ) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = hba1c_cat_0m),
    width = 1/10,
    alpha = 0.55,
    reverse = FALSE,
    decreasing = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_stratum(
    aes(fill = after_stat(stratum)),
    width = 1/10,
    color = NA,
    alpha = 0.8,
    reverse = FALSE,
    decreasing = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 2.5,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Baseline", "6 months"),
    expand = c(.05, .05),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.03, 0))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_sankey_3, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "HbA1c Category") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.background  = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

sfig7_sankey_diab_evol


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Convert NA-excluded crosstab into a grob table

# Copy table (NA-excluded crosstab)
tab_trans_cc <- copy(sfig7_tab_hba1c_diab_trans_na_excl)

# Ensure consistent column order
cat_order_cc <- c("Diabetic", "Pre-Diabetic", "Normal")
setcolorder(tab_trans_cc, c("hba1c_cat_0m", cat_order_cc, "row_total"))

# Optional: format totals with commas (counts)
tab_trans_cc[, row_total := as.integer(row_total)]
tab_trans_cc[, (cat_order_cc) := lapply(.SD, as.character), .SDcols = cat_order_cc]
tab_trans_cc[, row_total := formatC(row_total, format = "d", big.mark = ",")]

# Build gt table with clear Baseline vs 6 Months labelling
sfig7_trans_tab <-
  gt(tab_trans_cc, rowname_col = "hba1c_cat_0m") |>
  tab_header(title = md("HbA1c Transitions: N (row %)")) |>
  tab_stubhead(label = "Baseline") |>
  cols_label(
    Diabetic       = "Diabetic",
    `Pre-Diabetic` = "Pre-Diabetic",
    Normal         = "Normal",
    row_total      = "Total"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(label = "6 Months", columns = all_of(cat_order_cc)) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = stub()) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(11), 
    heading.title.font.size = px(12), 
    column_labels.font.size = px(11), 
    row_group.font.size = px(11), 
    # Increase vertical spacing
    data_row.padding = px(9),          # main row spacing
    column_labels.padding = px(9),     # space above/below column headers
    heading.padding = px(9),           # space around title
    table_body.hlines.width = px(0.5)
  )

sfig7_trans_tab

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Stack sankey + table

# Convert gt -> grob (gtable)
tab_grob <- gt::as_gtable(sfig7_trans_tab)

# Remove outer left/right padding columns of the gtable
tab_grob$widths[1] <- unit(0, "pt")
tab_grob$widths[length(tab_grob$widths)] <- unit(0, "pt")

# Force the gtable to behave like a flexible layout that can expand
tab_grob$widths <- unit(rep(1, length(tab_grob$widths)), "null")

# Put the grob onto a full-width ggplot canvas
tab_plot <- ggdraw() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)) +
  draw_grob(
    tab_grob,
    x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1,
    hjust = 0, vjust = 0
  )

# Tighten vertical gap between Sankey (top) and table (bottom)
sankey_tight <- sfig7_sankey_diab_evol +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0))

tab_plot_tight <- tab_plot +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0))

# Stack Sankey + table (now the table will fill the width)
sfig7_sankey_diab_evol_full <-
  sfig7_sankey_diab_evol /
  tab_plot +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.35)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") &  # adds a, b, c, d
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.tag.position = c(0.01, 0.995)    # top left, manually change height as necessary
  )


sfig7_sankey_diab_evol_full


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export

# PNG
ggsave(
  sfig7_sankey_diab_evol_full,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig7-sankey-diab-evol.png",
  width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300
)

# SVG
ggsave(
  sfig7_sankey_diab_evol_full,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig7-sankey-diab-evol.svg",
  width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300
)

# PDF
ggsave(
  sfig7_sankey_diab_evol_full,
  filename = "Final Outputs/sfig7-sankey-diab-evol.pdf",
  device = cairo_pdf,
  width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# LFig1. Dosing schedule stacked bar chart
################################################################################ 

Data Prep
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create dosing schedule plot dataset

#-----------------------------#
# Dataset creation

# Check dosing categories
table(data$mounjaro_mg, useNA = "always")
table(data$wegovy_mg, useNA = "always")

# Keep relevant columns
data_dose <- data[, .(
  patid, glp1_idx, attr_status,
  glp1_drug_locf, mounjaro_mg_locf_num, wegovy_mg_locf_num
)]

# Unify prescription columns into plot dataset
rx_long <- rbindlist(list(

  data_dose[!is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
       .(
         patid,
         glp1_idx,
         glp1_drug_locf = "Mounjaro",
         dose = mounjaro_mg_locf_num
       )],

  data_dose[!is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
       .(
         patid,
         glp1_idx,
         glp1_drug_locf = "Wegovy",
         dose = wegovy_mg_locf_num
       )]

), use.names = TRUE)

# Ensure correct temporal ordering
setorder(rx_long, patid, glp1_idx)

#-----------------------------#
# Collapse weekly dose to monthly dose

# Set month weeks (13 wks = 3 months, 26 weks = 6 months)
month_weeks <- c(0, 4, 8, 13, 17, 21, 26)

# Create monthly snapshots for mounjaro
mounjaro_monthly <- data_dose[
  glp1_idx %in% month_weeks & !is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
  .(patid, month = glp1_idx, drug = "Mounjaro", dose_mg = mounjaro_mg_locf_num)
]

# Create monthly snapshots for wegovy
wegovy_monthly <- data_dose[
  glp1_idx %in% month_weeks & !is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
  .(patid, month = glp1_idx, drug = "Wegovy", dose_mg = wegovy_mg_locf_num)
]

# Combine into monthly dose dataset
dose_monthly <- rbindlist(list(mounjaro_monthly, wegovy_monthly), use.names = TRUE)
```

Plot stacked bar charts
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create dosing schedule stacked bar chart

#-----------------------------#
# Create % distribution dataset

# % of patients at each dose per month by mounjaro and wegovy
dose_monthly_pct <- dose_monthly[
  ,
  .N,
  by = .(drug, month, dose_mg)
][
  ,
  pct := 100 * N / sum(N),
  by = .(drug, month)
]

# Relabel months
month_labs <- c(
  "0"  = "Baseline",
  "4"  = "1 Month",
  "8"  = "2 Months",
  "13" = "3 Months",
  "17" = "4 Months",
  "21" = "5 Months",
  "26" = "6 Months"
)
dose_monthly_pct[, month_lab := factor(month, levels = month_weeks, labels = month_labs)]

# Make dose a factor so stacking order is stable (low to high)
dose_monthly_pct[, dose_mg := factor(dose_mg, levels = sort(unique(dose_mg)))]

# Create a combined fill label
dose_monthly_pct[, dose_drug := paste0(as.character(dose_mg), " mg")]

#-----------------------------#
# Split tables by drug

# Split tables
dose_mounjaro <- dose_monthly_pct[drug == "Mounjaro"]
dose_wegovy   <- dose_monthly_pct[drug == "Wegovy"]

# Ensure stable stacking order within each drug
dose_mounjaro[, dose_mg := factor(dose_mg, levels = sort(unique(dose_mg)))]
dose_wegovy[,   dose_mg := factor(dose_mg, levels = sort(unique(dose_mg)))]

# As dose_drug already encodes dose, dose_mg can be used directly for fill
dose_mounjaro[, dose_lab := paste0(as.character(dose_mg), " mg")]
dose_wegovy[,   dose_lab := paste0(as.character(dose_mg), " mg")]

dose_mounjaro[, dose_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = unique(dose_lab[order(as.numeric(as.character(dose_mg)))]))]
dose_wegovy[,   dose_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = unique(dose_lab[order(as.numeric(as.character(dose_mg)))]))]


#-----------------------------#
# Plot Mounjaro

p_mounjaro <- ggplot(
  dose_mounjaro,
  aes(x = month_lab, y = pct, fill = dose_lab)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Mounjaro",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Patients (%)",
    fill = "Dose"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.06))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe2_6) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p_mounjaro

#-----------------------------#
# Plot Wegovy

p_wegovy <- ggplot(
  dose_wegovy,
  aes(x = month_lab, y = pct, fill = dose_lab)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Wegovy",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,          # avoid repeating y-label on right
    fill = "Dose"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.06))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_5) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p_wegovy

#-----------------------------#
# Create patient count tables (mounjaro)

# Choose the month order from existing factor
month_levels <- levels(dose_mounjaro$month_lab)

# Aggregate counts by month x dose (you already have N, but sum defensively)
tab_ptcount_mounjaro <- dose_mounjaro[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(month_lab, dose_lab)
]

# Ensure stable ordering
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels)]
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, dose_lab  := factor(dose_lab,  levels = levels(dose_mounjaro$dose_lab))]

# add a Total N row
tab_total_mounjaro <- dose_mounjaro[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = month_lab
][
  , dose_lab := "Total"
]

tab_ptcount_mounjaro <- rbind(tab_total_mounjaro, tab_ptcount_mounjaro, fill = TRUE)

# Label formatting
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, label := fifelse(is.na(N), "", formatC(N, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","))]

# For plotting as a grid of text, we want rows = dose, cols = month
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, row_lab := as.character(dose_lab)]
tab_ptcount_mounjaro[, row_lab := factor(row_lab, levels = c("Total", levels(dose_mounjaro$dose_lab)))]

#-----------------------------#
# Create patient count tables (wegovy)

# Choose the month order from existing factor
month_levels <- levels(dose_wegovy$month_lab)

# Aggregate counts by month x dose (you already have N, but sum defensively)
tab_ptcount_wegovy <- dose_wegovy[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = .(month_lab, dose_lab)
]

# Ensure stable ordering
tab_ptcount_wegovy[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels)]
tab_ptcount_wegovy[, dose_lab  := factor(dose_lab,  levels = levels(dose_wegovy$dose_lab))]

# add a Total N row
tab_total_wegovy <- dose_wegovy[
  , .(N = sum(N, na.rm = TRUE)),
  by = month_lab
][
  , dose_lab := "Total"
]

tab_ptcount_wegovy <- rbind(tab_total_wegovy, tab_ptcount_wegovy, fill = TRUE)

# Label formatting
tab_ptcount_wegovy[, label := fifelse(is.na(N), "", formatC(N, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","))]

# For plotting as a grid of text, we want rows = dose, cols = month
tab_ptcount_wegovy[, row_lab := as.character(dose_lab)]
tab_ptcount_wegovy[, row_lab := factor(row_lab, levels = c("Total", levels(dose_wegovy$dose_lab)))]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create mean dose and range tables 

# Add month_lab onto the patient-level monthly dataset
dose_monthly[, month_lab := factor(month, levels = month_weeks, labels = month_labs)]

# Choose same month ordering as the plots
month_levels_m <- levels(dose_mounjaro$month_lab)
month_levels_w <- levels(dose_wegovy$month_lab)

#-----------------------------#
# Mounjaro: mean + range dose per month

tab_meandose_mounjaro <- dose_monthly[drug == "Mounjaro",
  .(
    mean_dose = mean(dose_mg, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_dose  = min(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE),
    max_dose  = max(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = month_lab
]

tab_meandose_mounjaro[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels_m)]

# Create two display rows
tab_meandose_mounjaro_long <- rbindlist(list(

  tab_meandose_mounjaro[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Mean dose (mg)",
      label = ifelse(is.na(mean_dose), "", sprintf("%.1f", mean_dose))
    )
  ],

  tab_meandose_mounjaro[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Range (mg)",
      label = ifelse(
        is.na(min_dose) | is.na(max_dose),
        "",
        sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", min_dose, max_dose)
      )
    )
  ]

))

tab_meandose_mounjaro_long[
  ,
  row_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = c("Mean dose (mg)", "Range (mg)"))
]

#-----------------------------#
# Wegovy: mean + range dose per month

tab_meandose_wegovy <- dose_monthly[drug == "Wegovy",
  .(
    mean_dose = mean(dose_mg, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_dose  = min(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE),
    max_dose  = max(dose_mg,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = month_lab
]

tab_meandose_wegovy[, month_lab := factor(month_lab, levels = month_levels_w)]

tab_meandose_wegovy_long <- rbindlist(list(

  tab_meandose_wegovy[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Mean dose (mg)",
      label = ifelse(is.na(mean_dose), "", sprintf("%.1f", mean_dose))
    )
  ],

  tab_meandose_wegovy[
    ,
    .(
      month_lab,
      dose_lab = "Range (mg)",
      label = ifelse(
        is.na(min_dose) | is.na(max_dose),
        "",
        sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", min_dose, max_dose)
      )
    )
  ]

))

tab_meandose_wegovy_long[
  ,
  row_lab := factor(dose_lab, levels = c("Mean dose (mg)", "Range (mg)"))
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine plots

# Assign shared axis labels as grobs
x_lab <- wrap_elements(
  full = textGrob(
    "Months Since GLP-1 Initiation",
    gp = gpar(fontsize = 11)
  )
)

# Create mean dose table block (mounjaro)
p_mounjaro_meandose <- make_count_table(
  tab_meandose_mounjaro_long,
  title = "Dose Summary by Month"
) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create mean dose table block (wegovy)
p_wegovy_meandose <- make_count_table(
  tab_meandose_wegovy_long,
  title = "Dose Summary by Month"
) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create patient count table block (mounjaro)
p_mounjaro_counts <- make_count_table(
  tab_ptcount_mounjaro,
  title = "Patient Counts by Dose"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create patient count table block (wegovy)
p_wegovy_counts <- make_count_table(
  tab_ptcount_wegovy,
  title = "Patient Counts by Dose"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = 6))
  )

# Create internal padding within blocks (three layer limitation)
pad_tbl <- theme(
  plot.margin = margin(t = 6, r = 0, b = 6, l = 0)
)

p_mounjaro_meandose <- p_mounjaro_meandose + pad_tbl
p_mounjaro_counts   <- p_mounjaro_counts   + pad_tbl

p_wegovy_meandose <- p_wegovy_meandose + pad_tbl
p_wegovy_counts   <- p_wegovy_counts   + pad_tbl

# Assign blocks
mounjaro_block <- p_mounjaro / p_mounjaro_meandose / p_mounjaro_counts +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.10, 0.25))

wegovy_block <- p_wegovy / p_wegovy_meandose / p_wegovy_counts +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.10, 0.25))


# Assemble with an extra bottom row + extra left column
lfig1_dose <-
  (mounjaro_block | wegovy_block) / x_lab +
  plot_layout(
    widths  = c(0.02, 1),
    heights = c(1, 0.06)
  )

lfig1_dose


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export plot

ggsave(
  lfig1_dose, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/lfig1-dose.png",
  width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# LFig2. Attrition by side effect count, severity, and care package use
################################################################################ 

Treatment discontinuation among participants experiencing side effects,
stratified by side-effect count (0, 1, 2, 3+). 

Panels are stratified by care package use and by the worse side effect experienced 
(mild, moderate, or severe side effects). 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create bar chart dataset (wide by patient) + side-effect count variables

data_sdeff_bar <- data_wide[
  , .(
    patid,
    attr_wk, attr_ever_yn, attr_status,

    # Side effect summary / severity
    sdeff_total_n,
    sdeff_mild_n,
    sdeff_moderate_n,
    sdeff_severe_n,

    # Ever indicators by severity
    sdeff_mild_ever,
    sdeff_moderate_ever,
    sdeff_severe_ever,
    
    # Most severe side effect
    most_severe_sdeff,

    # Care pack
    sdeff_carepack_ever
  )
]

# Assign 26 to NA in attr_wk (max follow-up)
data_sdeff_bar[is.na(attr_wk) & attr_status == FALSE, attr_wk := 26]

# Ensure side-effect counts exist (treat missing as zero for "Overall"/"0" group)
data_sdeff_bar[is.na(sdeff_total_n),    sdeff_total_n    := 0L]
data_sdeff_bar[is.na(sdeff_mild_n),     sdeff_mild_n     := 0L]
data_sdeff_bar[is.na(sdeff_moderate_n), sdeff_moderate_n := 0L]
data_sdeff_bar[is.na(sdeff_severe_n),   sdeff_severe_n   := 0L]

# 6-month discontinuation indicator (event occurs by week 26)
data_sdeff_bar[
  ,
  disc_6m := (attr_ever_yn == 1 & attr_wk <= 26)
]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Plot discontinuation % by side-effect count (0 / 1 / 2 / 3+) and care pack

# 4 subplots: Overall, ≥1 mild, ≥1 mild or moderate, ≥1 mild/mod/severe

# Ensure stable ordering of panels
panel_levels <- c(
  "Overall",
  "Mild (Most Severe)",
  "Moderate (Most Severe)",
  "Severe (Most Severe)"
)

# Ensure stable ordering of count bands
count_levels4 <- c("0", "1", "2", "3+")

# Build long-format dataset for the 4 panels
dt_overall <- copy(data_sdeff_bar)
dt_overall[, panel := "Overall"]
dt_overall[, sdeff_n_panel := sdeff_total_n]

dt_mild <- copy(data_sdeff_bar)[most_severe_sdeff == "Mild"]
dt_mild[, panel := "Mild (Most Severe)"]
dt_mild[, sdeff_n_panel := sdeff_total_n]

dt_mild_mod <- copy(data_sdeff_bar)[most_severe_sdeff == "Moderate"]
dt_mild_mod[, panel := "Moderate (Most Severe)"]
dt_mild_mod[, sdeff_n_panel := sdeff_total_n]

dt_any <- copy(data_sdeff_bar)[most_severe_sdeff == "Severe"]
dt_any[, panel := "Severe (Most Severe)"]
dt_any[, sdeff_n_panel := sdeff_total_n]

dt_plot <- rbindlist(list(dt_overall, dt_mild, dt_mild_mod, dt_any), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Helper: 4-level count category (0, 1, 2, 3+)
count_cat4 <- function(n) {
  data.table::fcase(
    n <= 0, "0",
    n <= 1, "1",
    n <= 2, "2",
    default = "3+"
  )
}

# Count category + labels
dt_plot[
  ,
  sdeff_count_cat4 := factor(count_cat4(sdeff_n_panel), levels = count_levels4)
]

dt_plot[
  ,
  sdeff_carepack_ever_lab := factor(
    sdeff_carepack_ever,
    levels = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("No Care Package Use", "Any Care Package Use")
  )
]

dt_plot[
  ,
  panel := factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
]

# Summarise to % discontinuation by count x care pack within panel
dt_sum <- dt_plot[
  ,
  .(
    n      = .N,
    n_disc = sum(attr_ever_yn, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(panel, sdeff_count_cat4, sdeff_carepack_ever_lab)
][
  , disc_pct := n_disc / n
]

# Bar labels
dt_sum[
  ,
  lab := paste0(n_disc, "/", n)
]

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Remove "0" side-effect count from subgroup panels

dt_sum_plot <- copy(dt_sum)

# Drop 0 rows for all panels except the Overall panel
dt_sum_plot <- dt_sum_plot[!(panel != "Overall" & sdeff_count_cat4 == "0")]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Helper function: build a single panel plot to patchwork layout

bar_panel <- function(d, title, show_xlab = TRUE, show_ylab = TRUE, drop_x = FALSE) {

  # Keep dodge behaviour consistent even if one group is missing (e.g., 0 side effects)
  pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.75, preserve = "single")

  ggplot(
    d,
    aes(x = sdeff_count_cat4, y = disc_pct, fill = sdeff_carepack_ever_lab)
  ) +
    geom_col(position = pd, width = 0.65) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = lab),
      position = pd,
      vjust = -0.35,
      size = 3
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = drop_x) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
      limits = c(0, NA),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.06))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = if (show_xlab) "Side-Effect Count During Follow-up" else NULL,
      y = if (show_ylab) "Treatment Discontinuation (%)" else NULL,
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    fig_theme +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "plain", size = 12, hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 8)),
      axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal_custom_2)
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Build the four plots
# Overall keeps "0"; subgroup panels drop it

p_overall <- bar_panel(
  dt_sum_plot[panel == "Overall"],
  title     = "Overall",
  show_xlab = TRUE,
  show_ylab = TRUE,
  drop_x    = FALSE
)

p_mild <- bar_panel(
  dt_sum_plot[panel == "Mild (Most Severe)"],
  title     = "Maximum Side Effect Severity: Mild",
  show_xlab = FALSE,
  show_ylab = FALSE,
  drop_x    = TRUE
)

p_mild_mod <- bar_panel(
  dt_sum_plot[panel == "Moderate (Most Severe)"],
  title     = "Maximum Side Effect Severity: Moderate",
  show_xlab = FALSE,
  show_ylab = FALSE,
  drop_x    = TRUE
)

p_any <- bar_panel(
  dt_sum_plot[panel == "Severe (Most Severe)"],
  title     = "Maximum Side Effect Severity: Severe",
  show_xlab = TRUE,
  show_ylab = FALSE,
  drop_x    = TRUE
)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Combine: Overall on the left, 3 stacked on the right

lfig2_sdeff_attr_bar <-
  (p_overall | (p_mild / p_mild_mod / p_any)) +
  patchwork::plot_layout(
    widths = c(1.15, 1),
    guides = "collect"
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

lfig2_sdeff_attr_bar


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export figure

ggsave(
  lfig2_sdeff_attr_bar, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/lfig2-sdeff-attr-bar.png",
  width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# LFig3. Average dosage trajectories subgroup stratified (Mounjaro and Wegovy)
################################################################################ 

```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mean dose trajectory plots (subgroup stratified) — 8 panels total

# Layout: 2 columns (Mounjaro | Wegovy) x 4 rows (WtGrp, Age, IMD, Eth)
# Each panel = main mean-dose line plot + risk table underneath (like Fig4)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build weekly dose-long with subgroup vars (same as before)

dose_weekly <- data[, .(
  patid, glp1_idx,
  mounjaro_mg_locf_num,
  wegovy_mg_locf_num,
  age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat
)]

dose_long <- rbindlist(list(
  dose_weekly[!is.na(mounjaro_mg_locf_num),
    .(patid, glp1_idx, drug = "Mounjaro", dose_mg = mounjaro_mg_locf_num,
      age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat)
  ],
  dose_weekly[!is.na(wegovy_mg_locf_num),
    .(patid, glp1_idx, drug = "Wegovy", dose_mg = wegovy_mg_locf_num,
      age_cat5, area_imd_qt, eth_bin, wt_kg_0m_cat)
  ]
), use.names = TRUE)

setorder(dose_long, drug, glp1_idx)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions (p-value at week 26 via partial F-test + panel builder)

get_pal <- function(k) {
  if (!exists("pal_custom_5")) stop("pal_custom_5 not found.")
  pal <- pal_custom_5
  if (length(pal) < k) pal <- rep(pal, length.out = k)
  pal[seq_len(k)]
}

pf_dose_26 <- function(dt, subgroup_var) {
  d26 <- dt[glp1_idx == 26 & !is.na(get(subgroup_var)) & is.finite(dose_mg)]
  if (nrow(d26) < 3) return(NA_real_)
  if (uniqueN(d26[[subgroup_var]]) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  lm0 <- lm(dose_mg ~ 1, data = d26)
  lm1 <- lm(as.formula(paste0("dose_mg ~ ", subgroup_var)), data = d26)
  as.numeric(anova(lm0, lm1)$`Pr(>F)`[2])
}

make_dose_panel <- function(dt, subgroup_var, subgroup_lab, pal_vec,
                            panel_title, show_x = FALSE) {

  # Preserve factor ordering if present
  if (is.factor(dt[[subgroup_var]])) {
    levs <- levels(dt[[subgroup_var]])
  } else {
    levs <- sort(unique(dt[[subgroup_var]]))
  }
  dt[, (subgroup_var) := factor(get(subgroup_var), levels = levs)]

  # Summaries
  summ <- dt[
    !is.na(get(subgroup_var)) & is.finite(dose_mg),
    .(mean_dose = mean(dose_mg, na.rm = TRUE), n = uniqueN(patid)),
    by = .(glp1_idx, grp = get(subgroup_var))
  ][order(glp1_idx, grp)]

  # p-value
  p <- pf_dose_26(dt, subgroup_var)

  # Main plot
  p_main <- ggplot(
    summ,
    aes(x = glp1_idx, y = mean_dose, colour = grp, group = grp)
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
    labs(
      x = if (show_x) "Weeks Since GLP-1 Initiation" else NULL,
      title = panel_title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "P for difference between ", tolower(subgroup_lab), " groups at 6 months: ",
        ifelse(is.na(p), "NA", format_pval(p))
      ),
      colour = subgroup_lab
    ) +
    fig_theme +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.4)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 26, by = 2)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal_vec)

  # Risk table
  x_breaks <- seq(0, 26, by = 2)
  risk_df  <- summ[glp1_idx %in% x_breaks]
  
  levs2  <- levels(risk_df$grp)
  sep_df <- data.frame(y = seq_along(levs2) + 0.5)
  
  p_risk <- ggplot(
    risk_df,
    aes(x = glp1_idx, y = grp, label = n)
  ) +
    geom_text(size = 3.5) +
    geom_hline(
      data = sep_df,
      aes(yintercept = y),
      colour = "grey85",
      linewidth = 0.3
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, limits = range(x_breaks)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levs2)) +
    theme_void(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, margin = margin(r = 12)),
      plot.margin  = margin(t = 6, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 5.5),
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, margin = margin(b = 5))
    ) +
    labs(title = "Number of Patients")

  p_main / p_risk + plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1))
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build the 8 panels (4 rows x 2 cols)

build_4_for_drug <- function(drug_name, show_x_last_row = TRUE) {
  dt <- dose_long[drug == drug_name]

  # palettes sized to each subgroup
  pal_wtgrp <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$wt_kg_0m_cat))))
  pal_age   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$age_cat5))))
  pal_imd   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$area_imd_qt))))
  pal_eth   <- get_pal(length(levels(factor(dt$eth_bin))))

  p1 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(wt_kg_0m_cat)],
    "wt_kg_0m_cat", "Baseline Weight", pal_wtgrp,
    panel_title = paste0(drug_name, ": Stratified by Baseline Weight"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p2 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(age_cat5)],
    "age_cat5", "Age Group", pal_age,
    panel_title = paste0(drug_name, ": Stratified by Age Group"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p3 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(area_imd_qt)],
    "area_imd_qt", "IMD Quintile", pal_imd,
    panel_title = paste0(drug_name, ": Stratified by IMD Quintiles"),
    show_x = FALSE
  )

  p4 <- make_dose_panel(
    dt[!is.na(eth_bin)],
    "eth_bin", "Ethnicity", pal_eth,
    panel_title = paste0(drug_name, ": Stratified by Ethnicity"),
    show_x = show_x_last_row
  )

  list(wtgrp = p1, age = p2, imd = p3, eth = p4)
}

m_pan <- build_4_for_drug("Mounjaro", show_x_last_row = TRUE)
w_pan <- build_4_for_drug("Wegovy",   show_x_last_row = TRUE)

# Row-wise (2 columns, 4 rows): (M | W) stacked vertically
lfig3_dose_subgrp_core <-
  (m_pan$wtgrp | w_pan$wtgrp) /
  (m_pan$age   | w_pan$age)   /
  (m_pan$imd   | w_pan$imd)   /
  (m_pan$eth   | w_pan$eth) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16))
  )

# Shared y-axis label like Fig4
lfig3_dose_subgrp_core <- ggdraw(lfig3_dose_subgrp_core) +
  draw_label(
    "Mean Dose (mg)",
    angle = 90,
    x = 0.01, y = 0.5, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
    size = 12
  )

plot(lfig3_dose_subgrp_core)


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export

ggsave(
  lfig3_dose_subgrp_core,
  filename = "Final Outputs/lfig3-dose-subgrp.png",
  width = 18, height = 25, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# LFig4. Side effect counts in discontinued vs continued patients
################################################################################ 

Data prep for cumulative attrition proportion by side effect count (time varying)
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Attrition proportion within subgroup by timepoint (glp1_idx)

data.table::setorder(data, patid, glp1_idx)

# Use valid follow-up only
dt_fu <- data[fu_valid == TRUE]

# Define subgroup at each timepoint from cumulative side effect count
dt_fu[
  ,
  sdeff_cum_band := fcase(
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 0L, "0",
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 1L, "1",
    sdeff_total_cum_n == 2L, "2",
    sdeff_total_cum_n >= 3L, "3+",
    default = NA_character_
  )
]

dt_fu[, sdeff_cum_band := factor(sdeff_cum_band, levels = c("0","1","2","3+"))]

# Build long attrition table: at each week, within each subgroup band
tab_attr_sdeff <- dt_fu[
  !is.na(sdeff_cum_band),
  .(
    n_total        = uniqueN(patid),
    n_attrited     = uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 1L]),
    n_not_attrited = uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 0L]),
    pct_attrited   = 100 * uniqueN(patid[attr_status == 1L]) / uniqueN(patid)
  ),
  by = .(glp1_idx, sdeff_cum_band)
][order(glp1_idx, sdeff_cum_band)]

# Create numerator/denominator string
tab_attr_sdeff[, frac := sprintf("%d/%d", n_attrited, n_total)]
tab_attr_sdeff[, frac_pct := sprintf("%d/%d\n%.1f%%", n_attrited, n_total, pct_attrited)]

tab_attr_sdeff[]

# Only keep counts at week 26 (6 months)
tab_attr_sdeff_6m <- tab_attr_sdeff[glp1_idx == 26]
tab_attr_sdeff_6m

```

Plot attrition proportion at 3m and 6m by side effect count
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Attrition % at 3m (wk 13) and 6m (wk 26) by cumulative side-effect band

# Convert to long format for plotting
tab_plot_6m <- melt(
  tab_attr_sdeff_6m,
  id.vars = "sdeff_cum_band",
  measure.vars = c("n_attrited", "n_not_attrited"),
  variable.name = "drop_status",
  value.name = "n"
)

# Clean labels
tab_plot_6m[
  ,
  drop_status := factor(
    drop_status,
    levels = c("n_not_attrited", "n_attrited"),
    labels = c("On Treatment", "Discontinued")
  )
]

# Ensure ordered band factor
band_levels <- c("0", "1", "2", "3+")
tab_plot_6m[, sdeff_cum_band := factor(as.character(sdeff_cum_band), levels = band_levels)]

# Calculate within group (continued vs discontinued) proportions
tab_plot_6m[
  ,
  n_group := sum(n),
  by = drop_status
]
tab_plot_6m[, prop := n / n_group]
tab_plot_6m[, pct  := 100 * prop]

# label position + label text (percent; optionally include n)
tab_plot_6m[, lab_y := prop + 0.03 * max(prop, na.rm = TRUE)]
tab_plot_6m[, lab_pct   := sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)]
tab_plot_6m[, lab := sprintf("%s\n(%.1f%%)", formatC(n, big.mark=","), pct)]


#-----------------------------#
# Custom x spacing: tight within group, bigger gap between groups

# Ensure band is an ordered factor (needed for stable positions)
band_levels <- c("0", "1", "2", "3+")
tab_plot_6m[, sdeff_cum_band := factor(as.character(sdeff_cum_band), levels = band_levels)]

within_step <- 1.0   # spacing between 0/1/2/3+ within the same group (smaller = tighter)
between_gap <- 0.5   # extra gap inserted between groups (bigger = more padding)

band_pos <- setNames(seq_along(band_levels) - 1, band_levels)

# Index groups: 1 = Did Not Discontinue, 2 = Discontinued (based on factor order above)
tab_plot_6m[, drop_idx := as.integer(drop_status)]

tab_plot_6m[
  ,
  x_pos := (drop_idx - 1) * (within_step * length(band_levels) + between_gap) +
    unname(band_pos[as.character(sdeff_cum_band)]) * within_step
]

# Build x ticks and labels
x_ticks <- unique(
  tab_plot_6m[
    order(drop_idx, sdeff_cum_band),
    .(x_pos, x_lab = paste0(as.character(sdeff_cum_band), "\n", as.character(drop_status)))
  ]
)

# Add label y-position (slight lift above bar)
tab_plot_6m[, lab_y := prop + 0.03 * max(prop, na.rm = TRUE)]


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Plot graph

lfig4_sdeff_attr_prop <-
  ggplot(tab_plot_6m, aes(x = x_pos, y = prop, fill = drop_status)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = lab, y = lab_y),
    size = 3,
    vjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = x_ticks$x_pos,
    labels = x_ticks$x_lab,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Cumulative Side Effect Count by Treatment Status at 6 Months",
    y = "Proportion of Patients (%)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  fig_theme +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.95)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_okeefe1blue_2)

lfig4_sdeff_attr_prop


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Print plot (for reference)

ggsave(
  lfig4_sdeff_attr_prop, 
  filename = "Final Outputs/lfig4-sdeff-attr-prop.png",
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```



################################################################################ 
# LFig5. Discontinuation cox regression by patients with and without side effects, with IMD as exposure
################################################################################ 

Data Prep and Regression Modelling
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Prepare two datasets

# Check counts
table(data_wide$sdeff_any_ever, useNA = "always")

# Cohort with any side effects + complete IMD
data_sdeff_none <- data_wide[sdeff_any_ever == 0 & !is.na(area_imd_qt)]
length(unique(data_sdeff_none$patid))

# Cohort without any side effects + complete IMD
data_sdeff_any <- data_wide[sdeff_any_ever == 1 & !is.na(area_imd_qt)]
length(unique(data_sdeff_any$patid))

```

Cox Regression
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Cox Regression - No Side Effects

# Without IMD
cox_attr_sdeff_none <- coxph(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ 
                               age_cat5 + eth_bin + wt_kg_0m_cat, 
                             data = data_sdeff_none)
summary(cox_attr_sdeff_none)

# With IMD
cox_attr_sdeff_none_imd <- coxph(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ 
                                   area_imd_qt + age_cat5 + eth_bin + wt_kg_0m_cat, 
                                 data = data_sdeff_none)
summary(cox_attr_sdeff_none_imd)

# Likelihood Ratio Test
lrt_cox_attr_sdeff_none <- anova(cox_attr_sdeff_none, cox_attr_sdeff_none_imd, test = "LRT")
lrt_cox_attr_sdeff_none


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Cox Regression - Any Side Effects

# Without IMD
cox_attr_sdeff_any <- coxph(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ 
                              age_cat5 + eth_bin + wt_kg_0m_cat,  
                            data = data_sdeff_any)
summary(cox_attr_sdeff_any)

# With IMD
cox_attr_sdeff_any_imd <- coxph(Surv(attr_wk, attr_ever_yn) ~ 
                                  area_imd_qt + age_cat5 + eth_bin + wt_kg_0m_cat,
                                data = data_sdeff_any)
summary(cox_attr_sdeff_any_imd)

# Likelihood Ratio Test
lrt_cox_attr_sdeff_any <- anova(cox_attr_sdeff_any, cox_attr_sdeff_any_imd, test = "LRT")
lrt_cox_attr_sdeff_any

```

Format into table
```{r}
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Create cox regression output table

#-----------------------------#
# Create helper functions

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(is.na(p), NA_character_,
         ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p)))
}

fmt_hr_ci <- function(estimate, conf.low, conf.high, digits = 2) {
  paste0(
    formatC(estimate, format = "f", digits = digits),
    " (",
    formatC(conf.low,  format = "f", digits = digits),
    ", ",
    formatC(conf.high, format = "f", digits = digits),
    ")"
  )
}

extract_imd_rows <- function(model, ref_label = "IMD 1 (Most Deprived)") {
  est_rows <-
    broom::tidy(model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    # Only keep IMD terms
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, "^area_imd_qt")) |>
    mutate(
      # Adjust labelling in table
      Variable = stringr::str_replace(term, "^area_imd_qt", "IMD "),
      `HR (95% CI)` = fmt_hr_ci(estimate, conf.low, conf.high),
      `Estimate p-value` = fmt_p(p.value)
    ) |>
    select(Variable, `HR (95% CI)`, `Estimate p-value`)

  ref_row <- tibble::tibble(
    Variable = ref_label,
    `HR (95% CI)` = "1 (ref)",
    `Estimate p-value` = NA_character_
  )
  dplyr::bind_rows(ref_row, est_rows)
}


extract_lrt_p <- function(m0, m1) {
  a <- anova(m0, m1, test = "LRT")
  p_col <- grep("^Pr\\(|^P\\(", colnames(a), value = TRUE)
  if (length(p_col) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  p <- a[nrow(a), p_col[1]]
  p_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(p))
  if (length(p_num) == 0) NA_real_ else p_num[1]
}

#-----------------------------#
# Build results

make_block <- function(label, m0, m1, ref_label = "IMD Q1 (ref)") {

  lrt_p <- fmt_p(extract_lrt_p(m0, m1))

  out <-
    extract_imd_rows(m1, ref_label = ref_label) |>
    mutate(
      Group = label,
      `LRT p-value` = NA_character_
    )

  # Put the LRT p-value on the reference row only (cleaner)
  out$`LRT p-value`[out$Variable == ref_label] <- lrt_p

  out |>
    select(Group, Variable, `HR (95% CI)`, `Estimate p-value`, `LRT p-value`)
}

tab_df <-
  bind_rows(
    make_block(
      label = "No side effects",
      m0 = cox_attr_sdeff_none,
      m1 = cox_attr_sdeff_none_imd,
      ref_label = "IMD 1 (Most Deprived)"
    ),
    make_block(
      label = "Any side effects",
      m0 = cox_attr_sdeff_any,
      m1 = cox_attr_sdeff_any_imd,
      ref_label = "IMD 1 (Most Deprived)"
    )
  )

#-----------------------------#
# Render with gt

lfig5_cox_attr_imd_tab <-
  tab_df |>
  gt(groupname_col = "Group") |>
  #tab_header(title = md("Attrition Cox models: IMD and likelihood ratio test")) |>
  cols_label(
    Variable = "IMD Quintile",
    `HR (95% CI)` = "HR (95% CI)",
    `Estimate p-value` = "P value",
    `LRT p-value` = "LRT P value"
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(Variable)) |>
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`HR (95% CI)`, `Estimate p-value`, `LRT p-value`)
  ) |>
  sub_missing(
    columns = everything(),
    missing_text = ""
  ) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(everything())
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(100),
    container.width = pct(100),
    table.align = "left",
    table.font.size = px(11),
    heading.title.font.size = px(12),
    row_group.font.size = px(11)
  )

lfig5_cox_attr_imd_tab


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Export table

gtsave(lfig5_cox_attr_imd_tab, "Final Outputs/lfig5-cox-attr-imd-tab.html")

```
